| [](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkL2u2zAMhZ_G3hxYlOw4g4YCF9lu146CItExC_1BouPety-cFn2BcuR3QB58Pz7Np6VA6fndRtS8ZpPwaAGZsZq27ezzkRL-4sZYEh5c91iKgRGmQYhBCOMthS-jeq9XREDZoxbXGZbpNi7Qb9pPEyh3Uw5WtCvYZRZuWpwU6_Rw4GRP-rwm3jMqBZdlnkYFboT1YR9uuXVqRE-c6-VnpsQbruErv7BeXI590BtzaZ381sG9gzulFza-RPK50vCgfKZOJO87R9PyXh128gOjpdDBfC4jetpjJz-KJT8UWzlhbRuVv9zZWCw90zvxD4plHOQkzR8NeyK2RswSZrXcrmLuq_bloBCozddOjc_z4bsy_5fnl4bfAQAA___Bx5Lo) | |
# Tuesday, November 11, 2025
---|---
**Good Morning!** On this day in 1918, [World War I ended](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLGurCAURb9GOwwcUZmC4jV289pXGpTDyAuCgTPD9e9vZnL_4O52Ze-drH_35W588PHx1xyoyaUlYi0BiTAvZX-STTVG_KJCeEaslJ_HeS7AYWBCMCEWa3y4Ftla7RAB-xa1mEZQw40raHeNMGwbt3KS4GBVyvbuNg4Ox6mfuJSq9fq9Jj7hUkKnxoFL2Di41aybujWSo_WUcvc_-Ug7unClF-ZuS0cb9E50lqb_08DcwFxr7XZfKOXrzRuYafeFWXMxH9kPaWCO6YXHipkJ8W6lHCyrJjPPMNrSZm3P6kPwZZwayR-H8eHzR7-S9NLwHQAA__9KInzH) with the signing of the armistice between Germany and the Allied Powers. Twenty years later, in 1938, the government officially designated Nov. 11 as Armistice Day to honor WWI veterans, and then in 1954 changed it to Veterans Day to honor those who served in any era. 🎖️As we mark Veterans Day, our _Feel Good Flyover Podcast_ honors a young man who has spent half his life ensuring that no hero is forgotten. Ten years after placing his first flag, 20-year-old Preston Sharp’s “Flags and Flowers” mission has grown into a global movement—one million tributes strong and still going. [Watch it here](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0M1uqzAUBOCnMbsg-_BnFl5cJYpuK6Xqql0iYw7BlbEpPuDm7SvyCp3ljPQt5vPW3bR11t_f9IyKxtB5TNEhEa5dnDYaQvIefygSLh4Trdu8LB1wqE5CnIToBm3doyuzQY2IgEWGSjQ1yKrlErJJcd1LFMI0HNFo7McKNR-llAUgtI3JrDo08QwvS8hlXfESDIex172RLSs5DpbCmn8F62nC0T3Cjmtuwpw5NREtkRX_GFwZXFNK-SNstPV47EejyUysuO6suLx_nz_M__q8v74wqIkVlxZitqphSdY5G-uGlfw-a-ueOP3pkV3BbwAAAP__abt0_Q). Today’s sponsor is [Midori Bio](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkL2u2zAMhZ_G3hxYlOw4g4YCF9lu146CItExC_1BouPety-cFn2BcuR3QB58Pz7Np6VA6fndRtS8ZpPwaAGZsZq27ezzkRL-4sZYEh5c91iKgRGmQYhBCOMthS-jeq9XREDZoxbXGZbpNi7Qb9pPEyh3Uw5WtCvYZRZuWpwU6_Rw4GRP-rwm3jMqBZdlnkYFboT1YR9uuXVqRE-c6-VnpsQbruErv7BeXI590BtzaZ381sG9gzulFza-RPK50vCgfKZOJO87R9PyXh128gOjpdDBfC4jetpjJz-KJT8UWzlhbRuVv9zZWCw90zvxD4plHOQkzR8NeyK2RswSZrXcrmLuq_bloBCozddOjc_z4bsy_5fnl4bfAQAA___Bx5Lo), the company turning plastic waste into clean energy. Their breakthrough ingredient helps plastic break down 64x faster, transforming trash into biogas that powers homes. With global brands already on board, Midori Bio is redefining sustainability and creating a billion-dollar opportunity for investors.
|

## House Races Back for Shutdown Vote
House Speaker Mike Johnson has [called all members back to Washington, D.C.](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLFuhDAQRL_GdCB7MZwpXKShu7QpkYH14cjYFl7Oub-POClfkC22Gc0bzXzdp7tx3oXHp9lRk41TwJI9EuEx5e2kNZYQ8IcyYQpY6Dj3lCbg0NVC1EJMq3H-Nclq1RYRsK1Qi1sPqhu4gmrTrV25VZK3FsxtVlL13cCHTvJBGuhVWzl90cT7uJTQqL7jEhYOdjbzogYmOa6O4tF8RxdoQ-tf8YlHs8S98nojSpm1HwxGBmMppVnCvFwig_FCMxiFuB5nMD4uZ9gxUP1Xrz4wnbN3iwm5zhgMYb3FM2Odg0nNRruvDr2m4rx3ub8xyR-7cf6dT_8a7anhNwAA__-6WIDz), for a vote on the Senate’s deal to end the 41-day government shutdown, the longest in U.S. history. The Senate advanced the plan Sunday night in a 60–40 vote, with eight Democrats breaking ranks to join Republicans, but final passage was delayed Monday when Sen. [Rand Paul objected to hemp-related](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0L9ugzAQx_GnMRuRfeBgBg9d2NK1IzL4HLvyP8ElNG9fEalP0FtP-kjf39dtvpkQQ75_moSaXJkzHntEItzm3T_IliNn_KGdsGY8aHukWmfgIFshWiFma0J8zX1jtUME7BrUYriCkiNX0Hg9LssoLHbD2hk7oATXWYnWKeXASbBN0Kcm3sf7Hi7qKnkPKwe3mGVVI-s52kBlu3yXkMmji6_yxO2yltRE7YnqzroPBhODiTz6EOP5YzD5kvAMYjDtmA0hg0nKUY2Ct_fTyAkztX-h7Waybat5xNZjqgymZtO2HiHGsF8H1vN7MuGNN_SvtZ4afgMAAP__awOAZg) language important to Kentucky growers. Johnson said the House will vote within 36 hours of Senate approval, as the upper chamber [prepares for up to seven votes](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkL2OozAUhZ_GdI7sG2NI4WIbumy7JTJwCXflH2TfhMnbj4g0TzCnPdJ39J1_9_HuKVB6_PURHa95THjUgMxYxro9eclHSvjFlXFPeHB5xn0fQUErtZZaj4un8B5Ns7gVEfDaoNOdhb69qR6azRmwMK-qm9puttqvftJordWr1YidnhtyJ01_ooyBS29bZWBWsE5-mvubMAoX4lwu_zMl3nAN7_zCcplzbILbmPcqrn8EDAIG3nCjEM5OwLDliKeQgMEvkRJVLp4pJwFD2966q1Ey0Avlc188Y5WPE5wiJpY_9vLjLOu7kBcwNMUt-0EhULWdMOoRPX3mGv7Vfy8H3wEAAP__f1uHUw) beginning Monday evening.

Many Democrats [expressed outrage Monday](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkL2OnTAUhJ_GdL4yx8ZwCxeRIrqN0qVE_jkGJwYj--wS3j66K-UJdtqRRvN9v96WN5tyOtYfdkdDsSwHXi0jEdalbe8UynUc-Jca4XngRfV9P88FBAy873nfL8GmfC-qCyYiAsoOTT9qmIanmKDbTJATyucgFEqEwY3aKal10HFSXkcRu2Rea_1nhFLwmPQgFHgB0VnnpydTAkOiUh-_Szpow5jv8oH14cveZbMRnY3JbwxmBrN1_kXwWMurZjD_LDlR8o3B7GzgR1o34gH34qulxqP1yF0ul7P-Dz9tpZv_5-YBbWYwNyr1ZnJOgcnvPYxyfA4SumrCeaWcU9MjU2Ldbcqfn-hLIj8M_AsAAP__Z06Ikg) at their eight members who voted to open the government without getting the healthcare subsidy extensions the party had demanded, fueling new calls to oust Minority Leader Chuck Schumer. The senators who voted yes—none up for reelection in 2026—defended their decision, arguing the [shutdown’s toll had grown too severe.](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLFuhDAQRL_GdCB7MZwpXKShu7QpkYH14cjYFl7Oub-POClfkC22Gc0bzXzdp7tx3oXHp9lRk41TwJI9EuEx5e2kNZYQ8IcyYQpY6Dj3lCbg0NVC1EJMq3H-Nclq1RYRsK1Qi1sPqhu4gmrTrV25VZK3FsxtVlL13cCHTvJBGuhVWzl90cT7uJTQqL7jEhYOdjbzogYmOa6O4tF8RxdoQ-tf8YlHs8S98nojSpm1HwxGBmMppVnCvFwig_FCMxiFuB5nMD4uZ9gxUP1Xrz4wnbN3iwm5zhgMYb3FM2Odg0nNRruvDr2m4rx3ub8xyR-7cf6dT_8a7anhNwAA__-6WIDz) President Trump has indicated he'll sign the measure once it clears Congress.
## Trump Demands Air Traffic Controllers Return
President Trump demanded that air traffic controllers "get back to work, NOW!!!" in a Truth Social post, threatening to "dock" their pay while offering a $10,000 bonus for those who remained on the job. [See the post here](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkL2u4yAQhZ_G7hLBePBPQbHSyl22WmlLi8AQs8JgwSS-efsr5xXuKc-RPh19_27LzYQY0uOP2Uizz0uio0ZiprLU9ckuHynRF1emPdHB5bnt-wIC1EXKi5SLMyG-F2yd9kRAXUtaDj2MahIjtKtGCYhAo_e2w1E4UtaPgxJk0aLtfRv0SZOfCES4jr0SCFaAv5u7HacGBbnAuVz_55B4JR_f-UXlavPWRr0y77XpfjUwNzBzefJasw0mnvtZoihk4u-cTHR_z_8NzHuuXBuYpVQKegkdCDUN04BTW7TbjxBjqP3QoHhsJnxQLf_Iz0vDdwAAAP__Frp4eA). The posting came as the 41-day shutdown intensified its impact, causing more than [2,000 flight cancellations](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLHO2yAUhZ8Gb0RwwTYZGCpV3v4uVdXRAnMdU2Gw4Dqp375KXqFnPdLR-b7fX_OXiynmxw-3o6W1zBlfLSER1rltJ4Xyyhn_UiM8Mr6onvtxzCCg51JyKefgYrpm3QW7IgKqDq0cBzD9XRjoNjtAGEZlhPRm7fXg5eiMN1oF70bvtOyifa_JT4TWcDNDLzQsAlbv_GLuTAsMkUq9_Skx04ZrusoT620pe5fsRnQ0pr4xmBhMzi9vgtujvGsG06-fDCbZC8HXFB8bNb64vGDCwM_G95KDuzhV98TEQ2z1PCiW3PhSMsV8IoOpUakXU1MMTH2XMKpRqLvqqg3HK6YU2zAyLR67i-nzif5L5NPCvwAAAP__v6CHzw) and over 6,000 delays on Monday. Though the shutdown now seems likely to end this week, Transportation Secretary Sean Duffy warned the situation remained dire, stating that many controllers—who earn an average annual salary of around $140,000—[could manage missing one paycheck](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkL2uozAQhZ_GdI7sweancLHSii7bbokMDGFWxrbMJGze_opI9wnulHOk8-l8f-_j3VOg-Pjjd3S8pjHieQRkxjIe25OXdMaI__lgzBFPLs895xEUWKm11HpcPIX3aKrFrYiAdYVOtw10tlcdVJvzrVWqMa2ta9N3jYdmmdDMqgOtPU6-Ine16c8pY-DWNVYZmBWsk5_mrhdG4UKcyu1fosgbruGdXlhuc9qr4DbmfIj6l4BBwMAbbhTClQkYcgo0v6938fHIqbBnSlHAYG3fGavkZ5D0VCQXv640yzlFLikELId8XJy4Y2T5LePC1ANPBVdR_95yVdySTwqBjqYVRj12Tx98xT_y-XLwFQAA__9OMIw0), but none could manage missing a second, leading to the absences. To maintain safety amid the critical staffing shortage, the Federal Aviation Administration ordered airlines to cut domestic flights. It remains unclear when that cutback will be lifted.
## Chart: US Inflation Rates by Category
A chart highlighting U.S. inflation by category shows that, while overall inflation is running at roughly 3% year-over-year, [beef and veal prices have risen significantly](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkL1urDAQRp8GOiMYMD-Fi9tst7dNaXnxABMZG-EBhzx9xOYRMuXo09HR-XjqpyFHfv5vVlQ8Be0xRYfMuOu4HGxD8h6_ODJuHhPvx7ptGkqQoqpEVWlryF26ya2aEAHrHFXVtdDLoewhX1QHZhg6C4DYyVrKEewkpbWVsR0MdZuTumnV-8qmgaJvZdnAWML0Mq-xH7KmREsc9uIzkOcFJ3eFE_diDGvu1MK8xaz-l8Ejg0dKqTgpHsaNZiM2jiLfwwwev2_6RisOEQX5yRmm4MXrEqNhnMN-CfLi1nnD6jmE2aE-afZ3kXxXdkvkHMW2y5pyXg25twX_Kd2p4CcAAP__6n6Fcw), up 14.7% from 2023. The data, based on [monthly inflation reports from _USAFacts_](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0DHO4yAUBODT4A4LvxhMCopt3GXbLS0Mj5gVBgue483tV8kV_unn02j-PJaHjSnm52-7o6FQloxXS0iEdWnbSb5cOeM_aoRHxovquR_HAgIkHwY-DIu3Mb2XsfMmIALeOjTDpEDLu9DQbcZ7J9BNKOWq5Bj0qp2636zSElXw09BF89GGb8Q4Qq-VFCM4AWG1q9N3Ngr0kUrt_5aYacOQ3uWFtXdl75LZiI7Gbr8YzAzms9lgHbW-1CeD2eZ2YW0M5muzxGPjtCF3Z62YiccckqVYMq-WkMHsypmpvj9OjoSeN7KEn3pXjT-umFJsamKjeO42pu8C-tFtLwP_AwAA__9q0ISq), shows that car maintenance had the second-highest price rise at 7.7%, followed by home insurance at 7.5%. Sugar and sweets rose 6.7%, and electricity rose by 6.4%. Some categories saw prices stay below the average inflation rate, including fresh vegetables (2.8%), seafood (2.1%), and jewelry and watches (2.0%). [The full chart is here](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLHOozAQhJ8GOiN7Y4gpXFxDl2uvRDZewp6MjfASjnv6X8n_CP-Uo9HM6PvzGB-OIqXnb7ei5TmPCc8SkRn3sSwHh3ymhP-4MG4JT96PddtGkNAKpYRSY3AUr1HXwc6IgLcarbp3YNpeGqgXe5uDCt73yvRegfS6NXqGYHR798Gbrib7blMfSa2hMV0rNUwSZu_8ZPpKSwzEeW_-Zkq84Byv_MK9mfJaR7swb6W6_apgqGA4z7N5UTlcnNxG7CIVfgcrGL5t-o9BHKIISnN0TDkJf4nJMT7zfglK4n2ngqHebdhOipFKd6-0fK6O4meTfwTqZeErAAD__9sIfs4).

### A Genius Way to Eliminate 400M Tons of Plastic Waste
Over 400 million tons of plastic are produced yearly. [Midori Bio](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkL2u2zAMhZ_G3hxYlOw4g4YCF9lu146CItExC_1BouPety-cFn2BcuR3QB58Pz7Np6VA6fndRtS8ZpPwaAGZsZq27ezzkRL-4sZYEh5c91iKgRGmQYhBCOMthS-jeq9XREDZoxbXGZbpNi7Qb9pPEyh3Uw5WtCvYZRZuWpwU6_Rw4GRP-rwm3jMqBZdlnkYFboT1YR9uuXVqRE-c6-VnpsQbruErv7BeXI590BtzaZ381sG9gzulFza-RPK50vCgfKZOJO87R9PyXh128gOjpdDBfC4jetpjJz-KJT8UWzlhbRuVv9zZWCw90zvxD4plHOQkzR8NeyK2RswSZrXcrmLuq_bloBCozddOjc_z4bsy_5fnl4bfAQAA___Bx5Lo) found a way to turn that waste into a gold mine. Their proprietary technology helps plastic break down 64 times faster, creating biogas that can be used as clean energy from existing landfills, called Advanced BioRecycleTM. It’s a [special ingredient](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkL2u2zAMhZ_G3hxYlOw4g4YCF9lu146CItExC_1BouPety-cFn2BcuR3QB58Pz7Np6VA6fndRtS8ZpPwaAGZsZq27ezzkRL-4sZYEh5c91iKgRGmQYhBCOMthS-jeq9XREDZoxbXGZbpNi7Qb9pPEyh3Uw5WtCvYZRZuWpwU6_Rw4GRP-rwm3jMqBZdlnkYFboT1YR9uuXVqRE-c6-VnpsQbruErv7BeXI590BtzaZ381sG9gzulFza-RPK50vCgfKZOJO87R9PyXh128gOjpdDBfC4jetpjJz-KJT8UWzlhbRuVv9zZWCw90zvxD4plHOQkzR8NeyK2RswSZrXcrmLuq_bloBCozddOjc_z4bsy_5fnl4bfAQAA___Bx5Lo) that can be added to any plastic manufacturing process with zero downtime at the factory and no capital expenditure. That’s why global brands like Head/Penn Tennis, Snibbs Footwear, and Planters Peanuts are already adopting Midori’s tech to meet their eco-pledges. Midori is in talks with over 150 additional potential partners. As plastic production is projected to triple by 2060, regulation and consumer demand will likely force more brands to act. clean_body: House Speaker Mike Johnson has , for a vote on the Senate’s deal to end the 41-day government shutdown, the longest in U.S. history. | | | | | | Tuesday, November 11, 2025 ---|--- Good Morning! On this day in 1918, with the signing of the armistice between Germany and the Allied Powers. Twenty years later, in 1938, the government officially designated Nov. 11 as Armistice Day to honor WWI veterans, and then in 1954 changed it to Veterans Day to honor those who served in any era. 🎖️As we mark Veterans Day, our Feel Good Flyover Podcast honors a young man who has spent half his life ensuring that no hero is forgotten. Ten years after placing his first flag, 20-year-old Preston Sharp’s “Flags and Flowers” mission has grown into a global movement—one million tributes strong and still going. . Today’s sponsor is , the company turning plastic waste into clean energy. Their breakthrough ingredient helps plastic break down 64x faster, transforming trash into biogas that powers homes. With global brands already on board, Midori Bio is redefining sustainability and creating a billion-dollar opportunity for investors. | House Races Back for Shutdown Vote House Speaker Mike Johnson has , for a vote on the Senate’s deal to end the 41-day government shutdown, the longest in U.S. history. The Senate advanced the plan Sunday night in a 60–40 vote, with eight Democrats breaking ranks to join Republicans, but final passage was delayed Monday when Sen. language important to Kentucky growers. Johnson said the House will vote within 36 hours of Senate approval, as the upper chamber beginning Monday evening. Many Democrats at their eight members who voted to open the government without getting the healthcare subsidy extensions the party had demanded, fueling new calls to oust Minority Leader Chuck Schumer. The senators who voted yes—none up for reelection in 2026—defended their decision, arguing the President Trump has indicated he'll sign the measure once it clears Congress. Trump Demands Air Traffic Controllers Return President Trump demanded that air traffic controllers "get back to work, NOW!!!" in a Truth Social post, threatening to "dock" their pay while offering a $10,000 bonus for those who remained on the job. . The posting came as the 41-day shutdown intensified its impact, causing more than and over 6,000 delays on Monday. Though the shutdown now seems likely to end this week, Transportation Secretary Sean Duffy warned the situation remained dire, stating that many controllers—who earn an average annual salary of around $140,000—, but none could manage missing a second, leading to the absences. To maintain safety amid the critical staffing shortage, the Federal Aviation Administration ordered airlines to cut domestic flights. It remains unclear when that cutback will be lifted. Chart: US Inflation Rates by Category A chart highlighting U.S. inflation by category shows that, while overall inflation is running at roughly 3% year-over-year, , up 14.7% from 2023. The data, based on , shows that car maintenance had the second-highest price rise at 7.7%, followed by home insurance at 7.5%. Sugar and sweets rose 6.7%, and electricity rose by 6.4%. Some categories saw prices stay below the average inflation rate, including fresh vegetables (2.8%), seafood (2.1%), and jewelry and watches (2.0%). . name: Office of U.S. Senator Angela Alsobrooks
subject: Honoring Our Veterans
body:
|

---
Friends and neighbors, Join me in honoring all our veterans here in Maryland and across our great country today. While we owe veterans our gratitude and respect every day of the year, this Tuesday in November allows us to take a moment, stop, and acknowledge those who risked their lives in service to our country. They woke up every morning with one goal: to protect each of us and the freedoms we hold dear. We have an obligation to do more than just thank our veterans for their service - we must provide them with benefits, health care, and opportunities to thrive after their service. That's why I made sure earlier this year to join a bipartisan group of colleagues in reintroducing the Major Richard Star Act. This legislation would provide full benefits to combat-injured veteran retirees. And I will continue to fight on behalf of our veterans here in Washington and across our state. So to the brave men and women who have served our country: thank you. And to everyone else - be sure to thank a veteran today and remember your freedom and safety are only possible because of their service. Sincerely, Angela D. Alsobrooks
United States Senator
[Click here to Unsubscribe.](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/EForm.aspx?__cid=SenAlsobrooks&__fid=100022&__crop=15773.14088382.5748988.7190525)

subject: Abbott Launches Campaign, Christmas Tree's Pit Stop, and At-Risk Homes
body:
Gov. Greg Abbott launched his campaign for a record fourth term Sunday night in Houston, touting conservative victories during his tenure while warning supporters about Democratic efforts to turn Texas blue.

| | |

| [](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0D2OrDAMwPHThG5QYiBAkeJJT3Sz7ZaRSRywFMgoZL5uv2K0N1h3PxfWX_6-2ity5H35wo1MeYVkd3oekUqhbHGeUykR77tb6XC43ZCX3a3ZgoTuotRFKeuR49u2lTez9KOqyKhew6ChHVS1mm7Us8TQBj0o14_j3A6D78JAcoZmhr5icx5T54BUaqxdkA5BN63zQF1HopXkuaRcl5UKvfAI8Z0elGuXtiqatZTbIZp_AiYBkyeMNe8uLTvXnARMGIJ1oplSCJQte9H81wL0uf5ANf3pe46_FqCPdM-OTmRaOO0Yz74qG397cox86F60ctmQ46ei_O13DwM_AQAA___ONYC0) | |
# Tuesday, November 11, 2025
---|---
**Good Morning!** On this day in 1928, jazz and blues singer [Ernestine Anderson](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0D1ywyAQxfHTSJ01Yo0wKihcRJ3TpmQQu5J2gkADxB-3zzhXyOte8yv-Xzd7cxw4rp9uJ1OfS7KRHiVQrZStm-dUa3A_0W9UvNsPx2v0W7bQw3AS4iSERcfhZWWLZu5xFC0ZcVGgFUgt2s1IiSjPGvVlnKWWiwI1wDhquQiNSmHL5o2J96AXYuz80nsH6iw9Ag0DNbIn5JpyVzeq9HRlCa90p9z5tLfBbLUepTlfG5gamCh2D_7mg5Bdl_LawPT-DUwfOVKpHMleI1IuKbbZ4PHgELioSyP7dXcc_tT6vxZ3A78BAAD__0Q5c4I) was born in Houston. Over a six-decade career, she recorded over 30 albums and was nominated for four Grammy Awards. World War II veteran Jose Mendez was [honored](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLGOszAQhJ_GdEbexUBcuPgbuvztlWixl2CdwZHxQfL2J_IKN-2MRjPf1328U4hhe_ynlW15zWnc-Nwjl8J5pGlKpUT62dzCu6P1SeGxuSWPqLCVABJg9BTie9SVt5PyBiq20Hd461DfoFrsDYzySC3NvvGOlfGkDCP2jtg73VXBXmVwCRWAqd2sHGHXaOeR25aFVuxDSbkuCxd-0T7Hdzo41y6tVbRLKc9dNP8EDgKH8zzr7xBCOS5b4EC5BBdZ4HA9EzjE5Ch-kiHIgwtn2uSStpTZCxxa1ciGPAN1s3SejdQalJwMsOzAN71Box1Ala1_niHGsHe90OqxUoifSeVvIA-LvwEAAP__yxaF8A) by Bible Believers Baptist Church in Corpus Christi this week. Mendez said he lost 16 schoolmates during the war, which is "why I’m telling people not to forget the fallen heroes." As we mark Veterans Day, our _[Feel Good Flyover Podcast](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0MFugzAQBNCvMbcg75pAfPChShS1lVL11B6RWS_BkoEUNpD8fUV_oXOckd5hvi_1xccUh-uH79nJox3rgdc5sQhPtW-aUST5-0Adz-T7m4_XgbqpRo37HcAOoA4-pmddZME1OljI2EFV4qHE4gBZ58AeWoKAHFqoDJOxwba-aVhXAS0VWXQbBltQA9icWk0eS1NQQN7vWRWaQ5RxyqVj4Yef2_QcF55yGvssuU7kNivzovCs8Lyua_4c73JveNu3xgt1ypwXZU6fP8cvei2Py_ubwlKUOVmcs8mF2xpTinNZqUJfex_THy7_u2Rx-BsAAP__ycx09A)_ episode honors a young man who has spent half his life ensuring that no hero is forgotten. Ten years after placing his first flag, 20-year-old Preston Sharp’s “Flags and Flowers” mission has grown into a global movement—one million tributes strong and still going. [Watch it here](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0MFugzAQBNCvMbcg75pAfPChShS1lVL11B6RWS_BkoEUNpD8fUV_oXOckd5hvi_1xccUh-uH79nJox3rgdc5sQhPtW-aUST5-0Adz-T7m4_XgbqpRo37HcAOoA4-pmddZME1OljI2EFV4qHE4gBZ58AeWoKAHFqoDJOxwba-aVhXAS0VWXQbBltQA9icWk0eS1NQQN7vWRWaQ5RxyqVj4Yef2_QcF55yGvssuU7kNivzovCs8Lyua_4c73JveNu3xgt1ypwXZU6fP8cvei2Py_ubwlKUOVmcs8mF2xpTinNZqUJfex_THy7_u2Rx-BsAAP__ycx09A). ## Abbott Launches Campaign for Fourth Term
Gov. Greg Abbott [launched his campaign](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkMGqpDAQRb8m7iJJtaZ1kUVv3PUwAwOzlJiUGoiJJNVt998P9uN9watVFQWXc-6_-3g3Pvi4_DIbanrNaYx4lIBEmEczTYkomEe0KxZrtt34Jdo1jyCg5VJyKUdnfHiPTeX0JFwvK9TyqqBT0HSyWrWEWTpnJbq-wUunWuFMozoUYKFXc1t5fYbJc0BI2dd2FtaAujTWAbYtskag85RyTSsSvkyZwzs9Mdc2bVXQK9Fe2OXGYGAwHMdRbyWePwYDRv4oDIZTisGwp-DJ23NdMi78S5B_G_KMGNCST5FP3vE5ZW54RpvyeTwyrZwwb9wU_gHhy8kRU2YwmMxvN_lH_U5_q6zdfvgQfFFX1ohlMz58aOlnHT81_A8AAP__-ROTPg) for a record fourth term Sunday night in Houston, touting conservative victories during his tenure while warning supporters about Democratic efforts to turn Texas blue. During his speech, Abbott unveiled a [property tax overhaul](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLGOgzAQRL8GOpB3YxsoXFxDl2uvRIu9BEsGcmYTkr8_kV-4qd_MSO_nOlwpprjevmlhJ69pG1Y-9sQinAcax00k0WP1M--eljvF2-rnPKBCUwFUAEOgmN6DLoMbVeigZAeNxdaibqGcXUu61S2GVltrDIBio4IhbIg9g-cyunMMzqAC6Go_KU9oL9oHZGO40IpDlC3XMrPwi_Ypvbcn59pvS5ncLHLfi8tXgX2B_XEcJ-d5Fc7774Myn1yB_adaYE9Zok88jFNjLyHYajTIlW55qkj7rmqUgRF8CIG7epYlldmF-xFTirttCq1uC8X0OZf_KXs6_AsAAP__Z1p_mw) that he promised would "drive a stake through the heart of local property tax hikes for good." Among other measures, the [plan](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLHOozAQhJ_GdI7wAk5SuEhDl1-66kq0sRewfmMjexPg7U_c6Z7gn2400mi--f0cnuiDj9MXLmR4H9MQaSuBmCkP-Hol5oDvaGcqFpcV_RTtnAeooZNKSaUGhz4cQ1s586rdXVVk1FXDTUN7U9VsrjelwHaNfumxUXe4o7aucc6S1XWnofLmLFOnoFbqfrFjbRF001oH1HUk2pqc55QvPBPTjmUMR_pQvti0VMHMzGsRzUNAL6Dftu2ylHhmAnqK8l0E9EuKdAjoGXc6_ZRpkv_w5Le330WmcZSZKJBln6L8Dys3z7PEacpUiv-QXHNaKfMhGXeZKXga5RowCugxy8dD_dLP21eVjVs3H4Iv-iraelrQh797-Wcvfwz8CQAA___ot5PT) would lower appraisal caps and let Texans vote on abolishing school property taxes. "_What we have in Texas is precious. But it can all be destroyed in one bad election_ ," [said Abbott](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0D2O7CAMB_DTkI4IPPmAguI1dPPaLSNCnASJhBHxTCa3X7GrOcG6cGX97Z-_7sPdhRj25b_b0NB7TsOO5xGRCPPgxjERRffc_YqHd9vDhWX3ax5AQMul5FIOkwvxGppqMqOYtKzQyL4D1UGjZLWa0Xe61Qpw9krduhacVsKhViCE7BtdBVPCZCkQUuraz8I76G6NnwDbFlkjcAqUck0rEr7dMccrvTDXPm1VNCvR42C3fwwsA3ueZ_08HKXJXWWAgT0o5YuBLTIGtqxjYKUsTTCwS3rxJePCf738A-YZMaKnkHb-wfM5ZT6nZ6aVE-YSr3qp275phegZ2Cqb6XGGGMPR9awRy-ZC_LmU_vbfl4HvAAAA___D7o1b). "_We will defend our culture, and we will safeguard our Texas values, from the radicals who are trying to shove our state to the left_." Abbott has nearly $90 million in campaign funds, and he doesn't currently face any serious primary challengers. Yesterday, 2006 gubernatorial candidate [Chris Bell](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkL2OqzAQhZ8GOiPbGEMKF2nocqWrLbZE_hnAkbGRPQnJPv2KvMJO9Umjme_ofN-mm_bBx-Wf3kDha05ThKMEQIQ8aWMSYtCPaFcoVm-79ku0a5445R1hjDA2Oe3DexK1U4a6C6tBsV7yQXIxsHpVphVmtqbvpbCD6QRtDethFnKwwkknaq_OZ-wcThm7NHamVnPZCus4dB1UgoLzmHKDKyC8dJnDOz0hNzZtdVAr4l6q9lrxseLjcRzNVuK5q_gIkTxKxcc9BY_enggBLPoUT9Yx4QqZONiSzRrJPflYCK5AsrZA5pTJx0iWUxhTJj4STrk8jzO5Xtl__fVzr7Ny--FD8EX2laDLpn34xMO_lfpU_DcAAP__bUCNUQ) joined State Rep. Gina Hinojosa, businessman Andrew White, and rancher Bobby Cole in vying for the Democratic nomination.
## TxDOT Calls for Expanded Public Transit
TxDOT's first-ever Statewide Multimodal Transit Plan calls for [expanding public transit](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkDuy2zAMRVdDdfSQsH6vYJFG3UubUgOSkMUJPxoSz7J3n5G3EBRo8Dn33j_f6zeGGPLjNyYy_NrKmulskZiprmhtYY74k91OzWE6MDyy2-sKCgaptdR69Rjie-07b6zyX7ojo6cR5hH6WXe70Y78bDd7H9COE07kps2qQfU4w0z3sQvmeqavAqX1181tyiGM9955oGEg0SvygUu98U5ML2xbfJcn1ZsrqYtmZz6auP8SsAhYzvO8_W3I11DAcrkRsHzOBCwXSsCi9dWUgMVhlb6kkDGz_GzJTOSbTKWSPH5sDE5yxdwCSy4yEbFMxYYY-C09Jcy-SX75wrLSUSrLhu8L1VXjjzPEGNo4iV49Eob4kcz_F_TTwL8AAAD__0GHlFk) across Texas to accommodate the state's growing population, which is projected to increase by more than 9 million residents by 2050. The agency has [proposed $146 billion](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkL3OpDAMRZ8mdEHE_BcptqH7tt0ShcRApBCjxAMzb79i9hHWpSUf33P__Mw_xgcft9_mQM3vleaIdw7IjGk2y0LMwbyi3TFbc5zGb9HuaYYKWqmUVGp2xofP3BROL5UbVYFa9R0MHTSDKnbdO3TtMCiwWMNYu7XGsRv6tRqdw8a4wusHpp6BSqmxtGtlDXR1Yx1g26JoKnSeKZW8I-Pb5DV86MJUWjqKoHfmM4v6l4BJwHTfd8lvR1xudAmYzEIvFjA9WonoEDBlpuQxC5g2uuQ_SWlipFe0mOWDlqrp5OJD8BTl96fkZGI-KbHhZ-njhZkPjFzufIQiaXfez0HuetFU22F8-Abk_6v10vA3AAD__xnqkRo) for upgrading and expanding Texas highways over the next decade. State lawmakers have traditionally opposed public transit projects, such as Austin's Project Connect and the proposed Texas Central high-speed rail line. The draft report predicts that Texans in rural areas will increasingly depend on public transit to access medical care and other services. It also highlights the difficulty of traveling within the Texas Triangle—the area between DFW, Houston, and San Antonio—where more than [two-thirds of Texans live](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLuOgzAQRb8GuolsQ4gpXGxDl223RIM9gCU_Invy-vsV0f7BTjm69-jo_lznK_rg0_aNkQy_1jwnetZAzFRmXJbMHPCe7E7VYryh35Ldy6yEOoOUIOXs0If33LfOLMKNsiUjL4PSg-q1bHfjrESrJeoRdSeUFZ1baOit1ouVTo-tNwdMHqeElOPJrsKiGrreOkXnMzW9IOc5lxPvxPTCuoZ3flA52RzbYHbmW226r0ZNjZoO_b5iwsQ5-Xxk_r6NmkK2GBo1xVwIeMcE_MzAuy-uQl7hQ4dOdBB9CD4nKFS9o8QVgn8Q-ARrvhcIWDaqDJG4ZMBCWNti3O151OpwaXqxRfTho8j_G_Zh1G8AAAD__6ogkMU). Achieving statewide connectivity for cities with at least 10,000 residents could cost $30 billion in initial expenditures and $5 billion a year to operate. TxDOT is holding meetings on the plan tomorrow in San Antonio and Thursday in Austin. Feedback can also be [submitted online](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkD2PszAQhH8NdCDbED4KF2-TLm97JVrsBfa0xsjehOTfn3JV-pvq0RSj0fN1m25ATPv6HwJaeS5x2vHMjCKYJpjnKMJw392G2UE4gNbdbWkyylwqrSutJw_Er6ktvZ2VH3WJVvedGTrTDrrcLPatGmAA5ZtGLx00emyHTsNoRpzR9CXZ95h-xyitx9otyoHpmtZ5g5cLFq1CTxJTLRsKPiEv_IoPTLWLoWS7iRy5aP4V5lqY63metTx9lHqNj8JcjxS_0Un-wCrL3RO-qywgeJLHT67CnYVC9MCVJNgzSXUw7PUmgctk_XESM-WuL1q1BiD-fSJ_8_ew5icAAP__tiuH9w) until November 20.
## U.S. Capitol Christmas Tree Visits Amarillo
Hundreds of Amarillo residents gathered at a downtown art gallery on Sunday to view the 2025 U.S. Capitol Christmas Tree [as it made its way](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLGOgzAQRL8GOpBtjCGFi2vocu2VaLGXeCWDkb0Jyd-fuF-4qUYaaUbzfu7zHSjS_viGDS2_1zTveJaIzJhnWJbEHOG5u4DFwXYAPXYX8qyE6hspGylnDxQ_s669XYS_yRqtHIwajdKjrINdhR8XHM3SrdL7UaPozKgGA70GjV1Xk73K5CUlpLy1bhUOlOm08wr7Hist0BOn3HJAxjeUNX7SC3Pr0lZHG5iPUnVflZoqNZ3n2X4gpHSllZquO5WaIDO5iJd9Ng4O4hQbFzIV3qA0nBGbFxXi0shByE4bfWsDb7HO1h8nxUjFDJUWjw0o_i3z_3i9rPoNAAD__6Tzf-A) to Washington, D.C. This year's stop was only the third time it's come through Amarillo. The [last](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLHSpCAQhJ8GMywZ0V8DgkvM9tILrRFGoQrFgnHdffsr7xWuww66-vv-vOYXhhiO7TfuZPizpvmgu0RipjzjsiTmiNdhPRWL-4lhO6zPMzTQSaWkUrPDEL-zrpxZGjeqioz66WHoQQ-q8qbHEZQdddc6ZQlGN7Ru1EOnlV1G1-sqmGdMPYFGqbG2a2MR-lZbB9R1JHRDLnDKNXti-mBZ4ze9Kdc27VU0nvksov0lYBIw3fddX8VhvaW3gAmXdLF8CgHTgyZgWmLaBExXkRbPwClK63MovGORnInkFlaWJ6UzkvSEjpzkdGN28sbiw7FxOqSzVTbuvEOMofQ_QjfbjiH-O8X_p_Jt4G8AAAD___41ivc) was in 2013. The 53-foot red fir, the first to be harvested from Nevada's Humboldt-Toiyabe National Forest, was visible through windows in the specially decorated trailer, which attendees could sign. The tree will make additional stops in Oklahoma, Kansas, Missouri, Kentucky, and Maryland before being installed at the U.S. Capitol on November 21. Follow its progress [here](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykzzuO6zAMheHV2F0CkX6mUHGbdLntlAJDUZEQ2QpkTh67H3i2MKc9wAf8Xxd3oZTTevtPi1h9h-JWeW1ZVKU6ul6LaqbvlaNsTMuD0m3lWB0aHA4ABwDnKeWP61tvr8afoBUL04jziP0MbbQQODD6ISAHT9MEpuNO5in4eaBAoU12x2AfGoDTkYNhwrHr2aMMgzS9EZ-01KNGUXnTFvKnPKUeuSxttlH1sTXdvwbPDZ7vsr5K1bifDZ61ihy0Et-lttX6xyvlnLZxanpzWyjlX0T_lv60-BMAAP__SHBvIQ). Food trucks, vendors, and Santa Claus were also on hand [for the celebration](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkL1uhDAQhJ_GdEa2-bmjcJGG7tKmRIu9YCsLRnjvON4-IsobZLqRPo1m5usxPCBSXOdPWNDye0rDikcmZMZ9gHFMzATP1QXMDpYN4ry6sA9GmUZqLbUePEQ6h7rwdlS-0wVafWvNvTX1XRfB4qhd5SZ_8x6nUWnltK-q2ruua5q21UW0V5i-ZJTWXekm5cC0Ve28waZBUSv0kdNeckDGN-SJzvTCvXRpKcgG5i2L6kOYXpj-OI5yOUOcw0YQ13xBwvTXKmF6Sg5I_hkOKJ8ySwdb5ETShT1mXiBL3hHlAt-YJcjMaZNxlRd-IlE6pIt8CtMXu_XbEYlibm-iVvMCkX5b8f--fFnzEwAA__-SvIvI). Greg Abbott for a record fourth term Sunday night in Houston, touting conservative victories during his tenure while warning supporters about Democratic efforts to turn Texas blue. During his speech, Abbott unveiled a that he promised would "drive a stake through the heart of local property tax hikes for good." Among other measures, the would lower appraisal caps and let Texans vote on abolishing school property taxes. "What we have in Texas is precious. But it can all be destroyed in one bad election ," . "We will defend our culture, and we will safeguard our Texas values, from the radicals who are trying to shove our state to the left." Abbott has nearly $90 million in campaign funds, and he doesn't currently face any serious primary challengers. Yesterday, 2006 gubernatorial candidate joined State Rep. Gina Hinojosa, businessman Andrew White, and rancher Bobby Cole in vying for the Democratic nomination. TxDOT Calls for Expanded Public Transit TxDOT's first-ever Statewide Multimodal Transit Plan calls for across Texas to accommodate the state's growing population, which is projected to increase by more than 9 million residents by 2050. The agency has for upgrading and expanding Texas highways over the next decade. State lawmakers have traditionally opposed public transit projects, such as Austin's Project Connect and the proposed Texas Central high-speed rail line. The draft report predicts that Texans in rural areas will increasingly depend on public transit to access medical care and other services. It also highlights the difficulty of traveling within the Texas Triangle—the area between DFW, Houston, and San Antonio—where more than . Achieving statewide connectivity for cities with at least 10,000 residents could cost $30 billion in initial expenditures and $5 billion a year to operate. TxDOT is holding meetings on the plan tomorrow in San Antonio and Thursday in Austin. Feedback can also be until November 20. U.S. Capitol Christmas Tree Visits Amarillo Hundreds of Amarillo residents gathered at a downtown art gallery on Sunday to view the 2025 U.S. Capitol Christmas Tree to Washington, D.C. This year's stop was only the third time it's come through Amarillo. The was in 2013. The 53-foot red fir, the first to be harvested from Nevada's Humboldt-Toiyabe National Forest, was visible through windows in the specially decorated trailer, which attendees could sign. The tree will make additional stops in Oklahoma, Kansas, Missouri, Kentucky, and Maryland before being installed at the U.S. Capitol on November 21. Follow its progress . Food trucks, vendors, and Santa Claus were also on hand . name: The Maine Wire
subject: Foreign Cyber Crew Hit Maine ATMs in “Jackpotting” Spree, Authorities Say
body:
Here's the latest breaking news at The Maine Wire

** Foreign Cyber Crew Hit Maine ATMs in “Jackpotting” Spree, Authorities Say (https://mainepolicy.us9.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a975bf67c40a8d8be91ad606d&id=0c28de481d&e=f093fa355c)
------------------------------------------------------------
By Maine Wire Staff on Nov 10, 2025 06:02 pm

name: Marsha Blackburn
subject: May God bless our veterans.
body:
We owe our men and women in uniform everything for answering the call and risking it all to protect us and our freedom.

| | | | Today, on Veterans Day, I invite you to join me in thanking the brave men and women who serve our country.
America is exceptional beyond measure, but it couldn’t be that way – it wouldn’t be that way – without our veterans.
We owe our men and women in uniform everything for answering the call and risking it all to protect us and our freedom.
Today, and every day, may God bless the American veteran, and may God always bless the greatest country on earth.
Thank you,
Marsha Blackburn

name: Governor Kelly Ayotte
subject: Honoring All Who Have Served
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Kelly for New Hampshire, Chris Connelly Treasurer, PO Box 4723 Manchester, NH 03108
body:
Happy Veterans Day!

| | | | | |
---
| | | | | | Dear Friend,
Happy Veterans Day!
Today, we honor all the brave men and women who served our nation in the United States Armed Forces. It’s thanks to their service and sacrifice that we live in the greatest nation in the world.
To our veterans, thank you for defending our freedom. To our active duty service members and our military families, thank you for continuing to serve.
Veterans Day is an especially important day for our family. My husband, Joe, served our country in the United States Air Force, and our daughter, Kate, is now continuing our family’s tradition of service as a cadet at the Air Force Academy. I’m also remembering today my grandfather, who served in the Army Air Corps during World War II and lived to be 98 years old.

America is the land of the free because of the brave, and we owe these men and women a debt we can never repay. Join me today in thanking our veterans for defending our freedoms at home and overseas.
All the best,
Kelly
---
| | Paid for by Kelly for New Hampshire, Chris Connelly Treasurer, PO Box 4723 Manchester, NH 03108
---

name: NEGOP
subject: 📣 You're Invited! Campaign and Candidate Virtual Bootcamp Series
body:
You don't want to miss this!
| | |
---
| |
---
| You're Invited!
---
| We're excited to invite you to the next installment of the 2025 Campaign and Candidate Virtual Bootcamp Series, hosted in partnership with the Republican National Committee (RNC).
This series of training sessions are designed to equip you with the tools, strategies, and confidence to run a successful campaign and win. These trainings are designed for candidates running for office, campaign staff and volunteers who are involved or want to be involved in campaigns at any level, or those just wanting to learn the basics of running a political campaign. The [Virtual Bootcamp series](https://feyrpzhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001sdM29cjOjzP6_6vya-ov4RgPMQqE2MnEMevn8T1TIFGN5o0C56Zfs5UV2FSk8p5Qr7hXJ85tjYqGQ_q9EoPRrOD0XKvSaLvruNqhR8hT_fCuEW7E9EjqQBXi21z_ssZ2UcR8wX95cfq2Ns-vK7sAe_BYO4e98JwkjG7RVofWbVTVIIvzHJfH62a0IQ3SD9dI0Ap7XcJT1BE=&c=eljBy3Qdi9NDpgcE1a4Lkt13tb67GiX8BQwJ5D0zn88Gh0vzF0qLKQ==&ch=yaWIcVJqWn7NX4Sa7kSUuaZUJfGZCOzR7Gc_yABk0VaWgNtZmg_mZg==) will take place over four weeks. Each session will have its own registration link, so be sure to sign up for the ones you plan to attend:

November 13, 2025 at 6pm - Training 4: Political - The Ground Game and Putting it All Together
* Build grassroots infrastructure, execute voter contact strategies, manage mail campaigns, implement absentee voting, early voting, and Get Out The Vote (GOTV) efforts to mobilize voters, along with election integrity.

name: Trisha
subject: Today we honor those who served. Lauren Boebert doesn’t.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Trisha 4 Colorado
body:
| | | | | [](https://t7pkg9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001P5XiuEsN0UGXC_j27LwBNsR-_zl7dDOa6RrWR9JWExM6eW0DXV4nKWRnBP2Wqz0Y7dTlnqXZCOdWC0qr6DJKVPn2O-daBEqEgFVvpMpifxUlXgnVppmOla9pwuvi2yugKOo85o3DNOSWYuVtSgzM1n7jZhetVdTN473b5U5wmrqklb-OFCir4IqKX9waXo4Dl9hBNTV2z1-x3_MCz3ZlErxFe3QHmR4YzP9QjL7DNtc=&c=i5D8Nv6zY2pGj-acIpPiqcRNCPq2_LL9njhQ4309pKQWRThrV327Cw==&ch=yapl3tq-iSlK_qZvvTZ3BZBn3A45HjNt5b9kqYwFIcQUp2pL_3BMfA==)
---
|

Peter,
Every day, especially today on Veterans Day, we should honor the courage and sacrifice of American veterans. For me, this is personal.
My Grandpa Joe was a medic in the North African Campaign during World War II. He was lucky to survive after a bomb landed outside his foxhole without detonating (see pic below). His bravery and dedication to helping others shaped the person I am today.
---
| [](https://t7pkg9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001P5XiuEsN0UGXC_j27LwBNsR-_zl7dDOa6RrWR9JWExM6eW0DXV4nKWRnBP2Wqz0Y7dTlnqXZCOdWC0qr6DJKVPn2O-daBEqEgFVvpMpifxUlXgnVppmOla9pwuvi2yugKOo85o3DNOSWYuVtSgzM1n7jZhetVdTN473b5U5wmrqklb-OFCir4IqKX9waXo4Dl9hBNTV2z1-x3_MCz3ZlEtWWcxn4nVTvkcQ1UtdOJgk=&c=i5D8Nv6zY2pGj-acIpPiqcRNCPq2_LL9njhQ4309pKQWRThrV327Cw==&ch=yapl3tq-iSlK_qZvvTZ3BZBn3A45HjNt5b9kqYwFIcQUp2pL_3BMfA==)
---
Unfortunately, my Congresswoman Lauren Boebert doesn't honor our veterans.
In 2022, she voted against the PACT Act, a landmark law that expanded health care and benefits for veterans exposed to toxic substances like burn pits and Agent Orange.
Her opposition to this bill was so venomous that at the State of the Union she heckled Danielle Robinson, the widow of Sergeant Heath Robinson, who the bill was named after.
After this I met with Danielle and her mom, heard their story and even did an ad with them last year. Then when Boebert and I debated last year, I pressed her for an answer on why she opposed this common sense bill. She cited not wanting to increase big government spending.
---
| [](https://t7pkg9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001P5XiuEsN0UGXC_j27LwBNsR-_zl7dDOa6RrWR9JWExM6eW0DXV4nKWRnBP2Wqz0Y7dTlnqXZCOdWC0qr6DJKVPn2O-daBEqEgFVvpMpifxUlXgnVppmOla9pwuvi2yugKOo85o3DNOSWYuVtSgzM1n7jZhetVdTN473b5U5wmrqklb-OFCir4IqKX9waXo4Dl9hBNTV2z1-x3_MCz3ZlEvYxXHvJiAkMqRQC6i9tQaA=&c=i5D8Nv6zY2pGj-acIpPiqcRNCPq2_LL9njhQ4309pKQWRThrV327Cw==&ch=yapl3tq-iSlK_qZvvTZ3BZBn3A45HjNt5b9kqYwFIcQUp2pL_3BMfA==)
---

Boebert still went on to back a plan that will cut PTSD treatment and mental-health care for our nation's heroes (also under the auspices of shrinking the budget).
She will back unlimited military spending and foreign wars but when our veterans return home from fighting in these wars she kicks them to the curb. That isn't right.
She is vulnerable and remains in Congress because of just 546 votes. In 2024 when we went head to head, I had the best performance a Democrat has ever had in this district whereas she underperformed Trump in every county. In 2026 we can beat her but I will need your help.
This Veterans Day, I'm asking you to stand with me to defend the men and women who gave so much for our country from Lauren Boebert. [The biggest way you can support this effort to fire her, flip this seat, and take back the House, is with a monthly recurring contribution of whatever amount is meaningful to you.](https://t7pkg9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001P5XiuEsN0UGXC_j27LwBNsR-_zl7dDOa6RrWR9JWExM6eW0DXV4nKWRnBP2Wqz0Y7dTlnqXZCOdWC0qr6DJKVPn2O-daBEqEgFVvpMpifxUlXgnVppmOla9pwuvi2yugKOo85o3DNOSWYuVtSgzM1n7jZhetVdTN473b5U5wmrqklb-OFCir4IqKX9waXo4Dl9hBNTV2z1-x3_MCz3ZlEp5ZUVQsyH4FMo4bGgO-mRU=&c=i5D8Nv6zY2pGj-acIpPiqcRNCPq2_LL9njhQ4309pKQWRThrV327Cw==&ch=yapl3tq-iSlK_qZvvTZ3BZBn3A45HjNt5b9kqYwFIcQUp2pL_3BMfA==)
---

**High school dropout. Waffle House waitress. First woman to graduate from The Citadel. Single mom. Fighter.** **Nancy Mace wasn’t born into power. She worked for every inch of ground she’s gained.** At 17, she dropped out of high school and took a job slinging hash browns at Waffle House to survive. No silver spoon. No Ivy League connections. Just grit, grace, and a whole lot of graveyard shifts. **By the grace of God, she turned that struggle into a story no politician can touch.** She made history as the first woman to graduate from The Citadel, South Carolina’s legendary military college. She built her own business. She raised two kids on her own. And when the Left came for everything she believed in, she stood her ground.Now, she’s running for Governor of South Carolina. Nancy Mace is a battle-tested conservative who’s walked through fire and come out swinging. In Congress, she’s been one of President Trump’s fiercest allies, fighting to secure our border, protect our daughters, and stop the Left’s sick agenda dead in its tracks. **She’s not here to make friends. She’s here to make history.** Nancy is fighting to:
* Eliminate South Carolina’s Income Tax—the highest in the Southeast
* Prosecute violent criminals and pedophiles with zero leniency
* Crack down on sanctuary sheriffs who let illegal aliens roam free
* Defund radical public universities that indoctrinate, not educate
* Reform education so students learn how to think—not what to think
* Work hand-in-hand with President Trump to put South Carolina FIRST

Through every hardship, Nancy never gave up. And now she’s ready to do what she’s always done: roll up her sleeves, take the heat, and lead from the front. **South Carolina is worth fighting for. And Nancy Mace is just getting started.**

clean_body: Please read what I have to say about my daughter| | | | | | | | Hi, This is Brigadier General James Emory Mace, U.S. Army (Retired). Today, I write to you not as a soldier... but as a father. I'm a Purple Heart veteran, and I’ve seen battle. I’ve worn the uniform. I’ve buried friends who never made it home. And I’ve felt the sting of a Nation that sometimes forgets what our sacrifices were for. Nancy was raised in the shadow of the flag. She saw the uniform. She lived with the deployments. She knew the cost. And instead of running from it, she ran toward it. When Nancy was 17, she dropped out of high school. Most kids in that situation fall apart. But not Nancy.She picked herself up, took a job at Waffle House, and fought her way back. By 21, she became the first woman to graduate from The Citadel, South Carolina’s Military College. A place I knew well. Nancy then made it to Congress, where she represented the people of South Carolina’s First District. She’s been fighting her whole life; for herself, for her kids, for the truth, and now...for her state. It’s hard sitting back as a father and hearing the terrible things the media and the establishment say about her. I want to ask you something from one parent, one patriot, to another: She’s up against a machine that wants to erase the values of faith-based, service-minded Americans who believe in truth, family, and country. Nancy stood up for me. She stood up for EVERY veteran who was forgotten. She stood up when sanctuary sheriffs released illegal alien criminals onto South Carolina streets. She stood up when they tried to let biological men compete in women’s sports. She stood up when other Republicans stayed silent. And now, she’s standing up for YOU. But she needs your backup. _ Will you answer it with me today? Nancy’s not running to make friends. She’s running to win. To fight. To serve. That’s what she learned growing up in our home. That’s what she learned watching me go off to war. And that’s what she’ll carry with her to the Governor’s Mansion. Please, on this solemn day, join me in standing with my daughter. # A Moment of Truth with CHD
---
| |
---
| This weekend, Children’s Health Defense's annual conference brought incredible energy to Austin. It was an honor to have this powerful event right here in Texas! As **proud bronze sponsors** , Texans for Vaccine Choice was thrilled to connect with so many likeminded advocates, leaders, and families who **share our commitment to medical liberty**. Throughout the weekend, conversations centered around **healing, unity, collaboration, and strengthening our movement** as one extended family. From inspiring speakers to meaningful connections, the conference was a reminder that when we stand together our impact multiplies. We’re grateful to CHD for bringing their vision and community to the Lone Star State and to everyone who stopped by to connect with TFVC. **The momentum for medical liberty is growing stronger than ever!**

---

clean_body: | | Powerful voices. Bold ideas. Real impact. | | | | | | A Moment of Truth with CHD | | | This weekend, Children’s Health Defense's annual conference brought incredible energy to Austin. It was an honor to have this powerful event right here in Texas! As proud bronze sponsors , Texans for Vaccine Choice was thrilled to connect with so many likeminded advocates, leaders, and families who share our commitment to medical liberty. Throughout the weekend, conversations centered around healing, unity, collaboration, and strengthening our movement as one extended family. From inspiring speakers to meaningful connections, the conference was a reminder that when we stand together our impact multiplies. We’re grateful to CHD for bringing their vision and community to the Lone Star State and to everyone who stopped by to connect with TFVC. The momentum for medical liberty is growing stronger than ever! | | | TOMORROW: Aaron Siri LIVE | Sponsored by Don Huffines for Comptroller | | | | | | LAST CALL FOR TICKETS! | Tomorrow Aaron Siri, America’s leading legal advocate for vaccine choice, will join us LIVE, in-person for an inspiring keynote and Q &A on his new book: Vaccines, Amen. Each ticket includes a copy of the book, with a VIP hour for a personal meet-and-greet and signing. | | | | News | | | | | | Candidate Surveys | The filing window for the March Primary is officially open! Do you know of a candidate running? Voters want to know where they stand on medical liberty! Candidates who complete the TFVC survey will gain consideration for our voter guide. name: Trisha
subject: Today we honor those who served. Lauren Boebert doesn’t.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Trisha 4 Colorado
body:
| | | | | [](https://t7pkg9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001ShkntoUyHY-r_sh_JLK__nRKtdl0LLpfTSL-5ZnotA8FDhc_SED87o4us8OYsWkX9prVtm-eHaPmgTuwOFwwilJ7ULYdq3YX7G5iu9Yz65ThPVJDeQAqPCfJ-i-vbRgb7pCWRzedQfsM6vSJW5Lj_uU6t-GOU4YIk2lYsKLpN-VU-GF-Rp7_S2OCm5Iqh_hcc_8ycFop0_kjV26qjP5mz4EwgHR0Cg44&c=7psWI_I5oy3B4KjrKuLUeEQIcuag8u_z14chdj1aWAFDUgapjvgvzQ==&ch=xuDOjuF4Wa3gqz8Ve8vXkws4TJDy4gvOljB1lkrFwlBB0AXevdrV4g==)
---
|

Peter,
Every day, especially today on Veterans Day, we should honor the courage and sacrifice of American veterans. For me, this is personal.
My Grandpa Joe was a medic in the North African Campaign during World War II. He was lucky to survive after a bomb landed outside his foxhole without detonating (see pic below). His bravery and dedication to helping others shaped the person I am today.
---
| [](https://t7pkg9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001ShkntoUyHY-r_sh_JLK__nRKtdl0LLpfTSL-5ZnotA8FDhc_SED87o4us8OYsWkX9prVtm-eHaPmgTuwOFwwilJ7ULYdq3YX7G5iu9Yz65ThPVJDeQAqPCfJ-i-vbRgb7pCWRzedQfsM6vSJW5Lj_uU6t-GOU4YIk2lYsKLpN-VU-GF-Rp7_S2OCm5Iqh_hcc_8ycFop0_kjV26qjP5mz8S3-g2YQijN&c=7psWI_I5oy3B4KjrKuLUeEQIcuag8u_z14chdj1aWAFDUgapjvgvzQ==&ch=xuDOjuF4Wa3gqz8Ve8vXkws4TJDy4gvOljB1lkrFwlBB0AXevdrV4g==)
---
Unfortunately, my Congresswoman Lauren Boebert doesn't honor our veterans.
In 2022, she voted against the PACT Act, a landmark law that expanded health care and benefits for veterans exposed to toxic substances like burn pits and Agent Orange.
Her opposition to this bill was so venomous that at the State of the Union she heckled Danielle Robinson, the widow of Sergeant Heath Robinson, who the bill was named after.
After this I met with Danielle and her mom, heard their story and even did an ad with them last year. Then when Boebert and I debated last year, I pressed her for an answer on why she opposed this common sense bill. She cited not wanting to increase big government spending.
---
| [](https://t7pkg9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001ShkntoUyHY-r_sh_JLK__nRKtdl0LLpfTSL-5ZnotA8FDhc_SED87o4us8OYsWkX9prVtm-eHaPmgTuwOFwwilJ7ULYdq3YX7G5iu9Yz65ThPVJDeQAqPCfJ-i-vbRgb7pCWRzedQfsM6vSJW5Lj_uU6t-GOU4YIk2lYsKLpN-VU-GF-Rp7_S2OCm5Iqh_hcc_8ycFop0_kjV26qjP5mz0kCcnPXCRGh&c=7psWI_I5oy3B4KjrKuLUeEQIcuag8u_z14chdj1aWAFDUgapjvgvzQ==&ch=xuDOjuF4Wa3gqz8Ve8vXkws4TJDy4gvOljB1lkrFwlBB0AXevdrV4g==)
---

Boebert still went on to back a plan that will cut PTSD treatment and mental-health care for our nation's heroes (also under the auspices of shrinking the budget).
She will back unlimited military spending and foreign wars but when our veterans return home from fighting in these wars she kicks them to the curb. That isn't right.
She is vulnerable and remains in Congress because of just 546 votes. In 2024 when we went head to head, I had the best performance a Democrat has ever had in this district whereas she underperformed Trump in every county. In 2026 we can beat her but I will need your help.
This Veterans Day, I'm asking you to stand with me to defend the men and women who gave so much for our country from Lauren Boebert. [The biggest way you can support this effort to fire her, flip this seat, and take back the House, is with a monthly recurring contribution of whatever amount is meaningful to you.](https://t7pkg9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001ShkntoUyHY-r_sh_JLK__nRKtdl0LLpfTSL-5ZnotA8FDhc_SED87o4us8OYsWkX9prVtm-eHaPmgTuwOFwwilJ7ULYdq3YX7G5iu9Yz65ThPVJDeQAqPCfJ-i-vbRgb7pCWRzedQfsM6vSJW5Lj_uU6t-GOU4YIk2lYsKLpN-VU-GF-Rp7_S2OCm5Iqh_hcc_8ycFop0_kjV26qjP5mz5oLUh0vIO8t&c=7psWI_I5oy3B4KjrKuLUeEQIcuag8u_z14chdj1aWAFDUgapjvgvzQ==&ch=xuDOjuF4Wa3gqz8Ve8vXkws4TJDy4gvOljB1lkrFwlBB0AXevdrV4g==)
---

name: Drain The Swamp
subject: Nancy Pelosi calls it quits
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Common Sense Club Inc
body:
Who should follow her out the door next

Friend,

**[Nancy Pelosi-- the lifelong politician and radical Leftist -- has officially announced her RETIREMENT from politics.](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zndivq7aeogg3dvar74a9d3ffpi6h5a9r7ntedtg_rp22sh2s8i6eor2c3ge2p72clh2bs "Nancy Pelosi — the lifelong politician and radical Leftist — has officially announced her RETIREMENT from politics.")**

After 37 long and grueling years, it's like a weight was lifted off America's shoulders.

**Even President Trump couldn 't hide his relief, saying:**

_" The retirement of Nancy Pelosi is a great thing for America. She was evil, corrupt, and only focused on bad things for our country."_

**But let 's be honest **- it's hard to celebrate too much when _Chuck Schumer_ and _Hakeem Jeffries_ are still calling the shots.

name: With Honor Action
subject: Veterans Day: A reminder of what unites us 🇺🇸
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY WITH HONOR ACTION, INC
body:
| | | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/withhonor/invitation-two-parties-two-veterans-one-conversation-rep-lalota-rep-ryan-special-session-with-president-carter-alumni-14054944?e=33f53a5b1a)
---
| | | | [ ](https://withhonor.us16.list-manage.com/track/click?u=80940e6d1b1e08928d721cf8d&id=f858064668&e=33f53a5b1a)
---
| | | | Friends,

Each year, Americans come together on Veterans Day to celebrate and say thank you to all the men and women who raised their right hands and swore an oath to serve this nation.

But in the midst of the longest government shutdown in American history, our nation needs the same courage and cooperation that veterans have demonstrated on battlefields and in communities alike.

Last month, [Gallup released a poll conducted in partnership with the With Honor Institute](https://withhonor.us16.list-manage.com/track/click?u=80940e6d1b1e08928d721cf8d&id=fb3f0f86fd&e=33f53a5b1a), which found that **majorities of Americans trust veterans to put the country ahead of party, to bridge divides, and to lead in crisis.**
---
|
---
| For Veterans Day, With Honor Co-Founder & CEO Rye Barcott submitted [an essay to the Ripon Forum](https://withhonor.us16.list-manage.com/track/click?u=80940e6d1b1e08928d721cf8d&id=e8b8952796&e=33f53a5b1a) about the need for more principled veteran leadership in Congress. The main takeaway is this: **service is the antidote to division** , and bipartisanship can still be found in Congress in places like the For Country Caucus, where veterans choose to step up and lead.

This Veterans Day, we hope you take the time not only to thank a veteran for their service, but also for their leadership.

Sincerely,
The With Honor Action Team

subject: Nancy Pelosi calls it quits

body:
Who should follow her out the door next

Friend,

**[Nancy Pelosi-- the lifelong politician and radical Leftist -- has officially announced her RETIREMENT from politics.](http://e.goppresidential.com/rd/9z2zubocnbgpk43espm9doa262qo7j3fj38p1jfn9do_rp22sh2s8i6eor2c3geao72c1h2bs "Nancy Pelosi — the lifelong politician and radical Leftist — has officially announced her RETIREMENT from politics.")**

After 37 long and grueling years, it's like a weight was lifted off America's shoulders.

**Even President Trump couldn 't hide his relief, saying:**

_" The retirement of Nancy Pelosi is a great thing for America. She was evil, corrupt, and only focused on bad things for our country."_

**But let 's be honest **- it's hard to celebrate too much when _Chuck Schumer_ and _Hakeem Jeffries_ are still calling the shots.

This is the first step in draining the swamp, but there's still plenty of work to be done.

_So as we celebrate this small win, help us look towards the future and answer one simple question:_

**[Who should be the next Democrat to go?](http://e.goppresidential.com/rd/9z2zn850ji7u7tsa2gkmn2tsrgfr1qi8aqb7fl5bcd8_rp22sh2s8i6eor2c3geao72c1h2bs "Who should be the next Democrat to go?")**

name: Niki Conforti
subject: At Long Last
body:
At Long Last | | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/bd38af38b1d6/conforti-for-congress-10611854?e=25f4fa3311)
---
| | |
---
| | | Dear Peter, After more than a month, the Senate has reached an agreement when Jacky Rosen (D-MN) became the 8th Dem to crossover and vote with the Republicans to pass the CR (continuing resolution) and open the government.

The CR was the same one that was put forth several weeks ago - and had zero concessions to healthcare. This is a victory for Republicans! The government will open with no funding for illegals' healthcare.

The dem strategy failed. Even our own senator, Dick Durbin, came forth and stated that the Dems were willing to starve children in order to hold Trump and his policies hostage. He was one of the eight senators that crossed the aisle to vote for the CR.

While there are still more steps in the process to actually open the government and make payments, we have passed the biggest hurdle. This is a win for the people!

Please consider making a contribution to my campaign to help me flip District 6, turn it RED, and push Conservative values in Congress.

Click the link below to make a donation today.

[](https://conforti4congress.us6.list-manage.com/track/click?u=7fd34b7284bf7f5e87a4e45e5&id=afb35420d0&e=25f4fa3311)

Don't stop believing in what we can achieve when we work together!
Your support is greatly appreciated.
---
| Thank you,

Niki Conforti
Candidate
Illinois 6th Congressional District

name: Bob Fertik, Democrats.com
subject: Denounce the 8 Senate Dems Who Surrendered on Healthcare Costs
body:
Let's tell them how angry we are | | |
---
[](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.EtNV8HBC60Tl7UuGmXS3sYMMMCXQg6tLXTP6gYXpdfQq2MbQQugd2T5PkG5JzB6Ab_pm8B7-Qnw4-kUGn5TwINgMhpk8hhjnR9IgESnwhdOV4kH-wqmqKfanvVF-Kuk-PduNj2D5RQCQzRLlk7aGRtvoltj-8-rcf9v0PRulZWTRmyfTaKdW0qMuCoSuWhv-z0UvBnUUZtjM7eSiSNVqGaskJ95Ho_QIHjXHu6_A4t7MCapOorE6GFOxLGjRVDyEp6xw4LUTTTaBJpSRyJp_nROf2c_cJwo3xvfXAuLxTnPDxZSzfSaFx000BqBLvOpZ_PeT04J1GSfDUJoMTTyRjdFpOvlOxVpZ0bAIaeWcNz4GXa9IhAfgtcDf47h5YtCvIpYVSQ6BalURnjPoW6FTAPJ52flQzc2APl9mAOxSyY2P-tBtSPlXOYcnfPqSbfYd5JEBDpx5XaTWyEh7Zlx8Cg/4li/-k49YRNPS-uCbAezYG7t4w/h0/h001.8g8aKlc-VTp1jah7usxcsXCzvZjiqN5O5aJxBaJ0LDo)Hi Friend, The fight to protect affordable healthcare was supposed to be non-negotiable. For weeks, Democrats stood united through a painful government shutdown to stop Donald Trump and Republican extremists from slashing healthcare subsidies and driving up costs for millions of Americans. **But at the last minute, eight Senate Democrats broke ranks** — handing Trump a victory that will leave working families paying the price. Senators **Tim Kaine, Dick Durbin, John Fetterman, Jeanne Shaheen, Maggie Hassan, Angus King, Catherine Cortez Masto, and Jacky Rosen** voted for a deal that reopens the government without restoring the Affordable Care Act subsidies that keep coverage affordable for millions. [**Hold Democrats Accountable**](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.EtNV8HBC60Tl7UuGmXS3sYMMMCXQg6tLXTP6gYXpdfQq2MbQQugd2T5PkG5JzB6Ab_pm8B7-Qnw4-kUGn5TwINgMhpk8hhjnR9IgESnwhdOV4kH-wqmqKfanvVF-Kuk-PduNj2D5RQCQzRLlk7aGRlQlz1SbmD6MCYFvp6_juSbpO9s905wJ9cK9sXYtjl_pX_opjww5DeC0omIY2lznjPILDJ9JTJDf3_she0XsSOQLABd-Vso8l8jg1BaxVnuXi5Bc1e_nWYt4qf3zdS7WyeX04I6_JyF7ve4k9kuHI8dHMNKMUubD9pN3-CzNPYZqO2-Oxe92O_1ZqwU-QTaJB_J72KOEp3Ob9EbtEwn_kXn1tJXKzXMxvsrNThunm3DhfkWic9A_8gD8JIVDp_XiK2mF7-sDC2njnwvLcoDmwDHLcAq64sDiLgY1IToXTBzy7sEfbZcVvS8JdgxTyC3Srg/4li/-k49YRNPS-uCbAezYG7t4w/h1/h001.G9vK_Aw3oD_0a2ufm8GrKf6BCLtFb12lvSjhiJIqaMA)
**Their votes guarantee one thing: higher healthcare costs for millions of Americans**. Without these subsidies, families could see their monthly premiums double next year. Seniors will pay more for prescriptions, and younger families will face impossible choices between rent, groceries, and healthcare. **Now, it’s up to us to hold them accountable**. Add your name to publicly denounce the eight Democrats who caved to Trump and the GOP — and send a clear message that voters will not forget who stood up for affordable care and who sold it out. clean_body: Let's tell them how angry we are | | | Friend, The fight to protect affordable healthcare was supposed to be non-negotiable. For weeks, Democrats stood united through a painful government shutdown to stop Donald Trump and Republican extremists from slashing healthcare subsidies and driving up costs for millions of Americans. But at the last minute, eight Senate Democrats broke ranks — handing Trump a victory that will leave working families paying the price. Senators Tim Kaine, Dick Durbin, John Fetterman, Jeanne Shaheen, Maggie Hassan, Angus King, Catherine Cortez Masto, and Jacky Rosen voted for a deal that reopens the government without restoring the Affordable Care Act subsidies that keep coverage affordable for millions. Their votes guarantee one thing: higher healthcare costs for millions of Americans. Without these subsidies, families could see their monthly premiums double next year. Seniors will pay more for prescriptions, and younger families will face impossible choices between rent, groceries, and healthcare. Now, it’s up to us to hold them accountable. Add your name to publicly denounce the eight Democrats who caved to Trump and the GOP — and send a clear message that voters will not forget who stood up for affordable care and who sold it out. These eight Democrats threw their own colleagues — and the American people — under the bus. Every Democrat in Congress had fought side by side to hold the line, to make sure Trump didn’t win by making healthcare more expensive and less accessible. And after weeks of sacrifice and suffering during the shutdown, these senators surrendered it all. All of the pain inflicted by Trump’s shutdown — the lost paychecks, delayed benefits, and mounting hardship — now feels like it was for nothing. Their cowardice let Trump and the GOP claim victory while working families get stuck with the bill. **** Thanks for all you do! Bob Fertik, President Democrats.com p.s. | Peter,
After a landslide victory in the Pennsylvania Supreme Court retention election last Tuesday, Defend Our Courts is already getting back to work.
Why?
Because in 2026, there are three more State Supreme Court elections in key swing states--Arizona, Georgia, and Michigan.
---
| | | | | [](https://yfq78zhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001DzhkFPpfYJzPPp4aqanhB4715HE456jtVouq1cbGM2KBB21egnzJRQIScJPhiy90eXeHuWgbmFCODjbXBMtqaptVDh8bQgKmGTNjF0Y7o3Zhdghf_keckQoZvSoYMbkAv_o6jJ6XVw_3zv_G_0WykZ96gftJobGeOwfoGfw-Ch4i3KvgLrq-67B1gDXgreqYC-DOxOkBDujFThxYlWyd72FQz7YArxKiODJJdCxlRS8=&c=dUhunNTs-c69ztF4mXcCNt_xD_kWhozBmKOvaQnCcM1c6dAmCj1fKA==&ch=M6IeMAr5SwNMTBUgCVK9NIIARapIiFehykRClHQanV0-ALg7w2sc0w==)

|
The stakes will be the same as PA for these elections--MAGA seizing control of these courts will give them control over election laws in each respective state, allowing them to issue decisions that could suppress Dem votes, and tilt the odds in MAGA's favor in future elections. This would all but make the Democrats path back to the White House impossible in 2028.
So our team is already looking ahead, crunching the numbers and identifying the Democrats that we need to turn out in each of these states to prevent a MAGA takeover.
But we haven't fully turned the page on PA despite our best efforts--we spent every last penny we had to fuel Tuesday's massive blowout victory. We have bills and staff to be paid first before we can go full steam ahead in AZ, GA, and MI.
We know we've asked a lot of you recently, and we wouldn't have had the success we did without your support. But that's also why we are planning to triple down, and take our winning formula to defend three more crucial courts in '26.
[So we're asking you today: Can you please make a donation of $50, $25, or whatever amount is meaningful to you to help us pay our team, close the books on PA, and move on to leading the defense of courts in GA, AZ, and MI?](https://yfq78zhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001DzhkFPpfYJzPPp4aqanhB4715HE456jtVouq1cbGM2KBB21egnzJRQIScJPhiy90BjOGEn9uBoH4R-PdScYot_Ol_kTH1L11hVu6DQ7xBhLfAFjFL2H-cbJ7lSUiUsckDh7k0zhP7G6yKxNZeIYy-OrES1P9nwhbFQE-9DeElxdoYwORY4Xjldu9c9prq_Itp6a6YeNe2ovIJCpkd2g0G7srry412nI9h3yHCaXUiJHyHuRD61TteQ==&c=dUhunNTs-c69ztF4mXcCNt_xD_kWhozBmKOvaQnCcM1c6dAmCj1fKA==&ch=M6IeMAr5SwNMTBUgCVK9NIIARapIiFehykRClHQanV0-ALg7w2sc0w==)

Antifa protesters **[reportedly rushed barricades and brawled with Trump supporters](https://substack.com/redirect/c518a27c-848f-462d-b732-2b85163a8594?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** outside a TPUSA campus event at the University of California, Berkeley, Monday evening.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/c518a27c-848f-462d-b732-2b85163a8594?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
"UC Berkeley is currently a war zone and ANTIFA has tried to rush the barriers into tonight's TPUSA event multiple times," Frontlines reporter Savannah Hernandez posted to X along with the following video. "The crowd is getting more and more rowdy."
[](https://substack.com/redirect/886d8b22-f210-426d-80d3-58710618d24f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* **Antifa agitators create chaos and mock Kirk 's assassination:** Antifa agitators lit flares causing attendees to be rushed inside, and reportedly chanted "any time, any place, punch a fascist in the face," and some were heard chanting "f*ck your dead homie" in reference to Turning Point USA founder Charlie Kirk who was assassinated in September during a campus event in Utah. **[A car backfire](https://substack.com/redirect/a42d7056-7ba9-4b20-a953-2408e58dc630?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** raised fears of gunshots within the crowd, according to X posts from the scene.
* **More violence leads to arrests:** A brawl between Antifa and Trump supporter attendees broke out, followed by arrests on both sides. A Trump supporter was captured on video bleeding on the ground while being detained.

[](https://substack.com/redirect/4cd2c87d-a035-4dfb-8d5f-63e01410d9b2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* **Four students arrested for felony vandalism:** Four UC Berkeley students, women ages 20-22, were arrested early Monday morning on suspicion of felony vandalism after officers found 28 flyers featuring Charlie Kirk quotes glued to the historic Sather Gate announcing a 4:30 pm protest rally stating "Hate is not welcome on our campus," with the vandalism causing over $400 in damages, **[The Post Millennial reported.](https://substack.com/redirect/c518a27c-848f-462d-b732-2b85163a8594?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**

Find more video footage of Monday night's chaos directly from reporters on the ground:

# **Trump says Schumer "made a mistake" with shutdown strategy, went "too far"**
President Donald Trump told Fox News' "The Ingraham Angle" Monday night that **[Senate Minority Leader Chuck Schumer went"too far" with the government shutdown](https://substack.com/redirect/a119c2ca-96cb-4f10-b99c-5c6b72c4a24d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**, saying "he thought he could break the Republicans, and the Republicans broke him" as the Senate approved legislation to end the 43-day shutdown--the longest ever.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/a119c2ca-96cb-4f10-b99c-5c6b72c4a24d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
* **Schumer faces Democratic calls to step down:** Despite Schumer joining 38 Democratic colleagues to oppose the spending bill that funds the government through January 30, 2026, while providing money for SNAP benefits and veterans programs, a growing chorus of Democrats are calling for him to step aside as party leader for failing to keep his conference united in favor of the shutdown.
* **Eight Democrats broke ranks to end shutdown:** Sens. Jeanne Shaheen, Maggie Hassan, Tim Kaine, Jacky Rosen, Catherine Cortez Masto, John Fetterman, Angus King, and Minority Whip Dick Durbin voted with 52 Republicans to end the shutdown after reaching a weekend agreement, with Democrats receiving few concessions beyond a promise of a future vote on Affordable Care Act tax credit extensions.
* **Trump criticizes Schumer 's political transformation:** Trump told Laura Ingraham that Schumer "was a pretty talented guy" who had "lost his talent," adding "I've known him since he was a person who loved Israel, and now he's a Palestinian. He's become a Palestinian," while claiming Democrats "really wanted $1.5 trillion for people that came in illegally" and criticizing Obamacare premiums that have "gone up like rocket ships."

* * *
# **Student arrested for antisemitic attack on Dave Portnoy - "F**k the Jews!"**
Patrick McClintock, a 20-year-old Mississippi State University junior and mechanical engineering major, **[was arrested and charged with disturbing the peace](https://substack.com/redirect/e94a6f62-cbb9-4792-b278-4e6ef70ec261?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** after being caught on camera screaming "F-k the Jews" and "F-k you, Dave" at Barstool Sports founder Dave Portnoy during a pizza review in Starkville, Mississippi, last week.
[](https://substack.com/redirect/b15cbf58-f719-4d57-9a35-db8036c63f87?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* **Confrontation captured on viral video:** Newly released footage shows McClintock smirking as he faced off with Portnoy, who is Jewish, shouting "Get the f-k out of Starkville" before being shouted down by bystanders and pushed away by Portnoy's security, with the hate-filled heckler also allegedly throwing objects at the Barstool founder.
* **Student withdraws from university:** McClintock voluntarily withdrew from Mississippi State University on Monday afternoon, just hours after Portnoy released the full clip of the now-viral pizza review and following his arrest for the disturbing confrontation that exploded on social media.

| Peter,
After a landslide victory in the Pennsylvania Supreme Court retention election last Tuesday, Defend Our Courts is already getting back to work.
Why?
Because in 2026, there are three more State Supreme Court elections in key swing states--Arizona, Georgia, and Michigan.
---
| | | | | [](https://yfq78zhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001W1Zdhjp6phv9poUtkWv4nZ1MlQlD6wj6WzOL8YowY96W8kO3GEFlk_mNoPombG0jmmLnm_vgn-BOKfcCKtZKZhRWXaN6faSRCxey_cGNy31hwN4uyZmNWYnt94D6hsIUoFn3pdZ584z6ilq2J84-ayGQS4P9xV3ebWRIDWeP_8XmuDgqDExYR_6mmEGqzkWSa8fnLQm7Y5RCN742ZRD2WaIS36N25_cnUMIKDtvC5Zk=&c=TdtwsEaz_6Txc-V7di2cTY2P_SUn7qoLOGjPgMa3TteQXOej-1P_xQ==&ch=Q1P_3pqUzca2rLcFEWfxO1pbbYgovWOKHFWzYh-Hp6NWEDU-xONSJA==)

The stakes will be the same as PA for these elections--MAGA seizing control of these courts will give them control over election laws in each respective state, allowing them to issue decisions that could suppress Dem votes, and tilt the odds in MAGA's favor in future elections. This would all but make the Democrats path back to the White House impossible in 2028.
So our team is already looking ahead, crunching the numbers and identifying the Democrats that we need to turn out in each of these states to prevent a MAGA takeover.
But we haven't fully turned the page on PA despite our best efforts--we spent every last penny we had to fuel Tuesday's massive blowout victory. We have bills and staff to be paid first before we can go full steam ahead in AZ, GA, and MI.
We know we've asked a lot of you recently, and we wouldn't have had the success we did without your support. But that's also why we are planning to triple down, and take our winning formula to defend three more crucial courts in '26.
[So we're asking you today: Can you please make a donation of $50, $25, or whatever amount is meaningful to you to help us pay our team, close the books on PA, and move on to leading the defense of courts in GA, AZ, and MI?](https://yfq78zhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001W1Zdhjp6phv9poUtkWv4nZ1MlQlD6wj6WzOL8YowY96W8kO3GEFlk_mNoPombG0jzuXqSl4Ob5ro4qjGyZE_u_oHy8azh4K1QYdRopLVw3ghgPralsld3Tvs_RNl5mD7h-xs45xMacgKN0FCC6s_zgMHr8KN-gZg80hitRajFKd_WrmixdWaFxFTryOoY6oYX13HKH2nQSKuKil6GNFhCiSWD4evIkk8uTKO2jGFq6QUFee7OfnV_z6Yb7mLJ5XW&c=TdtwsEaz_6Txc-V7di2cTY2P_SUn7qoLOGjPgMa3TteQXOej-1P_xQ==&ch=Q1P_3pqUzca2rLcFEWfxO1pbbYgovWOKHFWzYh-Hp6NWEDU-xONSJA==)

clean_body: | | | | | == | Peter, After a landslide victory in the Pennsylvania Supreme Court retention election last Tuesday, Defend Our Courts is already getting back to work. Why? Because in 2026, there are three more State Supreme Court elections in key swing states--Arizona, Georgia, and Michigan. | | | | | == | The stakes will be the same as PA for these elections--MAGA seizing control of these courts will give them control over election laws in each respective state, allowing them to issue decisions that could suppress Dem votes, and tilt the odds in MAGA's favor in future elections. This would all but make the Democrats path back to the White House impossible in 2028. So our team is already looking ahead, crunching the numbers and identifying the Democrats that we need to turn out in each of these states to prevent a MAGA takeover. But we haven't fully turned the page on PA despite our best efforts--we spent every last penny we had to fuel Tuesday's massive blowout victory. We have bills and staff to be paid first before we can go full steam ahead in AZ, GA, and MI. We know we've asked a lot of you recently, and we wouldn't have had the success we did without your support. But that's also why we are planning to triple down, and take our winning formula to defend three more crucial courts in '26. == | If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | | | | == | == | == | | == | == | == | | | | == We truly appreciate all that you do for us and for Democrats everywhere. **I was never supposed to be President.**

I was never supposed to survive the bullet of an assassin, **BUT I DID.**
I was never supposed to be President of the United States again, **BUT I AM.**
I was never supposed to Make America Great Again, **BUT I WILL NEVER SURRENDER!**
The great behemoth known as the Democrat Party threw everything at me: Hoaxes, Witch Hunts, Impeachments, Election Interference, Raids, Arrests, and so much more!
But the steady compass of Faith has guided my hands through it all.
NOW, the government is completely shut down, and they want **MAGA TO BE DONE.**
_We can’t take our foot off the gas!_
I was saved for this one reason: to Make America Great Again.
I was made for this, **BUT MAGA IS POWERED BY YOU.**
_That’s why I’ve launched a 24 HOUR TRUMP FUNDRAISING BLITZ, and I’m asking everyone to chip in $15 to make it one for the record books!_
Every time they count us out – count YOU out – we come back bigger, better, and stronger than ever before.
This is our moment to show the world that we will NEVER back down, NEVER give an inch, and NEVER SURRENDER in our fight to save this country.

name: Elise Stefanik
subject: I am officially running for governor of New York
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Elise for Governor
body:
You deserve to be among the FIRST to know.

You deserve to be among the FIRST to know.
| [](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2ztup6ohrba3p0diotibrhpu2usu46f6bd256c4io_rp22sh2s8i6eor2c3ge1pb1clh2bs)
---
**_OFFICIAL ANNOUNCEMENT FROM REP. ELISE STEFANIK_**
---
Friend,
---
As one of my biggest grassroots supporters, I wanted to tell you this HUGE news personally...
---
**_I’m officially launching my campaign for governor of New York – and I’d like _YOU_ to be one of our very first Founding Members!_**
---

[ACCEPT YOUR
FOUNDING MEMBERSHIP >>](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zndjb2h9mc719aistism6jv8fuolehi4t1shkgtg_rp22sh2s8i6eor2c3ge1pb1clh2bs)
---

Kathy Hochul is the WORST governor in America, and she’s brought our once great state to its knees with her crippling taxes, crushing government regulations, and pro-criminal agenda.
---
__But her embrace of communist jihadi Zohran Mamdani was the FINAL straw.__
---
**As I watched the results come in on election night, I felt it was my duty to step up to the plate and fight with everything I have to SAVE New York from this communist takeover!**
---
This isn’t just another election, Friend.
---
**_It’s the _FINAL_ _BATTLE_ to SAVE New York before it’s lost forever!_**
---
Hochul will rubber stamp Mamdani’s dangerous communist agenda that seeks to abolish prisons, seize private property, drown us in taxes, let violent illegal aliens torment U.S. citizens, and tyrannically weaponize the power of the state against anyone who disagrees.
---
**But as the next Governor of New York, I will be the _FINAL_ _FIREWALL_ that protects New Yorkers from this insanity!**
---

name: Congressman Jake Auchincloss
subject: This Veterans Day
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Massachusetts Democratic State Committee
body:
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP31/1/93809/images/Logo.png]
Dear Peter,
This Veterans Day, we commemorate with solemn gratitude American service members and their commitment to our democracy.
A century ago, my mother’s grandparents arrived in Greater Boston, fleeing the pogroms in Ukraine just before World War I. They started a business and a family in Chelsea. Their son, my grandfather, joined the Marines after Pearl Harbor.
He believed in America – and America believed in him. He had no money, but he was sharp, and the Corps sent him to school to learn engineering and medicine. He went on to pioneer the development of the prosthetic limb.
Inspired by his story, I commissioned into the Marines after college. I commanded infantry in Afghanistan and a multi-national counter-narcotics training mission in Panama. [https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP31/1/93809/images/Congressman%20Jake%20Auchincloss%20Military.jpg]
Every veteran has a story. And every veteran swore an oath. That oath is to the Constitution, not to a president or a political party.
There is perhaps no tradition more important in the United States than a nonpartisan military. This president is eroding that convention. From parades in Washington to deployments in Los Angeles, he treats troops like political props.
This Veterans Day, in honor of all those who have sworn and served, join me in making your own pledge to the Constitution. We the people will uphold it against all threats to our democracy, foreign and domestic.
In Service,
Congressman Jake Auchincloss
Congressman Jake Auchincloss is a Veteran of the United States Marine Corps.
Use of his military rank, job title, and photographs in uniform do not imply endorsement by the Department of Defense or the Marine Corps.

name: Brett Lindstrom
subject: This Veterans Day, we say thank you.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Brett Lindstrom for Congress
body:
Freedom isn’t free, it’s earned by those who wear our nation’s uniform. Thank you to all who served.
| | | | [](https://link.win.lindstromforne.)
---
| | Dear Peter,
Today, our nation pauses to honor the brave men and women who have worn the uniform of the United States of America. Veterans Day is a time to reflect on their courage, their sacrifice, and their unwavering commitment to defending the freedoms we hold dear. From the beaches of Normandy and the mountains of Afghanistan to the skies over the Pacific and the bases here at home — generations of Americans have stood watch so that liberty could endure. Their service reminds us that freedom has never been guaranteed; it’s earned and protected by those willing to stand in harm’s way for something greater than themselves.
We also recognize the families who serve alongside our veterans — the parents, spouses, and children who shoulder the quiet burden of separation, uncertainty, and sacrifice. Their strength makes our nation stronger.
As a proud Nebraskan, I’ve seen firsthand the incredible character of our veterans. They return home and continue serving their communities — as teachers, business owners, volunteers, and mentors. Their example of duty and honor inspires all of us to give back in our own way.
So today, and every day, we say: thank you.
To every veteran who has worn our nation’s uniform and to every military family who has stood behind them, we owe a debt that can never be fully repaid. Your courage and commitment make America the greatest nation on earth.
With gratitude,

---
[](https://link.win.lindstromforne.)
| | Brett Lindstrom
Republican Candidate for NE-02

---

National studies continue to rank Oklahoma as one of the worst states (if not THE worst state) for women. Oklahoma has the highest rates of domestic violence and the worst maternal death rates nationwide. They have one of the strictest abortion bans in the country, with no exceptions for rape or incest. Most recently, in the aftermath of Trump’s “Big Beautiful Bill,” more than half of Oklahoma's rural hospitals are at risk of closing.

Despite all of that, the Republican frontrunner for Governor of Oklahoma – Charles McCall, who is currently the GOP Speaker of the Oklahoma House of Representatives – has decided that the biggest issue he’d like to address with voters is that “gender is god-given, and there’s only two of ‘em.”

See below for his, truly, BANANAS, ad. You have to see it to believe it.
