God bless our veterans, God bless Tennessee, and God bless America.
Sincerely,

Tim Burchett

Their playbook is the same as always: bury the truth under a wave of dark money and lies. I’m running a different kind of campaign -- one built on trust, not transactions. _I won’t take a dime from corporate PACs or billionaires_ trying to buy our democracy. I grew up watching working people in the Valley get shut out of the decisions that impact our lives, and I’m determined to change that by building a campaign powered by _us_. | | Dorian Gray glanced at the picture, and suddenly an uncontrollable feeling of hatred for Basil Hallward came over him, as though it had been suggested to him by the image on the canvas, whispered into his ear by those grinning lips. The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him.
---

Ivan Dmytrovych Sirko was born in the early seventeenth century, approximately between 1605 and 1610. This is evidenced primarily by the results of research on Sirko's remains, according to which the Cossack ataman was 70-75 years old at the time of his death in 1680. The first researcher of Sirko's biography, the famous Ukrainian historian Dmytro Yavornytskyi, suggested that the future ataman was born in Sloboda Ukraine in the settlement of Merefa (now the city of Merefa, Kharkiv region). However, this is not true. Merefa was founded only in 1658, i.e. when Sirko was in his late teens. However, Sirko and his family lived in Merefa for a long time in his house, and after his death, his wife and some family members lived there. The fact that Merefa in Slobozhanshchyna is associated with the name of the Cossack Ataman is evidenced by another name for the town recorded in some Ukrainian chronicles, Sirkivka. Where, then, should we look for Sirko's homeland? In our opinion, the sources give grounds to localize it in Eastern Podillia, namely in the Vinnytsia region. It is worth recalling that Sirko was a colonel in Vinnytsia (Kalnytsia) in 1658-1660. As you know, at that time in Ukraine, people who came from the local area were usually elected to such positions. Let us also recall a letter from Hetman of the Left Bank Ukraine Ivan Samoilovych to the tsarist voivode Prince G. Romodanovsky of April 20, 1675*, which recounts (in Russian) Sirko's own words: "And since I was born among the Poles, I want to die there." Thus, it is clear that Sirko was born on the Ukrainian lands that were under the rule of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, not Muscovy (the latter included Slobozhanshchyna). The historian Volodymyr Borysenko's assumption that Sirko's birthplace was the hundred-man town of the Bratslav regiment Murafa, a few kilometers from Kalnyk, is correct. Now it is the village of Zhdanov, Vinnytsia region. By the way, Murafa in the Slobozhanshchyna probably originated from this Murafa, because immigrants from the Right Bank of Ukraine to the Left Bank often gave the newly founded settlements the names of their native villages or towns. It is also worth noting Sirko's great interest in Keleberda, which he founded in 1675 on the left bank of the Dnipro. For a long time, he sought from the Moscow tsar the right to own this town or sloboda, as well as the strategically important crossing of the Dnipro near Perevolochna (Sirko wanted this crossing not for himself, but for the entire Zaporozhian Lowland Army). Contrary to popular belief, we believe that Sirko did not come from a Cossack family, but from a small Ukrainian Orthodox gentry. For example, we know the name of a Podillia nobleman, Voytekh Sirko, who was married to a certain Olena Kozynska. Sources mention them in 1592. It is possible that they were relatives of the Cossack leader. Perhaps it is no coincidence that the kings of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, writing to Sirko, called him "urodzon." The word "nobleman" evokes negative associations for many people due to stereotypes instilled in school textbooks of the Stalinist era. However, this approach is not always correct. The term "nobleman" is identical to the words "knight," "chevalier," "nobleman," etc. and was commonly used not only in Poland but also in Ukraine, Belarus, and the Czech Republic. If scholars in Russia, Poland, and the Czech Republic, having contracted vulgar sociologism, no longer accuse Pushkin or Bunin, Suvorov or Kutuzov, Žižka or Kostiushko of being of noble or gentry origin, why have attacks on the Ukrainian and Belarusian gentry continued until recently? Is it not because in this way the imperial "divide and conquer" was transferred to our long-suffering history? Was it not because it was necessary to impose a nihilistic attitude toward the history of Ukraine, to "prove" that Ukrainians or Belarusians were just dark labor without national consciousness, ethnographic material doomed to build other people's empires? Meanwhile, the Ukrainian gentry produced many outstanding sons of Ukraine, fighters for its freedom. Suffice it to mention the names of Kryshtof Kosinski, Petro Konashevych-Sahaidachny, Ivan Sulyma, Ivan Mazepa, Bohdan Khmelnytsky, and his associates: Ivan and Danylo Vyhovsky, Ivan Bohun, Danylo and Ivan Nechai, Mykhailo Krychevsky, Jan Sokolovsky, Siluyan Muzhylovsky, and others. Sirko's appearance and personality do not always coincide with the conventional ideas about him that have developed in some works of art. The Cossack leader was slightly taller than average (174-176 cm), had regular facial features, and a straight nose. By the way, the great Relin intuitively caught this when he depicted him in the center of his painting "Cossacks Writing a Letter to the Turkish Sultan." It is interesting that the prototype for Sirko's image in the painting was the famous military leader General Dragomirov. Only one significant detail was not known to Repin and his consultant D. Yavornytskyi - a red birthmark on Sirko's lower lip on the right side. His contemporaries considered it a "sign of God" given to distinguish him from ordinary people. The Grand Hetman of the Crown, the future King of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, Jan Sobieski, who knew Sirko well and met him in Bratslav in 1671.

The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him. name: Seth Moulton
subject: Veterans Day
body:
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP25/1/74758/images/Email%20Wrapper%20Header%203.png]

Friend,

Today, we honor the Americans who’ve worn our nation’s uniform. Those who’ve served with courage, humility, and a commitment to something greater than themselves.

Every veteran’s story is different, but they share one thing in common: they showed up when their country needed them. They didn’t do it for recognition or reward, but because service — in its truest form — means putting others first.

[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP25/1/74758/images/IMG_8552.jpg]

I was honored to serve free meals to veterans and their families this morning at the Veterans Inc. annual pancake breakfast.

When I joined the Marine Corps after college, my mentor told me that it’s not enough to salute the flag; you have to find your own way to serve your country. Veterans Day is a reminder of that simple truth, and of our responsibility to live up to the ideals they fought to protect.

"While a bright future beckoned, they freely gave their lives and fondest hopes for us and our allies that we might learn from them the courage in peace to spend our lives making a better world for others.”

So today, I hope you’ll take a moment to thank a veteran, listen to their story, and reflect on what their service means. We owe them not just our gratitude, but our promise to keep building the kind of country they believed in when they raised their right hand.

With gratitude,

Seth
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP25/1/74758/images/moulton-header-headshot-3.jpg]

About Seth Moulton
Seth Moulton is a father, a husband, a Marine, and a Democrat with a mission to bring a new generation of leaders to Congress. He is the proud father of two girls, Emmy and Caroline, and husband to his wife, Liz. They live in Salem, Massachusetts.
Follow Seth

name: Deb Haaland
subject: Growing up in a military family taught me the importance of service and commitment
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR AND AUTHORIZED BY DEB FOR NEW MEXICO
body:
Our service members have more courage than Trump could ever hope to have.
# [](https://act.debhaaland.com/go/133?t=1&akid=5610%2E400544%2EE3LFhu)
Happy Veterans Day. Today, I can’t help but think about my parents.
Like so many New Mexicans, I grew up in a military family. My father was a U.S. Marine Corps Vietnam combat veteran. My mother served in the Navy until she got pregnant with my older sister. They taught me the meaning of service and sacrifice, and I’ve spent my life honoring those lessons.
[](https://act.debhaaland.com/go/807?c0=em_ms_dh_20251111_1_veterans.-RYkrM&amount=5&refcode=em20251111-5610&t=2&akid=5610%2E400544%2EE3LFhu)_My dad earned two Purple Hearts and a Silver Star for his service in Vietnam._
While my dad was deployed for a two-year tour in Vietnam, my mom raised the four of us kids on her own as a military wife. I remember she would sit us down at the kitchen table nearly every night to write him letters. **Even as young children, we knew very well there was a chance he would never return home.**
[****](https://act.debhaaland.com/go/807?c0=em_ms_dh_20251111_1_veterans.-RYkrM&amount=5&refcode=em20251111-5610&t=3&akid=5610%2E400544%2EE3LFhu)_My siblings and I bounced around from base to base as proud children of a Marine Corps officer._
The day my dad finally came home, my heart was whole again. 💛
**My parents taught me the importance of service and commitment. But right now, Donald Trump and his Republican allies are hurting our veterans and the people who serve them.**
It’s beyond shameful that they’re firing people who serve our Veterans, threatening essential healthcare, support services, and job security that our veterans and military families earned. Veterans are being punished again as critical VA services remain frozen, and the SNAP benefits our veterans receive to put food on the table hang in the balance.
As governor, I will always take a stand for those who’ve served our country with honor. And I will reject any attempts to rip away the benefits that veterans and their families so rightfully deserve.
**[Military service members and veterans across the country deserve leaders who will honor their sacrifices – not rip away vital services. If you can, please contribute $5 so we can win this race and stand up for our veterans.](https://act.debhaaland.com/go/807?c0=em_ms_dh_20251111_1_veterans.-RYkrM&amount=5&refcode=em20251111-5610&t=4&akid=5610%2E400544%2EE3LFhu)**
**[Donate $5 »](https://act.debhaaland.com/go/807?c0=em_ms_dh_20251111_1_veterans.-RYkrM&amount=5&refcode=em20251111-5610&t=23&akid=5610%2E400544%2EE3LFhu)**
Be fierce,
Deb
* * *
**[Donate »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_dh_footer?refcode=em20251111-1&t=24&refcode2=5610_400544_E3LFhu&akid=5610%2E400544%2EE3LFhu)**

## **HAPPY VETERANS DAY FROM PRESIDENT TRUMP!**

I LOVE the troops.
Over the past year, we’ve rebuilt the military, and our veterans are now treated with the honor & dignity that they DESERVE!
If you or a loved one has served our country, I’m proud to offer you a VETERANS FOR TRUMP hat for just today.
**_[Do you want one?](https://link.win.donaldjtrump.)_**
[](https://link.win.donaldjtrump.)
[CLAIM HAT](https://link.win.donaldjtrump.)
On behalf of EVERYONE from President Trump: **THANK YOU VETERANS!** ****
You truly make America great!
Sincerely,

[](https://link.win.donaldjtrump.)
[](https://link.win.donaldjtrump.)
President Trump

subject: A personal message on Veterans Day
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY MARLENE FOR CONGRESS
body:
| | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/119094652/581917345/-1882479441?refcode=LOC_EM_FR_2025.11.11_Veterans-Day_B1_F1_S1_C1_AC_N-T0&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTkvMS8xMDYwODgiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiNmQzZjllMGEtMzJiZi1mMDExLTgxOTQtMDAyMjQ4MjNmZjliIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=pbZrEjyPwH1PcOBmI9jsPaCFAX_21Z5VbpOSIlt8bDk=)

Happy Veterans Day, friends. Today, we honor and celebrate the brave men and women who put their lives on the line to protect our freedoms and defend our democracy. It’s infuriating to see Donald Trump breaking our country’s promises to our veterans. The Trump administration is rolling back veterans' benefits, shutting down rural hospitals they rely on, and cutting services at the Department of Veterans Affairs. **Now more than ever, we need strong champions in Congress for our service members.** It is past time for veterans to have access to health care, housing, and good-paying jobs they have earned. Today and every day, we must stand with our veterans and demand better from Washington. Let's keep raising our voices together. — Marlene Galán-Woods
| # **[Trump Pushes New Use for Tariffs That's Sparking Debate](http://trk.libertyledger.)**
---
In a bold and characteristically Trumpian move, President Donald Trump announced on Monday a sweeping new economic proposal: use U.S. tariff revenue to send $2,000 dividend checks to the vast majority of Americans — and apply the remainder to reduce the national debt. The idea, laid out in a series of posts on Truth Social, [...]
[**Read More**](http://trk.libertyledger.)
[](http://trk.libertyledger.)
# **[Internal Dem Tensions Explode Amid Shutdown Turmoil](http://trk.libertyledger.)**
---
The dust from the 41-day government shutdown may have settled in the Senate chamber, but a political firestorm is now swirling around Senate Minority Leader Chuck Schumer—this time from within his own party. Democratic lawmakers, progressive candidates, and left-wing activist groups have unleashed a wave of public condemnation against Schumer after eight Senate Democrats helped [...]

# **[NATO Country Reports Debris From Cross-Border Strike](http://trk.libertyledger.)**
---
The war in Ukraine edged unsettlingly closer to NATO’s doorstep this week, as Romania confirmed the discovery of possible Russian drone fragments inside its territory—once again exposing the growing risk of spillover from Moscow’s persistent air assaults on Ukraine’s Danube River infrastructure.
[**Read More**](http://trk.libertyledger.)
[](http://trk.libertyledger._c2V5aWQ9In0/cu5WrH7z04GbZxeakbAYrA)
# **[Pelosi Family Move Opens Lane for New Faces on Left](http://trk.libertyledger._c2V5aWQ9In0/cu5WrH7z04GbZxeakbAYrA)**
---
Christine Pelosi, daughter of former House Speaker Nancy Pelosi, officially entered the political arena in her own right this week—though not in the way many had speculated. Rather than launching a high-profile bid to succeed her mother in Congress, Christine announced Monday that she will seek a seat in the California state Senate, aiming to [...]

[](http://trk.libertyledger._c2V5aWQ9In0/PJG3pZiysq8q50fnI_YENA)
# **[Milei Welcomes Disenchanted New Yorkers with Open Arms](http://trk.libertyledger._c2V5aWQ9In0/PJG3pZiysq8q50fnI_YENA)**
---
In a moment that blended economic warning, historical perspective, and political theater, Argentine President Javier Milei issued a direct invitation to New Yorkers disillusioned by their city’s recent mayoral election: Come to Argentina. His message, delivered from the stage at CPAC at Mar-a-Lago, was far from symbolic—it was grounded in both Milei’s ideological convictions and [...]

[](http://trk.libertyledger.-gl9nD_dzHREg)
# **[MSNBC Voices Fume at Dems' Shutdown Move](http://trk.libertyledger.-gl9nD_dzHREg)**
---
If you blinked, you might’ve missed it—but make no mistake: Democrats just ended the longest government shutdown in U.S. history with nothing to show for it. And if you don’t believe conservatives pointing that out, just tune in to MSNBC, where even their own allies are sounding the alarm. The Sunday panel on The Weekend: [...]

Friend,
This is the sprint.
In **48 hours** , the **Kingsmill group rate closes** —and so does your easiest path to join Republicans from every corner of Virginia as we **build the plan for our Party’s future**.
**📍 Kingsmill Resort, Williamsburg, VA**
**🗓️ December 5–7, 2025**
This isn’t just another conference. It’s a **working Advance** to align our message, tighten our ground game, and leave with **clear targets and assignments** for 2026 and the road to 2027. We want **every Republican** in the room—grassroots leaders, candidates, elected officials, students, donors, and first-time activists.
**Why you need to be there**
* Unite the team: **one table, one plan**
* Get the tools: **field, finance, digital, legal & ballot ops**
* Hear from leaders: **candid strategy, real next steps**
* Leave ready: **county-by-county playbooks**

**⚠️ Hotel alert:** The **Kingsmill group rate expires in 48 hours.** Rooms are limited—once the block closes, standard rates and availability apply.

clean_body: | | | | DON'T WAIT: Book your Advance Hotel NOW! Friend, This is the sprint. In 48 hours , the Kingsmill group rate closes —and so does your easiest path to join Republicans from every corner of Virginia as we build the plan for our Party’s future. 📍 Kingsmill Resort, Williamsburg, VA 🗓️ December 5–7, 2025 This isn’t just another conference. It’s a working Advance to align our message, tighten our ground game, and leave with clear targets and assignments for 2026 and the road to 2027. We want every Republican in the room—grassroots leaders, candidates, elected officials, students, donors, and first-time activists. Why you need to be there Unite the team: one table, one plan Get the tools: field, finance, digital, legal & ballot ops Hear from leaders: candid strategy, real next steps Leave ready: county-by-county playbooks ⚠️ Hotel alert: The Kingsmill group rate expires in 48 hours. Rooms are limited—once the block closes, standard rates and availability apply. | | | Be there. Bring your team. Let’s build the plan together. — Republican Party of Virginia ⚠️ Important Note on Accommodations: *To secure your hotel reservation at the exclusive group rate within the RPV room block, you must purchase a ticket to the Advance. This policy ensures all attendees who are fully registered can take advantage of the negotiated lodging rates and enjoy the full Advance experience. PAID FOR AND AUTHORIZED BY THE REPUBLICAN PARTY OF VIRGINIA ==

body:

The Trump administration recently held an “Antifa Roundtable” - a staged event designed to brand Democratic organizers, grassroots fundraisers, and progressive movements as domestic threats.
They’re targeting Democratic PACs, nonprofits, and voter outreach groups in coordinated political hit jobs, claming that they're engaging in TERRORIST ACTIVITIES.
They’re calling your $5 donations “money laundering.”
This is a CRUCIAL MOMENT. We must fight back NOW.

name: Katie Porter
subject: Our veterans deserve better
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Katie Porter for Governor 2026
body:
# [](https://act.katieporter.com/go/21486?t=1&akid=40396%2E3422442%2EUj7buJ)
Peter,
On Veterans Day, I’m reflecting on how grateful I am for the brave service members who have protected and served our country.
Over **1.8 million veterans** call California home, but too many of them aren’t getting the care and respect they deserve from the federal government.
Donald Trump has significantly hurt our ability to give our veterans the care they were promised. Cuts at the Department of Veterans Affairs mean that we’re losing many critical health care workers who provide lifesaving care to veterans. And his attempt to dismantle the Department of Education has caused rising wait times to receive GI Bill education benefits.
This is unacceptable. Veterans shouldn’t have to fight through bureaucracy just to get the health care and education they were promised.
This fight is personal to me, Peter. My brother is a veteran, and I’ve seen firsthand the challenges our service members face when they leave the military. _We owe them the very best._
As governor of California, I will make sure we look after our veterans. I’ve gone toe to toe with Trump and his cronies in Washington before—and won. I’ll do it again as governor.
To our veterans: **thank you for your service.** You deserve leadership that has your back.
Sincerely,
Katie Porter

Katie is running to be California’s next governor because California and our country need a leader who will stand up to Trump to lower costs, protect our rights, and combat climate change. **[Donate ›››](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kp4g-em-footer?refcode=em20251111-40396&amounts=&t=3&refcode2=40396_3422442_Uj7buJ&akid=40396%2E3422442%2EUj7buJ)**

clean_body: Peter, On Veterans Day, I’m reflecting on how grateful I am for the brave service members who have protected and served our country. Over 1.8 million veterans call California home, but too many of them aren’t getting the care and respect they deserve from the federal government. Donald Trump has significantly hurt our ability to give our veterans the care they were promised. Cuts at the Department of Veterans Affairs mean that we’re losing many critical health care workers who provide lifesaving care to veterans. And his attempt to dismantle the Department of Education has caused rising wait times to receive GI Bill education benefits. This is unacceptable. Veterans shouldn’t have to fight through bureaucracy just to get the health care and education they were promised. This fight is personal to me, Peter. My brother is a veteran, and I’ve seen firsthand the challenges our service members face when they leave the military. We owe them the very best. As governor of California, I will make sure we look after our veterans. I’ve gone toe to toe with Trump and his cronies in Washington before—and won. I’ll do it again as governor. To our veterans: thank you for your service. You deserve leadership that has your back. Sincerely, Katie Porter Katie is running to be California’s next governor because California and our country need a leader who will stand up to Trump to lower costs, protect our rights, and combat climate change.

name: Voter Action Project
subject: Dangerous legislation
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Integrity First Voter Action Project, www
body:
Voter Action Project[https://voteractionproject.org/]
The Senate is standing up to stop the SAVE Act in its tracks, folks.
Defenders of voting rights know that non-citizen voting is already illegal, extremely rare, and that this bill is written in a way that could disenfranchise millions of eligible women across the country. Here's Colorado Senator Michael Bennet:
(Image of Michael Bennet speaking on the Senate floor) [https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/EA/EA007/1/89215/images/SAVE_MB.jpg]
Opponents of voting rights in Congress -- and in the White House -- favor this bill because they know it could suppress millions of eligible voters, changing the composition of the electorate and potentially empowering them and their party for years to come.
Please, Team, join these brave Senators today. Fuel our efforts to stop this dangerous legislation with $25 or whatever you can and ensure millions of women's voices continue to be heard in our elections >> [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?refcode=111125_1&amount=25]
If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:
GIVE $15 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?express_lane=true&refcode=111125_1&amount=15] GIVE $25 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?express_lane=true&refcode=111125_1&amount=25] GIVE $50 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?express_lane=true&refcode=111125_1&amount=50]
GIVE $100 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?express_lane=true&refcode=111125_1&amount=100] GIVE $250 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?express_lane=true&refcode=111125_1&amount=250] OTHER AMOUNT [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?refcode=111125_1&amount=35]
We know the SAVE Act was fast-tracked by opponents of voting rights to be in place before the next election, folks. This bill is a stunning assault on our fundamental right to vote, and we can not afford to let this anti-democratic legislation become law.
If you missed it: This bill would force every voter to present proof of citizenship to register, either a passport or a birth certificate that matches their current first and last name -- something millions of eligible American women don’t have. And that’s the point.
The bill would also obliterate popular methods of voter registration for all voters, including:
* Registration by mail

* 3rd party voter registration drives

* Online voter registration in states that allow it

* Automatic voter registration
Some estimates say that this bill could disenfranchise anywhere from 11 million to 65 million eligible Americans , especially women and those who live in under-resourced communities.
“ There’s never been an attack on voting rights out of Congress like this ,” said Alexander Keyssar, a professor of History and Social Policy at the Harvard Kennedy School, and a leading historian of voting rights. “ It’s always been the federal government trying to keep states in check on voting rights, for the most part. ”
The consequences of a bill like this passing would be simply catastrophic for our democracy. Chip in with $25 or whatever you can today to stop the SAVE Act and other voter suppression bills from becoming law >> [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?refcode=111125_1&amount=25]
https://voteractionproject.org/ [https://voteractionproject.org/]
https://www.facebook.com/voteraction [https://www.facebook.com/voteraction] https://bsky.app/profile/voteractionproject.org [https://bsky.app/profile/voteractionproject.org] https://www.instagram.com/voteractionproj/ [https://www.instagram.com/voteractionproj/] https://www.linkedin.com/company/voter-action-project/ [https://www.linkedin.com/company/voter-action-project/]
Integrity First Voter Action Project is dedicated to expanding ballot access and increasing voter participation across the country.
Since the Supreme Court gutted key provisions in the Voting Rights Act, extreme politicians have taken advantage to pass harsh laws that restrict access to the ballot box. These restrictions particularly affect disadvantaged communities and people of color. It’s our goal to bring awareness to these laws wherever they may crop up and organize voters to take action to ensure the right to vote is available to everyone in their states and communities.
Your support is crucial to our efforts. Will you donate today to help us protect voting rights? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/integrity-first-voter-action-project-1?refcode=111125_1&amount=20]
Donate Now [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/integrity-first-voter-action-project-1?refcode=111125_1&amount=20]

Patriot, For more than two decades, American Veterans Center has been dedicated to honoring the sacrifice and preserving the legacy of our nation's heroes. Each year, we host the **National Memorial Day Parade in Washington, D.C.** , bringing Americans together in a powerful display of remembrance and patriotism. We also proudly produce **" American Valor: A Salute to Our Heroes,"** a nationally televised tribute to the men and women who have served across generations.

But our work goes far beyond public events. At the heart of our mission is a commitment to **preserve the stories of veterans from every era of service** -- from the final voices of World War II, to those who served in Korea, Vietnam, and the post-9/11 generation. Through these efforts, we ensure that future generations understand the cost of freedom and the courage it has taken to defend it.

This year, American Veterans Center is marking historic military milestones: the 250th anniversaries of the U.S. Army, Navy, and Marine Corps...the 80th anniversary of the victories that won World War II...and key events in the Korean War 75 years ago, and the Vietnam War which ended 50 years ago this year.
---
[](http://links.justandfreegop.com/u/click?_t=8ca5ef3d31cc43bcb1fbe6968d953911&_m=30bff594815e4c73937bbe94420894b1&_e=os3c3GWHcg5ebS5OFdXgJbL16AkDQjTU7QpHe_GI-pnCrtQ8EPAlpX-_tYg4yA3lbzODJjwWkuA9f9LlNsrek--KJIyBfyeDDDhddL6r3MLvv42jBbgT_J6dCv9t69LbEWQo62XBrnk2DKRTx6eBAzA_Ai36Fxw9NWw7rPTHnIkzdHpJVdzjwVC9FjwjFoZQfXtWPTtf1Vz-eUJqXvn9jEE8gZ6145thURjEnVP53Rxtci-q8K2nPVQLrB9oGqBhxDQpDrh4grB9PU6QdpcNszwmizJXVyX2c1elGFwMEIZ-WYzgI17fYIlu3O2gRshuFvQHq-iDwysthllVprIZpj9LaOOz-02nANomyLkFGDexXk2_w0qvnutbFaL22xiwVbc9mGYQh7Xc1MK61rXpazlqZ5rYRR5bdc81Jn9kNOTz3SeySzLfxLK1lUjjS9l5SNkBOBniKMefCMJoHn9ViE8lnshanFc0DzxLFCBEauHQBh5EAN6uzC_nTRNnqaBwEPPAPmcx7lAj8z9z4QbUBpZGVjDkA5DIc2hjuZ72e8nuwwrKWmBcYUcWi3QDyTjemOn41NZQBIorQQitHYHQPX1UiMppDREBkc2jVkk2e3W3Vgrr0v3Kzvkh7X53J6XCQsFOvJGo0OTOc0SVkJan42ux5vRWvPeHZJT7ILlyHtJzGpOZv-BCorhrNShwgJVCwfyHQ01Uo8gz9_gEXM6G2_TddXL6lWBYtU9a3v8S8YrIcDAAbLSrljAhFU4OQ5NTTySPrp3wZpTrf6ZOUrvCag%3D%3D)

This is one of our most ambitious efforts ever, Patriot -- a national salute to those who fought to make and keep America free.

**Together, we 're capturing the stories of WWII veterans before it's too late...and preserving seldom-heard accounts from those who fought in Korea, Vietnam, and the post-9/11 wars.**

And with your gift today, I'll send you a free, full-size American flag as my thank you.

To some, it may look like just a flag.

_But to those who served, it 's a promise kept._

It flew over the beaches of Normandy.

It sailed with the Pacific fleet.

It was stitched into the sleeves of every soldier who never made it home.

**This flag reminds us of all they sacrificed, and what we 're now called to defend.**

Let it fly proudly as a reminder that freedom isn't free -- and that this generation will not forget the sacrifices of the last.

[**So please, Patriot make your gift today. Help us preserve their legacy while there's still time — and proudly fly the flag they fought for.**](http://links.justandfreegop.com/u/click?_t=8ca5ef3d31cc43bcb1fbe6968d953911&_m=30bff594815e4c73937bbe94420894b1&_e=os3c3GWHcg5ebS5OFdXgJbL16AkDQjTU7QpHe_GI-pnCrtQ8EPAlpX-_tYg4yA3lbzODJjwWkuA9f9LlNsrek--KJIyBfyeDDDhddL6r3MLvv42jBbgT_J6dCv9t69LbEWQo62XBrnk2DKRTx6eBAzA_Ai36Fxw9NWw7rPTHnIkzdHpJVdzjwVC9FjwjFoZQfXtWPTtf1Vz-eUJqXvn9jEE8gZ6145thURjEnVP53Rxtci-q8K2nPVQLrB9oGqBhxDQpDrh4grB9PU6QdpcNs2SxEp2aQ2jnwr-hTdqJs6cRiBsCtMwE6bZ4xH9Ylzva1GfDNkxzy4V5LXFSyA6_wFHXcsq55x5Avv6hnoJfOJZf4JcFO5x51m_tICmrDawV5hSHANRil1ALr0PIAEkBX_BaJpuJ93czEjc9zMlq832s_gVqbwy2XgUjrOTudNAVeLT0d43raf0RM-oFK-gqAPQIxZzhy8Ybxf3dAWxdtrGY0FpuEs-dwKqfGuz2bgaZlnvClbLGuwomsAPInXkW5si3abWUTcYG08wxkqlLlLyWD7jsVEBqoOuJsx0dWjwdi_s5kATfx5HK-C8Qr7oZRmxlLXLQ31Zep0dNS7CYBAJayCXKE6yPcAo8wWrIv9YTc0Zr7g8XQDR_vj0JLvquUy8h7RUd_WTXHi5bZK4zpDwnEuGiidtcdiRoKLrSWCwk51QQBlef_sOetMig4FETm33pTLQ45LaWL2pdU8SjQH1OJC0bEFB1COD9_woWNmix458USregJlmpo8rjhMyPwQ%3D%3D)

Patriot throughout American history, our nation's flag has stood tall.

**I hope you 'll take this opportunity to receive your American flag and fly it proudly, today and every day as a symbol AND promise of freedom.**

Thank you for standing with America's heroes -- past, present, and future.

Sincerely,

Tim Holbert

clean_body: This flag tells their story — claim yours now| | | Patriot, For more than two decades, American Veterans Center has been dedicated to honoring the sacrifice and preserving the legacy of our nation's heroes. Each year, we host the National Memorial Day Parade in Washington, D.C. , bringing Americans together in a powerful display of remembrance and patriotism. We also proudly produce " American Valor: A Salute to Our Heroes," a nationally televised tribute to the men and women who have served across generations. But our work goes far beyond public events. At the heart of our mission is a commitment to preserve the stories of veterans from every era of service -- from the final voices of World War II, to those who served in Korea, Vietnam, and the post-9/11 generation. Through these efforts, we ensure that future generations understand the cost of freedom and the courage it has taken to defend it. This year, American Veterans Center is marking historic military milestones: the 250th anniversaries of the U.S. Army, Navy, and Marine Corps...the 80th anniversary of the victories that won World War II...and key events in the Korean War 75 years ago, and the Vietnam War which ended 50 years ago this year. We may never have another opportunity like this to recognize their sacrifice on a national scale. This is one of our most ambitious efforts ever, Patriot -- a national salute to those who fought to make and keep America free. Together, we 're capturing the stories of WWII veterans before it's too late...and preserving seldom-heard accounts from those who fought in Korea, Vietnam, and the post-9/11 wars. And with your gift today, I'll send you a free, full-size American flag as my thank you. To some, it may look like just a flag. But to those who served, it 's a promise kept. It flew over the beaches of Normandy. It sailed with the Pacific fleet. It was stitched into the sleeves of every soldier who never made it home. This flag reminds us of all they sacrificed, and what we 're now called to defend. Let it fly proudly as a reminder that freedom isn't free -- and that this generation will not forget the sacrifices of the last. Patriot throughout American history, our nation's flag has stood tall. I hope you 'll take this opportunity to receive your American flag and fly it proudly, today and every day as a symbol AND promise of freedom. Thank you for standing with America's heroes -- past, present, and future. Sincerely, Tim Holbert President American Veterans Center The mission of the American Veterans Center and the World War II Veterans Committee is to preserve and promote the legacy and experiences of America's veterans and active duty service personnel from World War II through today. A non-profit educational organization, the AVC and WWIIVC are funded solely through generous contributions from people like you. All contributions are tax-deductible. (C) American Veterans Center 1100 N. Glebe Road Suite 910, Arlington, VA 22201

name: The Flyover
subject: Let’s Build the Future of Reader-Owned News—Together
body:
The Flyover is almost 2,000 reader-owners strong--and your investment can help us cross the finish line.

---
| | |
---
| |
#
---
Dear Reader,

Will you join me in accomplishing something special?

Yesterday, we kicked off a 12-day sprint to reach 2,000 _Flyover_ investors. And as I am writing this morning, we're just 300 investors away from that goal. If you've been waiting for the right moment to invest--this is it.

Be part of something bigger than a news outlet--be part of a special movement. Every new reader-investor brings us closer to 2,000 reader-owners standing up for fact-based, trustworthy journalism. name: Kirsten Gillibrand
subject: re: Veterans Day
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Gillibrand for Senate
body:
Hi folks,

This Veterans Day, we honor the brave Americans who served our nation at home and abroad.

We need to do more than just thank veterans for their service — we need to fight to get them the health care and benefits they have earned and deserve.

That’s why I fought to pass the PACT Act, expanding health care and disability benefits to veterans suffering from toxic exposure. More than 1 million claims have been approved and more veterans are finally receiving long-overdue support.

These veterans served our country with pride. They and their families shouldn't be forced to fight for basic health care. But right now, under Donald Trump, the Department of Veterans Affairs is facing massive staff cuts and barriers to providing quality care to the veterans who need it.

We owe them more than our thanks — we owe them care, dignity and support.

Our work continues until every veteran gets the benefits they earned. Let’s keep fighting.

To all who served: thank you.

— Kirsten Gillibrand
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com.

clean_body: \- - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - | Patriot, When I learned that leaving Congress to become U.N. Ambassador could cost us our House Majority, I made the only choice my conscience allowed: stay and fight for America in the People’s House. Now that a jihadi Communist has been elected Mayor of New York City, duty calls once again. I am officially running for Governor to SAVE New York and stop the spread of communism before it consumes the entire nation. Few know this, but Zohran Mamdani had a secret weapon that powered his win... ...He was able to unlock over $11 MILLION in MATCHING FUNDS. Well, this is my solemn promise to you: We will harness that same secret weapon and unlock the LEGAL MAXIMUM amount of matching funds to win this race. It is a real, authentic, certified match – at 600%! No games. No gimmicks. With TWO POLLS showing that we’re already 1 POINT AHEAD , I’m humbly asking for your help to WIN the governor’s race. New York’s motto is Excelsior – ever upward. We take bold risks. We strive for greatness. We do not settle for less than the best. As a native New Yorker, President Trump lives and breathes that motto. So do I. In 2013, I chose to run for Congress in New York to become the youngest member. Thanks to your support, we defied all odds. We beat the establishment. We WON. Now, our country is calling on us to be bold once again. New York is the POWER CENTER of the weaponized legal system against President Trump – Letitia James, Alvin Bragg, Judge Merchan, Judge Kaplan. The list goes on. By running for governor, we are challenging that machine HEAD ON. This is the choice before us: Do we accept a weaponized legal system, a communist jihadi, and a radical agenda that seeks to abolish prisons, seize private property, drown us in taxes, and let violent illegal aliens torment U.S. citizens? ...Or do we strive ever upward for GREATNESS and fight to FLIP New York RED? Polls prove we can win this race. But only if we take action right here, right now. So, are you with me? Will YOU help us unlock this 600% MATCH – the very same tool that Mamdani just used to win his election? Thank you, Elise Stefanik Paid for by Elise for Governor PO Box 500 Glens Falls, NY 12801

name: Brandon Herrera
subject: Own the first ever AKG Occam Warfighter
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Brandon Herrera Victory Committee
body:
Patriot,
You're not going to believe what I have in store for you as our next giveaway. But first, a little news:
Polling shows that I'm just 5% behind Tony Gonzales, 40.4% to 35.2%. Even more promising, the same poll shows me beating Tony 43.9% to 32.4% once voters learn about his personal scandal (reports of an affair with an employee who later took her own life) and his record of betraying President Trump.
The challenge for our campaign is making sure voters know who the real Tony is, and that takes resources: TV ads, digital ads, direct mail, and door-to-door canvassing. All of it is expensive.
That's why I'm so grateful for your continued support. And to show my appreciation, I've got something special for you: one lucky winner will take home the very first **AKG Occam Warfighter** , built right here in my shop in San Antonio.
This rifle, chambered in 7.62x39, features an Occam Defense rail, folding stock, and a suppressor-ready muzzle brake.
As always, every $5 you donate earns you one entry, $100 gets you twenty entries, and recurring donations count too.
Help our campaign get the word out so we can win, and get your chance to win something incredible, at [BrandonHerreraforCongress.com/Donate](https://contact.brandonherreraforcongress.com/l/6fI53a1vdu7637OWRF387YCA/o8l892x8923vq892Tlt95slzZZRA/MxBVBoxAIcpHkBdJraW6rg).
Thank you,

**Brandon Herrera**

name: Nancy Dahlstrom
subject: Thank a veteran.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Nancy Dahlstrom For Governor
body:
We owe them.| | |
---
Dear, Peter, I won't take up too much time. Today, we celebrate Veterans Day here in Alaska and across America. Alaska has the highest population of veterans among all states, and we are proud to have them living here. Every veteran deserves our respect and gratitude, not just today, but every day of the year. My son is currently serving in the Arizona Guard as an Army JAG officer, and I cannot thank him enough for his service to his country.
I also dug through some photos today and found one of my dad, who also served. His bravery and commitment have always been inspiring to me, and one of the reasons I wanted to give back to our community and serve in public office.
---
I promise to continue serving our veterans with the utmost respect and priority. Please join me in thanking a veteran or three today.
**Nancy Dahlstrom
** _Alaska's Lt. Governor & 2026 Republican Candidate for Governor_
---
---

clean_body: We owe them.| | | Dear, Peter, I won't take up too much time. Today, we celebrate Veterans Day here in Alaska and across America. Alaska has the highest population of veterans among all states, and we are proud to have them living here. Every veteran deserves our respect and gratitude, not just today, but every day of the year. My son is currently serving in the Arizona Guard as an Army JAG officer, and I cannot thank him enough for his service to his country. I also dug through some photos today and found one of my dad, who also served. His bravery and commitment have always been inspiring to me, and one of the reasons I wanted to give back to our community and serve in public office. I promise to continue serving our veterans with the utmost respect and priority. Please join me in thanking a veteran or three today. Nancy Dahlstrom Alaska's Lt. Governor & 2026 Republican Candidate for Governor | | | | | ---|---|---

name: Wesley Bell
subject: Our promise to veterans can't end when their service does.
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY BELL FOR MISSOURI
body:
Peter,

Honoring our veterans means more than saying "thank you." It means showing up for them — **not just on Veterans Day, but every single day of the year.**

More than 355,000 veterans live in Missouri, and nearly 34,000 of them call Missouri's First District home. They are our friends, family members, and neighbors. They made unimaginable sacrifices to keep us safe, protect our democracy, and strengthen the rights we often take for granted.

Because of the Republican shutdown, the Department of Veterans Affairs has halted services for nearly **one million veterans.** Veterans have already given so much, and they deserve better than partisan games that threaten their care and benefits.

_Our commitment to them shouldn't end when their service does._ **That's why I've introduced bipartisan legislation that would let military retirees and all disabled veterans continue to contribute to their retirement accounts even after they separate from service.**

To every veteran in Missouri and across the country: Thank you for your service, your sacrifice, and your unwavering commitment to our nation.

With gratitude and always in solidarity,

Wesley | [DONATE](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/119095807/581921894/-1347839382?refcode=em25_251111_vetda_enask_gq_act___WB_ettx&utm_campaign=em25_251111_vetda_enask_gq_act___WB_ettx&utm_medium=email&refcode2=b02f5200-39bf-f011-8194-00224823ff9b&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTkvMS8xMDYzODkiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiYjAyZjUyMDAtMzliZi1mMDExLTgxOTQtMDAyMjQ4MjNmZjliIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=f7JcTSAtx3N2SzT49i6lzeVv8_r9w393R9xIMuGEzoM=)

name: Ro Khanna
subject: Why I’m calling on Sen. Schumer to step down

body:
https://rokhanna.com [https://rokhanna.com]
Peter,
Sunday night, I became the first member of Congress to call on Chuck Schumer to be replaced as Minority Leader for caving to Donald Trump.
If you’re not willing to fight to stop premium hikes on 20 million Americans , then what are you willing to fight for?
The Democratic Party needs to be strong. We need leadership that will fight for working people, not fold under pressure from Trump or corporate donors.
Sign if you agree: It’s time for Senate Democrats to replace Chuck Schumer as Minority Leader and elect someone who will stand up to Trump and protect healthcare. [https://act.rokhanna.com/a/replaceschumer]
ADD YOUR NAME [https://act.rokhanna.com/a/replaceschumer]
https://act.rokhanna.com/a/replaceschumer [https://act.rokhanna.com/a/replaceschumer]
The donors and establishment won’t like me speaking out, but I’m confident that you will have my back. Will you stand with me?
Add your name now: Tell Democrats we need new leadership in the Senate that will fight for working people and protect our healthcare. [https://act.rokhanna.com/a/replaceschumer]
We cannot defeat extremism with weakness or compromise. We need courage, conviction, and clarity of purpose.
SIGN THE PETITION [https://act.rokhanna.com/a/replaceschumer]
Thank you for your activism and all that you do.
In solidarity,
Ro

name: Laura Friedman
subject: I won’t rubber stamp this Senate agenda

body:
| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/119096534/581924538/-1798313451?refcode=em-header&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDM1MzYiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiNzUwMDQxOWMtMzliZi1mMDExLTgxOTQtMDAyMjQ4MjNmZjliIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=UwwldrFcK5woX95tVu0dryKw8iXkdfEXk_Xm_NM1pHY=) Friend, I recently went into the _belly of the beast_ \- Fox News - to question why Donald Trump is more worried about redecorating his bathroom than watching millions of Americans lose healthcare or go hungry. [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/119096537/581924542/-1798313451?refcode=em-11.11.25-all-a&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDM1MzYiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiNzUwMDQxOWMtMzliZi1mMDExLTgxOTQtMDAyMjQ4MjNmZjliIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=UwwldrFcK5woX95tVu0dryKw8iXkdfEXk_Xm_NM1pHY=) Let’s be clear, friend: Even during this shutdown, SNAP should not have been cut-off. The money was there, and mandated by Congress to be used when other funding was unavailable. In fact, courts agreed. But Trump refused to follow their orders. Trump is willing to make Americans go hungry if it serves his political goals. And that’s unacceptable. Now, the Senate is hoping to push through an agreement that doesn’t address the expiration of ACA subsidies, meaning people in our district could see their insurance premiums rise by as much as 300%. Just like I said on Fox, I won’t rubber-stamp a Republican agenda that rips healthcare from millions of Americans. And since the bill coming from the Senate still could take away your ability to see your doctor and afford lifesaving medications, I’m a strong **no!** Laura | ****[Donate](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/119096540/581924543/-1798313451?refcode=em-11.11.25-all-a&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDM1MzYiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiNzUwMDQxOWMtMzliZi1mMDExLTgxOTQtMDAyMjQ4MjNmZjliIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=UwwldrFcK5woX95tVu0dryKw8iXkdfEXk_Xm_NM1pHY=)****

name: Mike Thompson
subject: Honoring our veterans
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Mike Thompson For Congress unsubscribe: https://support
body:
# [#]
Hi Peter — Today and every day we honor our nation’s veterans and the sacrifices they have made for our country.

As a combat veteran myself, I understand the true patriotism that it takes to serve. That’s why it is so important to me that veterans have all the support they have earned once they return home.

It is our duty to ensure our veterans have access to the most basic rights: healthcare, housing, and food. Yet, of the nearly 2 million California veterans, 10 percent rely on SNAP benefits for food assistance and 12 percent depend on Medicare or Medicaid for their healthcare.

We must continue to support our veterans and their families. Their service can be a reminder for us all,

Peter. Our fight is stronger when we stand together and put the greatness of our nation before our own.

I am proud to honor our veterans today and every day. Let us continue to celebrate true American patriotism together.

Thank you,

Mike Thompson

Peter, They shot my father. Then, they killed my friend Charlie Kirk. And still -- _the Left hasn't won._ But if we back down now... they will. That's why I'm asking you to stand with me -- and support an organization Charlie built to fight back and WIN: **Turning Point Action.** Charlie was assassinated for daring to teach young Americans to love this country. My father was almost murdered for refusing to give up on America. **My new book -- _Under Siege_ -- is the story of how we've endured and how YOU can help fight back.**

That's why I'm asking you to stand with me -- and support an organization Charlie built to fight back and WIN: Turning Point Action. Charlie was assassinated for daring to teach young Americans to love this country. My father was almost murdered for refusing to give up on America. Written before Charlie was taken from us, this book already highlighted that America is truly under siege. Now with him gone, I believe it's more relevant than ever. Peter, the fight is real. The stakes are high. And the moment is now. Let's make sure Charlie's voice echoes for generations.Let's show the Left we'll NEVER be silenced. We love having you in our network, but if you no longer wish to hear from us, you can[unsubscribe here](https://default.salsalabs.org/T923041e4-9437-4ae7-8818-7fd4ed1aa2be/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)._

| Peter — This is Adam Schiff. I wanted to bring you up to speed about one of Trump’s latest demands that I be punished for impeaching him and serving on the January 6th Committee. Trump publicly pressured his former personal attorney and now-Attorney General, Pam Bondi, to punish his political enemies, including me. He said that I was “guilty as hell,” that the Department of Justice “can’t delay any longer,” and that “JUSTICE MUST BE SERVED, NOW!!!” [I need you to stand with me right now. Trump is ramping up his public threats to abuse the justice system to punish me for my work to hold him accountable. Split a donation between Blue Amp Action and me today:](https://default.salsalabs.org/T1bd949bb-6a67-48a4-bfcb-e6efbe6e3455/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)

| [](https://default.salsalabs.org/T298a86e4-f7e1-4cf9-8324-e411a47ed047/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9) This public pressure campaign to punish his enemies does not happen in a vacuum. First, Donald Trump fired a U.S. Attorney in Virginia. And Trump was explicit about why he was firing him. It was because this U.S. Attorney resisted efforts by Trump and his Department of Justice to charge New York Attorney General Letitia James with the crimes that Trump already said she was “guilty” of, but for which there was no evidence. The implication is clear: if prosecutors don’t go after me and the president’s other enemies with meritless cases, they too will be fired. This is the biggest attack on the rule of law and independent justice since Watergate. After Nixon’s abuses of the Justice Department during Watergate, a wall was erected between the White House and Department of Justice to try and prevent a president from surreptitiously ordering the prosecution of his enemies. Now the wall is obliterated — and all of the corruption and weaponization is out in the open. [](https://default.salsalabs.org/T4b0df045-0d2c-440c-b90e-c6ff2843f4e8/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)
Trump won’t stop his attacks on me, and this will only get more dangerous. That’s why I need your support right now: [Can you rush $10 or more to Blue Amp Action and me right now to help me fight back against these lies, misinformation, and attacks that are designed to intimidate me into silence?](https://default.salsalabs.org/T7b5cc6bf-a51b-401b-809d-929c58e54e20/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)

| | | About Blue Amp Action: We cannot sugarcoat the current situation. Right-wing authoritarianism, defeated by a worldwide coalition during WWII, was only in hibernation. Over the past decade, it has infected democratic societies--including ours. It is as simple as it is terrifying: What we do now will define the future of our country and world. We at Blue Amp Action are dedicated to liberal, open societies, where reason and science are applied to solve our problems; where civil rights--including voting rights!--are protected for all. We fight for these values every day. Speaking up on our platforms, raising the voices and the political prospects of liberal pols and pundits. **And savvy grassroots activists like you know, just like we do, that we can’t stop watching the people we help elect:_ after the elections, we have to keep organizing support for the policies we like and pushing back against the ones we don’t._** **__When the public tunes out between elections and the only people our elected officials hear from are the paid lobbyists of wealthy corporations, our government stops being accountable to the needs of ordinary families.__** **Democracy never sleeps, and neither can we.** So we ask that you support our work, so we can continue to engage in this fight every moment of every day. So our kids grow up in a democracy, not a dystopia. Thank you for your support for all we do! — _You're getting this message because you’ve supported good causes and Democratic candidates. We need your support to continue fighting for our democracy. We love having you in our network, but if you no longer wish to hear from us, you can[unsubscribe here](https://default.salsalabs.org/Tf6467b90-7389-44ef-8302-728b9a634500/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)._

name: =?utf-8?q?Missing_Signature_=E2=9D=97=5Bprotectvoting=2Eorg=5D?=
subject: FWD: Thank Justice Sotomayor!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by VPP
body:
You’re about to lose your signature spot!
| Trump’s Supreme Court majority is on the verge of DESTROYING the Voting Rights Act — they just agreed to hear the case!

**Sonia Sotomayor is one of our ONLY fighters for democracy left on the Court, but you still haven’t signed our letter thanking her for her work.** [**We’re giving you ONE more chance to sign by 8am tomorrow, or you will lose your signature spot. THANK JUSTICE SOTOMAYOR NOW »**](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/96430?t=6&akid=21815%2E878179%2EpHoAHU) | [**Donate $3 to sign the letter now »**](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/96430?t=7&akid=21815%2E878179%2EpHoAHU)
---
Thank you,

_The Voter Protection Project_
**–BEGIN FORWARDED MESSAGE–**
_Justice Sotomayor just put the MAGA SCOTUS majority & Donald Trump to SHAME:_
[](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/96430?t=8&akid=21815%2E878179%2EpHoAHU)
**If a member of the Supreme Court — _who is supposed to be impartial_ — is sounding the alarm, _we should ALL be listening._**

Team, we’re so grateful for Sotomayor’s BRAVE leadership — but Republicans are FURIOUS with Sotomayor, and they’re _plotting revenge._

We need to show Supreme Court Justice Sotomayor that Democrats (especially Democrats in your state) have her back!

[**As one of the FEW protectors of our democracy left on the Supreme Court, we’re sending Sotomayor a letter thanking her for her incredible work. WE HAVE ONLY 18 SPOTS LEFT:**](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/96430?t=9&akid=21815%2E878179%2EpHoAHU)
[**Donate $3 to sign the letter now »**](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/96430?t=10&akid=21815%2E878179%2EpHoAHU)
---
TO: Justice Sonia Sotomayor
FROM: A proud Democrat
Subject: Thank you for taking a stand. **Dear Justice Sotomayor,

Thank you for standing up for our democracy against Donald Trump’s authoritarian agenda and blatant disregard of the Constitution.**

Your bravery continues to blow us away, time after time.

**As Trump tries to seize more and more control from Congress and the courts, your resilience is needed now more than ever.**

I’m sick and tired of the GOP letting him off scot-free — so I’m doing everything in my power to elect Democrats and ensure we appoint more amazing Supreme Court Justices like YOU in the near future.

[**I know you’re facing relentless attacks from Trump and his MAGA minions, so I’m signing this thank you letter and chipping in $3 to the Voter Protection Project to help Democrats keep fighting for justice »**](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/96430?t=11&akid=21815%2E878179%2EpHoAHU) [](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/96430?t=12&akid=21815%2E878179%2EpHoAHU) | [**Donate $3 to sign now »**](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/96430?t=13&akid=21815%2E878179%2EpHoAHU)
---
**_X A grateful Democrat_**
—The Voter Protection Project
| | Paid for by VPP
(www.protectvoting.org)
Not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. 401 2nd Ave. S, Ste. 303, Seattle, WA 98104

The Voter Protection Project is on an important mission: To end partisan gerrymandering and voter suppression by electing candidates at the state and federal levels that are forceful advocates for voting rights. Our enfranchisement agenda includes working to ensure that every state has automatic voter registration and online voter registration, convenient early voting, and independent redistricting commissions to ensure politicians are not able to draw their own maps.

Please, consider making a [one-time contribution by clicking here](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/121?t=1&akid=21815%2E878179%2EpHoAHU). **Or, to make the most powerful lasting impact and help us oust Republicans up and down the ballot who attack our right to vote, make a[monthly sustaining contribution by clicking here](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/122?t=2&akid=21815%2E878179%2EpHoAHU).**

[Click here](https://act.protectvoting.org/signup/fewer-emails/?t=3&akid=21815%2E878179%2EpHoAHU) to request fewer emails. Click here to [unsubscribe](https://act.protectvoting.org/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=14&akid=21815%2E878179%2EpHoAHU). | | [Please: Pitch In Once A Month To Protect Voting Rights »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/123?t=4&akid=21815%2E878179%2EpHoAHU)
---
[Donate Just One Time »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/124?t=5&akid=21815%2E878179%2EpHoAHU)

name: Jen Kiggans
subject: Before you head to bed
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the NRCC and not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee
body:
If you only read one thing before you head to bed tonight, please do me the honor by making it this...[](https://links.iterable.com/s/c/smhXhFO-nvN8uQsAO6S01Om8m9n9pOUeanwrSmcZ4WaqKXtiHnrI-lwgQGoJiWNx-5yZ229MqkK7uc_hfvrH7H7E-KvmpgRjsiZCbCd0oGLBNHE7ZrMQqCazRTXT3Vu7AheKT4XJ1sHBf6TsQn_dLGBBY_CgVfE4S-xwZycMSvKDybZIwaSxktceTXNJ2Gc-9knpp5I5L68V09OBWLS-5ObRFHcSh_npBNFGAn3dHWB1Hd7JdZisR-t3f4Df3Tu8M-RszYwaGMGAzm_GVxKv41B10gN0at6EATZJxC2TdNbWD-WE1klwVEsupLPkQsXp93IsZvBFZo9yTWVbnqAITzeoRfE-S_qbpiyKxBLEltsYrHPCVcXVbx3tUQ4-lJuaPJqenVlvI5h8BTcEB8K6XXe_x1HXJx_5aeyPAD9yFh7rv2jK_Y0hEs0YhboyTCtczQp2Q0VQBiLZA-aIc1ZOMqqd1nOxE1TG28rV2DDQ1yHFFVFZX3UfR7elzwmouHs6BuHnUUDu-a5HAmHz5dvCqcPfoxqmAs8s99gxK_C0Zb8UPFZl1nrKM1ndt0vOkkx_5vKJmHojPgvjcFis9vlqfI-H19HowXOtV4QeupHaCAldsedIzMEYcONBjR7CiCa2X3fVXDqNtFmC_m4RFymfLDGNQsgLv74hC_bDRCz4NFk7j3LEjHJLtfJwR92zW2VR0BNJxBY4cDqyf5C8rr5Lgv6wj7q16cayKNahYh9CrU7j732xQ0KD_zMCkkTGyYLAyXOPheRmNvyoV1FRQMpSENakTWUW7RXf108u-_k_wk9YzHCEb7gc3egHHXoDZY9Ya3VTu6eRM7jSQdd3IDmpdRm24MD2FCnRdyU_/-bQmV1vPLnMDWzmXzlW8nHDFk8T-ELVh/14)Peter, it's Jen Kiggans, and if you haven't met me yet, I'd really love to introduce myself. It'll only take a minute or two.

My dad served during the Vietnam War as a Green Beret and retired as a Colonel. My husband is a retired F/A-18 pilot in the Navy, flying in Iraq and Afghanistan. And now, I'm a Navy mom, too!

clean_body: If you only read one thing before you head to bed tonight, please do me the honor by making it this..., it's Jen Kiggans, and if you haven't met me yet, I'd really love to introduce myself. It'll only take a minute or two. grandpa served in the Navy during WWII as a postman. My dad served during the Vietnam War as a Green Beret and retired as a Colonel. My husband is a retired F/A-18 pilot in the Navy, flying in Iraq and Afghanistan. And now, I'm a Navy mom, too! As a Navy veteran serving in Congress, is more than simply voting "yea" or "nay." It's about rolling up your sleeves, doing the research, listening to your neighbors, and dedicating every day to making our country better and stronger. Now that I've been in Congress for two years, I can say with total certainty that whatever you think of the DC Swamp and its creatures, it's even WORSE than what you're imagining. For too long, egos and power struggles have stopped Washington politicians from creating meaningful change for hard-working families. It's like once these spineless politicians win their elections, they stop caring about the people who elected them to serve and represent them. My mission in Washington is the same as when I served in the Navy: . In fact, when you're being sworn into Congress or the Armed Forces, the oath starts the same: "I will support and defend the Constitution of the United States against all enemies, foreign and domestic." The career politicians would do well to reflect on those words when reporting for work in DC and at home. Service - especially to our country - has guided me my whole life, and I've been lucky to be surrounded by veterans who hold that same value so dear. know this was a long email, so from the bottom of my heart, for reading my story! -Jen Kiggans USE OF MILITARY RANK, JOB TITLES AND PHOTOGRAPHS IN UNIFORM DOES NOT IMPLY ENDORSEMENT BY THE DEPARTMENT OF DEFENSE OR ANY OF ITS BRANCHES.| | Paid for by the NRCC and not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. name: Jena Griswold
subject: Veterans Day 🇺🇸
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jena for Colorado
body:
https://www.jenaforcolorado.com/ [https://www.jenaforcolorado.com/]
Peter,
This Veterans Day, we honor the brave men and women who have served our country. Their courage, sacrifice, and commitment to protecting our freedoms are what make our democracy possible.
Let’s remember that democracy depends on each of us, just as it has always depended on those who served. The strength of our nation lies in our willingness to stand up for one another and for the values that define us.
Today, I hope you’ll take a moment to thank the veterans in your life and community for their service. Their dedication continues to inspire us all to do our part for this country we love.
– Jena Griswold

body:

# **Senate Republican Leader Brian Jones Endorses Nathan Magsig for State Senate**
---
|
---
| | **FOR IMMEDIATE RELEASE** November 11th, 2025Contact: nathan@nathanmagsig.com
---
| | | | | |
**Sacramento, CA** – Senate Republican Leader Brian Jones today issued a strong endorsement of Nathan Magsig for State Senate, pledging full support for his campaign.Nathan Magsig, a Fresno County native and former Mayor of Clovis, currently serves on the Fresno County Board of Supervisors. A proven leader for our Valley, he has been a dedicated champion for farmers and families across the region, fighting for our oil and agricultural industries, public safety, and wildfire reduction.Senate Republican Leader Brian Jones released the following statement urging his fellow legislators to join him in supporting Magsig for Senate. _“In our state’s most critical moments, we need real leaders with real experience, who will fight for common sense policies. Nathan Magsig is the fighter we need. Join me today and let’s elect a strong Valley conservative.”_ In response to Leader Jones' endorsement, Magsig issued the following statement, _“I am deeply grateful for the support of Senate Republican Leader Brian Jones. I look forward to working with him and my future colleagues in the Senate to strengthen our communities, support our farmers, and ensure the Central Valley’s voice is heard in Sacramento.”_ Leader Jones’ backing signals strong support from state Republican leadership as he urges everyone to stand together and endorse and support Nathan Magsig for State Senate. Join him and fellow caucus members, including: Senator Tony Strickland, Senator Rosilicie Ochoa Bogh, Senator Steven Choi, and Senator Kelly Seyarto.

---
| | ###
Nathan Magsig is a current Fresno County Supervisor, former Mayor of Clovis, and is running for the 12th Senate District. His family, wife Julie and their three children, reside in Clovis, CA.
---
|
---
|
---

name: Andy Kim
subject: On this Veterans Day
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Andy Kim for New Jersey
body:
I want to take a moment to say thank you to all the men and women who have served our country.
| | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/119098772/581932338/41798106?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84Nzk3MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1MDc3ZTA5MS00NWJmLWYwMTEtODE5NC0wMDIyNDgyM2ZmOWIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=EK_1b6EcULFR6n9v93tMOvamCifgxM9cHlwEPJ8VPQs=&emci=888f0d4c-7ebe-f011-8194-00224823ff9b&emdi=5077e091-45bf-f011-8194-00224823ff9b&ceid=26261389)
---

| | On this Veterans Day, I want to take a moment to say thank you to all the men and women who have served our country. Their sacrifice, their courage, and their commitment to something larger than themselves are what make our nation stronger, and our communities safer. I've made it a priority to fight for a government that works for veterans, service members, and their families. Here are a few of the ways we've worked toward that goal:
* Advocating for better mental-health and suicide prevention programs for veterans. I've supported efforts to double funding in these areas for veterans and their families.

* Standing up for veterans and service members in my communities by working to resolve issues with federal agencies and making sure the benefits promised are the benefits delivered.

* Fighting to recognize the role our veterans played in defending our country and honoring the transition they make from active duty to civilian life.

Even as we reflect today, the work is not done. Veterans are still navigating challenges around healthcare, employment, housing, mental-health support, and community reintegration. I believe America must deliver on the promise we make to our veterans: that their service is valued, their sacrifices are remembered, and their futures are honored. Today and every day, I'm committed to standing with our veterans. clean_body: I want to take a moment to say thank you to all the men and women who have served our country. | | | | On this Veterans Day, I want to take a moment to say thank you to all the men and women who have served our country. Their sacrifice, their courage, and their commitment to something larger than themselves are what make our nation stronger, and our communities safer. I've made it a priority to fight for a government that works for veterans, service members, and their families. Here are a few of the ways we've worked toward that goal: Advocating for better mental-health and suicide prevention programs for veterans. I've supported efforts to double funding in these areas for veterans and their families. Standing up for veterans and service members in my communities by working to resolve issues with federal agencies and making sure the benefits promised are the benefits delivered. Fighting to recognize the role our veterans played in defending our country and honoring the transition they make from active duty to civilian life. Even as we reflect today, the work is not done. Veterans are still navigating challenges around healthcare, employment, housing, mental-health support, and community reintegration. I believe America must deliver on the promise we make to our veterans: that their service is valued, their sacrifices are remembered, and their futures are honored. Today and every day, I'm committed to standing with our veterans. name: AOC
subject: We have to have a politics of integrity, not a politics of cowardice anymore.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress
body:
We can change the page.
#
For so long, we have had political leadership — including in the Democratic Party — that has just wanted to play it safe.
That has just wanted to be neutral. Where so many people are motivated by their own individual political careers.
We have to have a politics of _integrity_ , not a politics of cowardice anymore.
We have to stand up for what is right in this country. **We can change the page**.
We will never get past Donald Trump if we continue to elect the same people and make the same decisions that got us here in the first place.
It does not matter your positions, your content, your ideas, how experienced you are. It doesn’t matter how much you work with them — they will _still_ choose their old friends and an old generation _every single time_.
**Our future will not be different until we make a different choice.**
Together, we’ve built a movement fighting for critical issues — from Medicare for All to democratizing fundraising power in politics.
[Help us continue the fight with a grassroots contribution today.](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/go/14659?c0=aoc-email-20251111.AXm_rG&refcode=em20251111-10192-P_FND_EGR_GRM&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1&akid=10192%2E2526796%2EgPlrfm)
**[Contribute $5](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/go/14659?c0=aoc-email-20251111.AXm_rG&refcode=em20251111-10192-P_FND_EGR_GRM&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=20&akid=10192%2E2526796%2EgPlrfm)**
In solidarity,
Alexandria

name: David Hogg
subject: Our veterans deserve more than “thank you”
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY LEADERS WE DESERVE, LEADERSWEDESERVE
body:
We owe our veterans so much more than just words.
# [](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/go/852?refcode=em20251111-1&t=1&akid=2828%2E587540%2EkBbXaK)
Hi Peter. David Hogg here.
This Veterans Day, I’m thinking about my dad. He served as a helicopter pilot in the Navy, an FBI agent, a teacher, and above all, a father who never stopped showing up for his family — even after he was diagnosed with early-onset Parkinson’s.
Before he passed, I watched him and my mom fight for his health, for his dignity, and for the VA benefits he had earned, even in his final days. Unfortunately I know the story of families like ours fighting for my father's benefits while on his deathbed is not uncommon.
**No family should have to spend their final moments together battling with a system that’s supposed to support them.**
Just a few years prior to my father’s passing, I thought about that when I was pulling all-nighters outside the Senate fighting to pass the PACT Act. I sat beside veterans fighting for friends who had terminal cancer or who had passed from it or who were just simply asking for basic care after being exposed to toxic burn pits while serving our country.
I was proud to stand with them. I’d do it again in a heartbeat. Because saying “thank you for your service” isn’t enough. We have to fight for veterans — now more than ever before.
Trump cut thousands of healthcare workers from the VA, and more cuts are still being pushed. The VA was already struggling, and now veterans are waiting weeks or even months for appointments while exhausted nurses and doctors try to hold the system together.
And the cruelty doesn’t stop there. Republicans tried to strip funding from the very toxic exposure care fund that veterans fought so hard for in the PACT Act. Trump’s VA shut down a loan program preventing veteran foreclosures, leaving thousands in fear of losing their homes.
These aren’t isolated decisions. They’re all part of a long-term plan to chip away at the VA, privatize it, and walk away from the people who put their lives on the line for this country.
It’s not just wrong. It’s the deepest betrayal to the people and families who sacrificed everything and believed their country would have their backs in return.
That’s why honoring our veterans has to go beyond Veterans Day and thank you messages. It has to mean action. It means a fully funded VA, comprehensive health care, strong mental health resources, housing stability, economic opportunity, and dignity.
I’m thinking of my dad today, and every veteran and their family. We owe it to them to fight like hell for the care and benefits they deserve, not just today, but every single day. I hope one day it won’t be on our disabled vets to prove why their rare disease or neurological condition is “service connected” and instead it should be on the government to prove that — instead of the families of those veterans when they are on their death bed. After all, if we can't “afford” to take care of our vets we can’t afford the harm we send them into.
Thank you.
— David Hogg
_The appearance of U.S. Department of War visual information does not imply or constitute DOW endorsement._
