name: Chris Ahuja
subject: Millions Left Behind. We Deserve Better Leadership
body:
Public service is meant to serve the public. Period.

| | | | | | | | | | | | | | | [|
---](https://chrisahuja.us1.list-manage.com/track/click?u=97f443092fb7c091746070aa0&id=db4f6bb74e&e=5a6ee82cdb)
| | | | | |
Dear Peter,On Sunday, seven Senate Democrats joined Republicans to pass a continuing resolution that funds SNAP through January 19th. It also fails to extend ACA subsidies. That means millions of Americans are now facing sudden, unaffordable increases in their healthcare premiums.**Overnight, working families are being pushed toward financial crisis, while Donald Trump pushes tax breaks for billionaires and gilded ballrooms.**
This is not our America.
**Senate Minority Leader Chuck Schumer and Congressman Brad Sherman did not lead in this moment, and there is no plan in place to protect families from the fallout.** This is what happens when elected officials are insulated and untouched from the consequences of their decisions.
**When Democrats and Republicans send the same message — that working people are expendable — we must answer.**
I will not accept legislation that raises the cost of living for families in our district. That is not leadership. That is not public service.
**We deserve leaders who will fight with conviction.** Who will defend healthcare as a human right. Who will stand with children, seniors, veterans, and workers, not with corporate PACs or billionaire donors.
[**If you’re ready to demand better, I need you with me.**](https://chrisahuja.us1.list-manage.com/track/click?u=97f443092fb7c091746070aa0&id=04f2631b0c&e=5a6ee82cdb)

---
| [Donate](https://chrisahuja.us1.list-manage.com/track/click?u=97f443092fb7c091746070aa0&id=dedb518eb8&e=5a6ee82cdb)
---
| |
**In solidarity,
**[****](https://chrisahuja.us1.list-manage.com/track/click?u=97f443092fb7c091746070aa0&id=6d4b305a3e&e=5a6ee82cdb)**Chris Ahuja****Democrat for Congress, CA-32**
---

## Top tweets...
[@seanspicer](https://substack.com/redirect/d03a9b80-9191-49e3-a7f2-ac40e63f71fe?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg): How quick things can change. Democrats went a from a great Tuesday to a civil war Monday
## Start your day in the know
Are you starting you day in the know? Every morning at 9am ET you can join the Morning Meeting with myself, Mark Halperin and Dan Turrentine. We walk thru the news of the day like no one else and then take your questions. You can find it all on the same [YouTube channel](https://substack.com/redirect/1193dcb9-cdaf-4438-ad5c-7d1b57e82177?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) as my 6pm show. ## Wellness update...
If you watch the show or have been reading the newsletter for a while you know I have been on my own MAHA/wellness journey. One of the big things I have done is switched out many of the personal care products (soap, toothpaste, vitamins, etc) and home care products (cleaning supplies, detergent) with products from an American based, family company where wellness is front and center.
If you are interested in finding out what I did, text INFO to 276-500-1185 and join me!

## Democrats' shutdown stunt exposed...
After over a month of a government shutdown, there's finally an end in sight.
A deal is working its way through the Senate right now--all because a few Democrats finally decided to vote for the American people.
But the big question now is, what changed?
Why now?
What concession did Republicans make to bring Democrats to the table?
Answer: nothing.
For all intents and purposes, this deal is the same one from a month ago.
There was never any alternative.
They forced this shutdown just because they could, and the American people paid the price.
The left has been playing politics with people's lives and they need to be held accountable.
Let's break it down. ...

name: James Talarico for Texas
subject: Texas-sized goals
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Talarico for Texas
body:
#
Peter --
We say everything is bigger in Texas, and this campaign is a serious reminder that it’s no joke.
More people will vote in Texas than in any other competitive state in 2026 — upwards of 8 million people.
Barnstorming a state the sheer size of Texas is no easy task. We’ve already put thousands and thousands of miles on James’ pickup truck.
We also have more media markets than any other competitive U.S. Senate race. Most campaigns for the U.S. Senate only have to worry about media markets in the single digits. We have 20 different markets where we need to advertise and get James’ message in front of voters.
Running a campaign of this scale with these stakes requires serious resources.
That means our goals have to be Texas-sized too, and the only chance we have to reach them is with all of us chipping in what we can. So we need to ask:
[**Can you chip in $5 to keep us on track to reach our November goal and compete for every vote in every corner of Texas? We can’t afford to wait until next year to scale up our campaign, so your early investment is critical if we’re going to send James to the U.S. Senate.**](https://act.jamestalarico.com/go/512?c0=jt-em-launch.bF5RK3&refcode=em251111-1-ak&t=1&akid=217%2E1347274%2EtZDUdw)
_If you 've stored your info with ActBlue, we'll process your contribution instantly._

name: This is dark
subject: Boebert’s new conspiracy: Minority Leader Jeffries is “Reptilian”
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Trisha 4 Colorado
body:
We laugh at Boebert’s antics because she's a joke, but why she’s pushing this one is sinister...
| | | | | [](https://t7pkg9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001Fh5FtuizGMHLovvvYIZprTf2f2qT-PkV5BS_s5NrOItIh7dum05fqYsf2R5CJkeBl_WjAOHJvfxTYtFAAOiOfPmguqKt5eVMpHBqnfrW4s7FzAYS56hMwkES1Y-jSC1-br4JlOCkqhGPLgpcZNN4l3Mq2i7YmF5q9Tx2RYDGKnwBuDjcXWDq0x_PF-nom-c4DsaU37lD4gFqNlfidV3kaQpYHpoX0usA38IUaRIbFC0=&c=B1BFhlj0lcyTwEzL7l4nDJfkicOU8IbkcgH4jCfB8_jJMmZVYYFQIg==&ch=EXabes9pwxpvUZTlbX47C_dl56aPI0h8gz5sz8Aj3yg34zx6nmrANQ==)
---
|

Peter,
Lauren Boebert is pushing a new conspiracy theory that House Speaker Hakeem Jeffries is a "reptilian."
This is related to a conspiracy pushed by Holocaust denier David Icke who postulates that most Jews, and many people in positions of power across the world, secretly belong to a race of reptilians, with a sinister plan to eradicate humanity.
It's funny to laugh at Boebert's antics because she's a joke, last week it was her push to get to the bottom of extraterrestrials now it's lizardmen. However she has a dark reason for pushing this one...
---
| [](https://t7pkg9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001Fh5FtuizGMHLovvvYIZprTf2f2qT-PkV5BS_s5NrOItIh7dum05fqYsf2R5CJkeBl_WjAOHJvfxTYtFAAOiOfPmguqKt5eVMpHBqnfrW4s7FzAYS56hMwkES1Y-jSC1-br4JlOCkqhGPLgpcZNN4l3Mq2i7YmF5q9Tx2RYDGKnwBuDjcXWDq0x_PF-nom-c4DsaU37lD4gFqNlfidV3kaUiz-K_IL5_tyudBQoxldUI=&c=B1BFhlj0lcyTwEzL7l4nDJfkicOU8IbkcgH4jCfB8_jJMmZVYYFQIg==&ch=EXabes9pwxpvUZTlbX47C_dl56aPI0h8gz5sz8Aj3yg34zx6nmrANQ==)
---
Congressman Clay Higgins is a close ally of Boebert. In 2023 when an activist asked Boebert an unfriendly question at a presser with Higgins, he went on the attack manhandling the protestor and dragging him away.

The reason why Boebert is spreading this theory is because it dehumanizes her political opponents and primes her base to accept and participate in violence committed against them.
It's the same reason why she claims that Trans people are behind a wave of mass shootings or that Ilhan Omar is a jihadi.
Democrats, LGBTQ, Muslims, and Jews all groups she is targeting she wants violence committed against them. She is pushing for it at a policy level and she is priming her base for it.
We have a rapidly closing window to fire her before she escalates America's epidemic of political violence past the point of no return.
Lauren Boebert is in Congress because of a mere 546 votes. In 2024 when labor leader Trisha Calvarese went head to head with her, Trisha had the best performance a Democrat has ever had in this district while Boebert underperformed Trump in every county. Now Trisha is back to finish the job.
[Please, make a contribution of $25, $10, or whatever you can afford, to help the Calvarese campaign defeat Boebert, flip this seat, and take back the House for Democrats.](https://t7pkg9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001Fh5FtuizGMHLovvvYIZprTf2f2qT-PkV5BS_s5NrOItIh7dum05fqYsf2R5CJkeBAnI8yANof_wD3fT1GsvRsfzTQIlvAwVGQbUWLOSS5ELxgjzvZFem2D_X2yDSgMqLuEWCGCOJoTyz_o3NYOU1TLhK-RAysK0AEWDy6BwM-SrDl_m-7zsjTXUC9Ry50n4u_04UmrCfNVorYB9qycfApvvs3JxPwyONk2YX8iI9G5YklRUdsgVNcg==&c=B1BFhlj0lcyTwEzL7l4nDJfkicOU8IbkcgH4jCfB8_jJMmZVYYFQIg==&ch=EXabes9pwxpvUZTlbX47C_dl56aPI0h8gz5sz8Aj3yg34zx6nmrANQ==)

clean_body: We laugh at Boebert’s antics because she's a joke, but why she’s pushing this one is sinister... | | | | | == | Peter, Lauren Boebert is pushing a new conspiracy theory that House Speaker Hakeem Jeffries is a "reptilian." This is related to a conspiracy pushed by Holocaust denier David Icke who postulates that most Jews, and many people in positions of power across the world, secretly belong to a race of reptilians, with a sinister plan to eradicate humanity. It's funny to laugh at Boebert's antics because she's a joke, last week it was her push to get to the bottom of extraterrestrials now it's lizardmen. However she has a dark reason for pushing this one... | == Congressman Clay Higgins is a close ally of Boebert. In 2023 when an activist asked Boebert an unfriendly question at a presser with Higgins, he went on the attack manhandling the protestor and dragging him away. The reason why Boebert is spreading this theory is because it dehumanizes her political opponents and primes her base to accept and participate in violence committed against them. It's the same reason why she claims that Trans people are behind a wave of mass shootings or that Ilhan Omar is a jihadi. Democrats, LGBTQ, Muslims, and Jews all groups she is targeting she wants violence committed against them. She is pushing for it at a policy level and she is priming her base for it. We have a rapidly closing window to fire her before she escalates America's epidemic of political violence past the point of no return. Lauren Boebert is in Congress because of a mere 546 votes. In 2024 when labor leader Trisha Calvarese went head to head with her, Trisha had the best performance a Democrat has ever had in this district while Boebert underperformed Trump in every county. Now Trisha is back to finish the job. == If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | | == | == | == | | | == | == | == | == | We can beat Boebert and stop the violence she is pushing. No donation is too small. Even $1 is doing your part. Thank you. name: DC Daily Journal
subject: Students Attacked
body:
Please read the following message from our sponsor.
* * *

|
Dear friend,
This is unbelievable!
In case you missed it during Free Speech Week, Young Americans for Liberty student activists held events on campuses all over the country.
While YAL students held a peaceful event, and acted as a positive example, a bunch of radical campus leftists had other ideas.
_These Left-Wing lunatics slashed a free speech ball at the Colorado School of Mines._
Imagine being so brainwashed by an ideology, that any challenge to it must be met with violence.
Attacks like this sicken me. But they prove how important YAL’s work is.
Every day, on campuses across America, conservative and libertarian students are attacked for the “crime” of voicing their beliefs.
And while some students might turn away from their values, YAL students are taking a stand.
_YAL students are NOT backing down. They’re continuing to fight for free speech.
_
I’m proud to stand with our student activists. But I need your help to stand with them. Please donate whatever you can right now!
[https://secure.anedot.com/yalc4/yal-free-speech-week](https://click.em.dcdailyjournal.com/t/c/86b81c81629d4f979c759fffa262e6c0-6fadc7948f3c40839d0659abbe5e2d07/63)
For liberty,
Jeff Frazee Founder & CEO Young Americans For Liberty

\- Sent from my iPhone
|

name: Heidi Campbell
subject: Honoring Those Who Serve
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Campbell for State Senate, Lee Jones, treasurer
body:
https://voteheidicampbell.com/ [https://voteheidicampbell.com/]
Peter,
Today, we honor those who have served this country in uniform.
Over the course of our history, Tennesseans have earned this state the moniker “The Volunteer State” with their willingness to step up and serve, from the War of 1812 to the most recent conflicts in Afghanistan and Iraq.
Every day I step into the Capitol, I am cognizant of the responsibility I have taken on to represent my constituents, and honor the sacrifice of those Tennesseans who fought for our democratic way of life, and "government of the people, by the people, for the people."
As you go about your day, I hope you also take a moment to consider those who served, and if you served in uniform, I hope you know how grateful we are for you.
With gratitude,
Heidi Campbell
State Senator
Senator Heidi Campbell is a Tennessee State Senator, representing District 20. She is championing change for Tennessee and won't rest until we see gun safety reform, corporate accountability, and a safe and prosperous community where everyone can thrive.

name: Heidi Ganahl
subject: The Mountain Minute: 11/11/2025
body:
Debate erupts as Eagle County high school students launch TPUSA chapters
| | | | [](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
---
**PRESENTED BY[ROCKYMOUNTAINVOICE.COM](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
| | Happy Veterans Day,
Today, we pause to honor the brave men and women who have worn our nation’s uniform. Their courage, sacrifice, and unwavering commitment to freedom remind us that the blessings we enjoy were hard-won—and must be protected. To all who have served, and to the families who stood beside them, we thank you. Freedom endures because of you.
Listen to today's Mountain Minute podcast [here](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.), or watch on [YouTube](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) or [Rumble](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.). Here is a summary of today's top stories:
🏔️ **Here in Colorado...** In Delta County, GOP-endorsed candidates Adena Kreutz and Sheldon Kier won their school board races, [flipping the board toward a conservative majority](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) focused on academics and fiscal integrity. Meanwhile, students in Eagle County [sparked heated debate by launching Turning Point USA chapters](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.), prompting petitions and pushback—but also defenses of their right to organize under the Equal Access Act. Fort Lewis College faced similar controversy, first rejecting and then approving a TPUSA chapter after a [1,000-signature petition](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.). In contrast, Denver voters backed [union-endorsed Democrats](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) in a school board sweep, while [Colorado Springs districts held firm for reform](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.). And beyond the classroom, federal officials opened an investigation into the [death of a reintroduced gray wolf](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)—part of a program now costing nearly four times what voters were promised.
🇺🇸 **On the National Stage...** After 40 days of gridlock, the Senate voted 60–40 to end the [longest government shutdown in U.S. history](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.), restoring pay for federal workers and funding agencies through early 2026 if passed by the House. Meanwhile, Rep. Barry Loudermilk revealed the FBI ignored multiple informant warnings of [planned violence and Antifa activity ahead of January 6](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.), raising new questions about withheld intelligence and missed opportunities to prevent the Capitol breach.
🗣️ **Commentary Spotlight...** Montrose County Commissioner Sean Pond’s [“The Math Behind America’s Slow Surrender”](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) argues every political “middle ground” pushes the nation further left, eroding liberty one deal at a time. Jon Caldara’s [“Colorado Buys the Socialist Sandwich”](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) blasts Denver voters for approving Bloomberg-backed tobacco restrictions, calling it proof Colorado is becoming “more California than California” in its embrace of government overreach.
This is the Mountain Minute for Tuesday, November 11, 2025.
– Team RMV
---
|
---
**TODAY'S TOP STORIES**
**[Debate erupts as Eagle County high school students launch TPUSA chapters](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** BY SHAINA COLE, [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
When students at Eagle Valley and Battle Mountain high schools set out to form local chapters of Turning Point USA, they probably didn’t expect the stir it would cause. At Eagle Valley, the club had just cleared the approval process when pushback started rolling in.
Shortly afterward, community member Jen Winkeller shared a post in the Vail Moms!! Facebook group citing Superintendent Phil Qualman’s confirmation that the group “identified a staff sponsor.” Winkeller encouraged those opposed to the club to “sign the petition” and “protest peacefully,” linking to the Change.org petition.
The petition claims that TPUSA “routinely engaged in racist, homophobic, and sexist hate speech” and asserting that the organization did not belong in a taxpayer-funded public school. Over two weeks the petition gathered just over 220 signatures — a modest number in a district serving thousands of families.
**[READ MORE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
|
---
**[Grassroots effort pays off as GOP-endorsed school board candidates win big in Delta County](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** BY ANGIE MANY & SHIRLEY BAUER, [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
* **Conservative victories reshape Delta County schools:** GOP-endorsed candidates Adena Kreutz and Sheldon Kier won their school board races by wide margins, giving conservatives a majority focused on academic excellence and fiscal responsibility.
* **Grassroots organization fueled the win:** Volunteers made thousands of calls, hosted meet-and-greets, ran radio ads, and used social media to raise awareness that the candidates were GOP-backed despite nonpartisan ballots.
* **Positive, community-driven campaigning:** Both candidates emphasized integrity, local engagement, and solutions for students over partisan attacks—marking a model for grassroots success in rural Colorado.

**[READ MORE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
|
---
The Rocky Mountain Voice is a 501(c)(3) that exists to promote informed civic engagement and the principles of self-governance through enterprise reporting on public policy, politics, government, and culture throughout the state of Colorado. The only way we can continue to spread truth is with your support!
[DONATE TO ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
|
---
**[The Math Behind America’s Slow Surrender](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** BY SEAN POND, [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
* **Compromise as a path to decline:** Sean Pond argues that America’s gradual loss of liberty isn’t caused by revolution but by repeated political compromise—each “middle ground” pushing the country further left toward government control.
* **The shifting center:** He explains that as conservatives continually “meet in the middle,” the ideological center drifts leftward, normalizing bigger government and eroding the principles the Founders intended to protect.
* **A call to stand firm:** Pond urges Americans to stop treating bipartisanship as a moral virtue and instead defend fixed constitutional principles, warning that freedom fades not through force but through negotiation.

**[READ MORE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
|
---
**ONLY ON ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE ⤵️ **
**[The Shoes of Peace: Stepping Into Purpose Instead of Panic](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**BY DRAKE HUNTER | COMMENTARY | NOVEMBER 9** _During my time in the Air Force around the year 2000, I had the wonderful opportunity to work as a high-ropes instructor for youth in Cheyenne, Wyoming. The high ropes courses were built to challenge people beyond their comfort zones — encouraging them to trust their harnesses, stay level, and keep moving with confidence._
__****
**[Mesa County sheriff appeals budget constraints that could cut 28 deputies](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**BY JEN SCHUMANN | LOCAL | NOVEMBER 7** _Mesa County Sheriff Todd Rowell appeared before the Board of County Commissioners on Nov. 6, 2025, to warn that this year’s budget proposal could undo nearly a decade of progress under the 2017 voter-approved 0.37% public-safety sales tax. The budget appeal on the third floor of the county administration building doubled as a press conference, drawing commissioners, staff and reporters eager to see what “doing more with less” means for Mesa County public safety.___
**[Colorado River reality check: The problem isn’t the compact, it’s overuse.](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)****BY STEVE HARRIS | COMMENTARY | NOVEMBER 7** _This is my final article on the centennial of the Colorado River Compact (CRC) and the 75th anniversary of the Upper Colorado River Compact (UCRC), collectively referred to as the ‘Compacts.’” In water time, the Compacts are not that old. I have been in the water business in the Colorado River basin for 50 years – half of the CRC’s life – and watched how people viewed the implications of the Compacts over time. The Compacts have gone from background documents to front and center of water discussions._
__

**Feel like you have a unique story, commentary or tip to share?** Email [submit@rockymountainvoice.com](mailto:submit@rockymountainvoice.com).
|
---
|
---
**🇺🇸 DID YOU KNOW?** Originally known as Armistice Day, today's holiday marked the end of World War I—when fighting ceased at the 11th hour of the 11th day of the 11th month in 1918 as peace with Germany took effect. First celebrated to honor the Allied victory, it was renamed Veterans Day in 1954 by President Dwight D. Eisenhower to recognize all who have served in America’s armed forces, in every war and generation.
|
---
**CARTOON OF THE DAY**
|
---
**QUOTE OF THE DAY** “The true soldier fights not because he hates what is in front of him, but because he loves what is behind him.” - G.K. Chesterton
|

| | | [](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/8072?t=2&akid=1305%2E49239%2EJ0aAwm)
---
[](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/8073?t=3&akid=1305%2E49239%2EJ0aAwm)
Hi, null,

[**I’m writing today to ask if you’ll donate $11 to my campaign.**](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/8074?t=4&akid=1305%2E49239%2EJ0aAwm) But first, I want to tell you why your $11 matters so much – especially today.
I’m a proud Michigander. I grew up here, the daughter of two hardworking parents who ran a small landscaping business. **I love Michigan – and I’ve spent my whole life fighting for families like mine.**

When the auto industry was on the brink during the Great Recession, I helped save 200,000 Michigan jobs. Later, as a congresswoman, I played a leading role in passing legislation to rebuild American manufacturing.
And when Trump tried to use the military to silence protestors, I introduced the Stop Trump’s Abuse of Power Act – because I believe no one, not even a president, should be above the law.

**Now I’m running for U.S. Senate because I believe Michiganders deserve someone who will always put working people first – not politics, not billionaires, and definitely not Donald Trump.**
But here’s the truth: Trump and his MAGA allies are desperate to turn Michigan red, and GOP megadonors are gearing up to spend tens of millions to make that happen.

Trump has already endorsed my Republican opponent because he _knows_ our battleground race will likely decide what party controls the Senate – and what kind of country we wake up to after Election Day in 2026.
_Will the MAGA agenda become the law of the land in America? Or will Democrats flip the Senate and provide a real check on Donald Trump’s dangerous vision for our country?_

**We need to fight back, and I need you with me.**[**That’s why I’m personally asking you to donate $11 today, on Nov. 11, to help us hold the line in Michigan, flip the Senate, and put a real check on Trump’s chaos agenda.**](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/8074?t=5&akid=1305%2E49239%2EJ0aAwm)
| | [Donate](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/8073?t=6&akid=1305%2E49239%2EJ0aAwm)
---
Thank you for believing in this campaign – and in me – and our fight for a better future.

Haley
|
* * *
**A note from Haley Stevens:** null, I’m proud to be a lifelong Michigander and the daughter of small-business owners. That’s why I’ve fought so hard for working families my entire life. Now, I’m running for Michigan’s open Senate seat to continue fighting for the people and communities I love. The path to the Senate majority runs right through Michigan, which means this race is shaping up to be the most competitive and expensive in the entire country. The GOP is already coming after me as a top target for defeat, so I’m counting on your grassroots support to fight back and win. [**If you’re in this fight with me, will you add your name to endorse my campaign?**](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/8075?t=7&akid=1305%2E49239%2EJ0aAwm) | | | [](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/8072?t=8&akid=1305%2E49239%2EJ0aAwm)

Republicans got everything they wanted from the bill. The Democrats got nothing. After 40 days of holding the line for working families, John Fetterman and the Democrats just gave up. They _abandoned_ us. If your healthcare premiums go up, if your grocery bills, rent, and everyday expenses spiral out of control, or if this weak surrender empowers Trump and the GOP to double down against working people — **you can thank John Fetterman.****** **[We’re sick and tired of Democrats who refuse to do the bare minimum to stand up for working people. name: Jocelyn Benson
subject: A green light for conspiracies and lies
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY JOCELYN BENSON FOR GOVERNOR
body:
Nothing will stop me from defending our democracy and ensuring free and fair elections here in Michigan.
# [](https://act.jocelynbenson.com/go/203?t=1&akid=974%2E181023%2EWruC9w)
Hi there —
I’m sure you saw the news: In the middle of the night on Sunday, President Trump pardoned Rudy Giuliani and several others who, five years ago, launched an unprecedented effort to overturn a free and fair election because they did not like the results.
These pardons do not change the truth: The 2020 election was safe, secure, and the results were an accurate reflection of the will of the people. Over 250 audits after the election reaffirmed those results. Clearly and unequivocally, Donald Trump lost the 2020 election.
**But they do send a green light to those who seek to use lies and conspiracy theories to disrupt, attack, and undermine future elections.**
I spoke with Laura Coates on CNN last night about these very real dangers:
[](https://act.jocelynbenson.com/go/239?c0=jmb-em-q42025.hprbVa&refcode=AU_JMB_FR_GEN_RES_AC_20251111_PM1_V1_S1&t=2&akid=974%2E181023%2EWruC9w)
_“While these pardons do send a signal that it’s suddenly ok, that the president will have your back, if you use lies to undermine free and fair elections,**what’s very clear is that states and state officials still have unique and independent authority to pursue charges against the illegal acts of election interference...**_
_There’s never been a more important time for governors, for secretaries of state, for attorneys general. We stood firm in 2020, and**I’m running for governor now in Michigan to stand firm against these federal attempts to undermine our elections.”**_
[DONATE](https://act.jocelynbenson.com/go/854?c0=jmb-em-q42025.hVxyII&refcode=AU_JMB_FR_GEN_RES_AC_20251111_PM1_V1_S1&t=3&akid=974%2E181023%2EWruC9w)
---
Trump’s pardons will embolden others to spread the same dangerous misinformation — and that’s already happening. Candidates for United States Senate and Governor — including my opponents — are now amplifying the same falsehoods and sowing seeds of doubt about the integrity of our elections.
But here’s the thing: Nothing — not Trump’s pardons, not my opponents’ lies — will deter me from standing firmly in support of the truth, the law, and the voices and votes of the American people.
I’ve done that as Michigan’s secretary of state. And I’ll do the same as Michigan’s next governor.
But make no mistake, this is an all-hands-on-deck moment for all of us on Team Democracy.
**[Help me stand up to Trump and _anyone_ who gets in the way of our elections as the next governor of Michigan. Pitch in to my campaign today. ](https://act.jocelynbenson.com/go/239?c0=jmb-em-q42025.hprbVa&refcode=AU_JMB_FR_GEN_RES_AC_20251111_PM1_V1_S1&t=4&akid=974%2E181023%2EWruC9w)**
_If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_
| [CHIP IN $10 NOW](https://act.jocelynbenson.com/go/263?c0=jmb-em-q42025.pPlc_V&refcode=AU_JMB_FR_GEN_RES_AC_20251111_PM1_V1_S1&t=5&akid=974%2E181023%2EWruC9w)
---
[CHIP IN $25 NOW](https://act.jocelynbenson.com/go/264?c0=jmb-em-q42025.-XXH4U&refcode=AU_JMB_FR_GEN_RES_AC_20251111_PM1_V1_S1&t=6&akid=974%2E181023%2EWruC9w)
---
| [CHIP IN $50 NOW](https://act.jocelynbenson.com/go/265?c0=jmb-em-q42025.aWa82m&refcode=AU_JMB_FR_GEN_RES_AC_20251111_PM1_V1_S1&t=7&akid=974%2E181023%2EWruC9w)
---
[CHIP IN $100 NOW](https://act.jocelynbenson.com/go/266?c0=jmb-em-q42025.56FD2q&refcode=AU_JMB_FR_GEN_RES_AC_20251111_PM1_V1_S1&t=8&akid=974%2E181023%2EWruC9w)
---
| [CHIP IN $250 NOW](https://act.jocelynbenson.com/go/605?c0=jmb-em-q42025.UZInbw&refcode=AU_JMB_FR_GEN_RES_AC_20251111_PM1_V1_S1&t=9&akid=974%2E181023%2EWruC9w)
---
[ANOTHER AMOUNT](https://act.jocelynbenson.com/go/239?c0=jmb-em-q42025.hprbVa&refcode=AU_JMB_FR_GEN_RES_AC_20251111_PM1_V1_S1&t=10&akid=974%2E181023%2EWruC9w)
---
I’ve run against sore losers and election deniers before — and won. _That’s what we will do again, with your help._
I will stand up to anyone, anytime, who tries to stand in the way of Michiganders' voices and votes. No political rhetoric or threats will ever deter me from that mission.
**[That’s why I need your help. Your support today will help me protect every Michigander’s right to a fair, secure vote. Will you chip in $10, $25, or whatever you can to stand with me — and stand up for Michigan’s democracy?](https://act.jocelynbenson.com/go/239?c0=jmb-em-q42025.hprbVa&refcode=AU_JMB_FR_GEN_RES_AC_20251111_PM1_V1_S1&t=11&akid=974%2E181023%2EWruC9w)**
[DONATE](https://act.jocelynbenson.com/go/854?c0=jmb-em-q42025.hVxyII&refcode=AU_JMB_FR_GEN_RES_AC_20251111_PM1_V1_S1&t=12&akid=974%2E181023%2EWruC9w)
---
Thank you.
— Jocelyn

* * *
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with supporters like you.
* [Unsubscribe](https://act.jocelynbenson.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe-jbmi/?t=13&akid=974%2E181023%2EWruC9w)

PAID FOR BY JOCELYN BENSON FOR GOVERNOR

23133 Woodward Ave, Number 116,
Ferndale, MI 48220

name: PA Dems
subject: Thank you to those who served
disclaimer_text: Paid for by The Pennsylvania Democratic Party
body:
| |
---
Peter,

Today, we pause to honor the courage, service, and sacrifice of the men and women who have worn our nation’s uniform.

**From every corner of Pennsylvania and across every generation, veterans have answered the call to defend our freedoms and protect our democracy.** Their service reminds us that our rights and liberties endure because brave Americans stand ready to safeguard them.

As Democrats, we believe honoring our veterans means more than words, it means action. It means ensuring every veteran has access to quality health care, affordable housing, job opportunities, and the respect they’ve earned when they return home.

On this Veterans Day, we extend our deepest gratitude to those who have served, to the families who’ve shared in their sacrifice, and to those still serving today.

From all of us at the Pennsylvania Democratic Party, thank you for your service, your courage, and your commitment to our country.

— PA Dems
* * *

clean_body: | | Peter, Today, we pause to honor the courage, service, and sacrifice of the men and women who have worn our nation’s uniform. From every corner of Pennsylvania and across every generation, veterans have answered the call to defend our freedoms and protect our democracy. Their service reminds us that our rights and liberties endure because brave Americans stand ready to safeguard them. As Democrats, we believe honoring our veterans means more than words, it means action. It means ensuring every veteran has access to quality health care, affordable housing, job opportunities, and the respect they’ve earned when they return home. On this Veterans Day, we extend our deepest gratitude to those who have served, to the families who’ve shared in their sacrifice, and to those still serving today. From all of us at the Pennsylvania Democratic Party, thank you for your service, your courage, and your commitment to our country. — PA Dems | Follow us on , , and **! | | Dorian Gray glanced at the picture, and suddenly an uncontrollable feeling of hatred for Basil Hallward came over him, as though it had been suggested to him by the image on the canvas, whispered into his ear by those grinning lips. The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him.
---

And today, Musk is pointing to something far bigger than cars or rockets. He’s warning about **your retirement, your cash, and your ability to control your own future.**

He believes America’s financial system is on the verge of a seismic transformation — one that could hand unprecedented power to central authorities and leave everyday savers exposed.

The mainstream media won’t acknowledge this until the damage is done. By then, most people’s choices will be gone.

That’s why a confidential new report lays out exactly what’s happening — and the simple steps you can take now to stay protected from the fallout.

If you want to stay one step ahead of the crisis Musk predicts.

Ivan Dmytrovych Sirko was born in the early seventeenth century, approximately between 1605 and 1610. This is evidenced primarily by the results of research on Sirko's remains, according to which the Cossack ataman was 70-75 years old at the time of his death in 1680. The first researcher of Sirko's biography, the famous Ukrainian historian Dmytro Yavornytskyi, suggested that the future ataman was born in Sloboda Ukraine in the settlement of Merefa (now the city of Merefa, Kharkiv region). However, this is not true. Merefa was founded only in 1658, i.e. when Sirko was in his late teens. However, Sirko and his family lived in Merefa for a long time in his house, and after his death, his wife and some family members lived there. The fact that Merefa in Slobozhanshchyna is associated with the name of the Cossack Ataman is evidenced by another name for the town recorded in some Ukrainian chronicles, Sirkivka. Where, then, should we look for Sirko's homeland? In our opinion, the sources give grounds to localize it in Eastern Podillia, namely in the Vinnytsia region. It is worth recalling that Sirko was a colonel in Vinnytsia (Kalnytsia) in 1658-1660. As you know, at that time in Ukraine, people who came from the local area were usually elected to such positions. Let us also recall a letter from Hetman of the Left Bank Ukraine Ivan Samoilovych to the tsarist voivode Prince G. Romodanovsky of April 20, 1675*, which recounts (in Russian) Sirko's own words: "And since I was born among the Poles, I want to die there." Thus, it is clear that Sirko was born on the Ukrainian lands that were under the rule of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, not Muscovy (the latter included Slobozhanshchyna). The historian Volodymyr Borysenko's assumption that Sirko's birthplace was the hundred-man town of the Bratslav regiment Murafa, a few kilometers from Kalnyk, is correct. Now it is the village of Zhdanov, Vinnytsia region. By the way, Murafa in the Slobozhanshchyna probably originated from this Murafa, because immigrants from the Right Bank of Ukraine to the Left Bank often gave the newly founded settlements the names of their native villages or towns. It is also worth noting Sirko's great interest in Keleberda, which he founded in 1675 on the left bank of the Dnipro. For a long time, he sought from the Moscow tsar the right to own this town or sloboda, as well as the strategically important crossing of the Dnipro near Perevolochna (Sirko wanted this crossing not for himself, but for the entire Zaporozhian Lowland Army). Contrary to popular belief, we believe that Sirko did not come from a Cossack family, but from a small Ukrainian Orthodox gentry. For example, we know the name of a Podillia nobleman, Voytekh Sirko, who was married to a certain Olena Kozynska. Sources mention them in 1592. It is possible that they were relatives of the Cossack leader. Perhaps it is no coincidence that the kings of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, writing to Sirko, called him "urodzon." The word "nobleman" evokes negative associations for many people due to stereotypes instilled in school textbooks of the Stalinist era. However, this approach is not always correct. The term "nobleman" is identical to the words "knight," "chevalier," "nobleman," etc. and was commonly used not only in Poland but also in Ukraine, Belarus, and the Czech Republic. If scholars in Russia, Poland, and the Czech Republic, having contracted vulgar sociologism, no longer accuse Pushkin or Bunin, Suvorov or Kutuzov, Žižka or Kostiushko of being of noble or gentry origin, why have attacks on the Ukrainian and Belarusian gentry continued until recently? Is it not because in this way the imperial "divide and conquer" was transferred to our long-suffering history? Was it not because it was necessary to impose a nihilistic attitude toward the history of Ukraine, to "prove" that Ukrainians or Belarusians were just dark labor without national consciousness, ethnographic material doomed to build other people's empires? Meanwhile, the Ukrainian gentry produced many outstanding sons of Ukraine, fighters for its freedom. Suffice it to mention the names of Kryshtof Kosinski, Petro Konashevych-Sahaidachny, Ivan Sulyma, Ivan Mazepa, Bohdan Khmelnytsky, and his associates: Ivan and Danylo Vyhovsky, Ivan Bohun, Danylo and Ivan Nechai, Mykhailo Krychevsky, Jan Sokolovsky, Siluyan Muzhylovsky, and others. Sirko's appearance and personality do not always coincide with the conventional ideas about him that have developed in some works of art. The Cossack leader was slightly taller than average (174-176 cm), had regular facial features, and a straight nose. By the way, the great Relin intuitively caught this when he depicted him in the center of his painting "Cossacks Writing a Letter to the Turkish Sultan." It is interesting that the prototype for Sirko's image in the painting was the famous military leader General Dragomirov. Only one significant detail was not known to Repin and his consultant D. Yavornytskyi - a red birthmark on Sirko's lower lip on the right side. His contemporaries considered it a "sign of God" given to distinguish him from ordinary people. The Grand Hetman of the Crown, the future King of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, Jan Sobieski, who knew Sirko well and met him in Bratslav in 1671.

clean_body: The mainstream media won’t acknowledge this until the damage is done. By then, most people’s choices will be gone.| | | | November 11 | | | | | Musk Says the System Is About to Change | | | Dorian Gray glanced at the picture, and suddenly an uncontrollable feeling of hatred for Basil Hallward came over him, as though it had been suggested to him by the image on the canvas, whispered into his ear by those grinning lips. The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him. If you want to stay one step ahead of the crisis Musk predicts. | | | 🟡 News From "American Alternative Assets" | (Sponsored) ---|--- Ivan Dmytrovych Sirko was born in the early seventeenth century, approximately between 1605 and 1610. This is evidenced primarily by the results of research on Sirko's remains, according to which the Cossack ataman was 70-75 years old at the time of his death in 1680. The first researcher of Sirko's biography, the famous Ukrainian historian Dmytro Yavornytskyi, suggested that the future ataman was born in Sloboda Ukraine in the settlement of Merefa (now the city of Merefa, Kharkiv region). However, this is not true. Merefa was founded only in 1658, i.e. when Sirko was in his late teens. However, Sirko and his family lived in Merefa for a long time in his house, and after his death, his wife and some family members lived there. The fact that Merefa in Slobozhanshchyna is associated with the name of the Cossack Ataman is evidenced by another name for the town recorded in some Ukrainian chronicles, Sirkivka. Where, then, should we look for Sirko's homeland? In our opinion, the sources give grounds to localize it in Eastern Podillia, namely in the Vinnytsia region. It is worth recalling that Sirko was a colonel in Vinnytsia (Kalnytsia) in 1658-1660. As you know, at that time in Ukraine, people who came from the local area were usually elected to such positions. Let us also recall a letter from Hetman of the Left Bank Ukraine Ivan Samoilovych to the tsarist voivode Prince G. Romodanovsky of April 20, 1675*, which recounts (in Russian) Sirko's own words: "And since I was born among the Poles, I want to die there." Thus, it is clear that Sirko was born on the Ukrainian lands that were under the rule of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, not Muscovy (the latter included Slobozhanshchyna). The historian Volodymyr Borysenko's assumption that Sirko's birthplace was the hundred-man town of the Bratslav regiment Murafa, a few kilometers from Kalnyk, is correct. Now it is the village of Zhdanov, Vinnytsia region. By the way, Murafa in the Slobozhanshchyna probably originated from this Murafa, because immigrants from the Right Bank of Ukraine to the Left Bank often gave the newly founded settlements the names of their native villages or towns. It is also worth noting Sirko's great interest in Keleberda, which he founded in 1675 on the left bank of the Dnipro. For a long time, he sought from the Moscow tsar the right to own this town or sloboda, as well as the strategically important crossing of the Dnipro near Perevolochna (Sirko wanted this crossing not for himself, but for the entire Zaporozhian Lowland Army). Contrary to popular belief, we believe that Sirko did not come from a Cossack family, but from a small Ukrainian Orthodox gentry. For example, we know the name of a Podillia nobleman, Voytekh Sirko, who was married to a certain Olena Kozynska. Sources mention them in 1592. It is possible that they were relatives of the Cossack leader. Perhaps it is no coincidence that the kings of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, writing to Sirko, called him "urodzon." The word "nobleman" evokes negative associations for many people due to stereotypes instilled in school textbooks of the Stalinist era. However, this approach is not always correct. The term "nobleman" is identical to the words "knight," "chevalier," "nobleman," etc. and was commonly used not only in Poland but also in Ukraine, Belarus, and the Czech Republic. If scholars in Russia, Poland, and the Czech Republic, having contracted vulgar sociologism, no longer accuse Pushkin or Bunin, Suvorov or Kutuzov, Žižka or Kostiushko of being of noble or gentry origin, why have attacks on the Ukrainian and Belarusian gentry continued until recently? Is it not because in this way the imperial "divide and conquer" was transferred to our long-suffering history? Was it not because it was necessary to impose a nihilistic attitude toward the history of Ukraine, to "prove" that Ukrainians or Belarusians were just dark labor without national consciousness, ethnographic material doomed to build other people's empires? Meanwhile, the Ukrainian gentry produced many outstanding sons of Ukraine, fighters for its freedom. Suffice it to mention the names of Kryshtof Kosinski, Petro Konashevych-Sahaidachny, Ivan Sulyma, Ivan Mazepa, Bohdan Khmelnytsky, and his associates: Ivan and Danylo Vyhovsky, Ivan Bohun, Danylo and Ivan Nechai, Mykhailo Krychevsky, Jan Sokolovsky, Siluyan Muzhylovsky, and others. Sirko's appearance and personality do not always coincide with the conventional ideas about him that have developed in some works of art. The Cossack leader was slightly taller than average (174-176 cm), had regular facial features, and a straight nose. By the way, the great Relin intuitively caught this when he depicted him in the center of his painting "Cossacks Writing a Letter to the Turkish Sultan." It is interesting that the prototype for Sirko's image in the painting was the famous military leader General Dragomirov. Only one significant detail was not known to Repin and his consultant D. Yavornytskyi - a red birthmark on Sirko's lower lip on the right side. His contemporaries considered it a "sign of God" given to distinguish him from ordinary people. The Grand Hetman of the Crown, the future King of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, Jan Sobieski, who knew Sirko well and met him in Bratslav in 1671. | | You are receiving this newsletter from Your Capital Minds (YCM), an esteemed member of the Stark Media LLC family. name: The Real Texas PAC
subject: 🚨 Breaking: MAGA pushes “bounty hunter” plan with your tax dollars
body:
Donald Trump is going around the country BRAGGING about his so-called Big Beautiful Bill — calling it a “birthday present for America.”
We're raising funds NOW to hold EVERY SINGLE Republican who voted for this cruelty accountable and elect Democrats who will RESTORE what’s been taken. Chip in NOW to show that Democrats are not backing down >>> [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-biguglybill?refcode=071025_2]
Because here’s what Trump is signing into law today:
❌16 million people will lose their healthcare
❌ 25% of nursing homes will shut down
❌ 51,000 preventable deaths every single year
❌ 2 million kids will lose access to food
❌ 1.75 million blue collar jobs will be wiped out
❌ 1.4 million students will lose their Pell Grants
❌ And the ultra-wealthy will get a $975 BILLION tax break
That’s not “beautiful.” That’s a national tragedy.
Our plan is clear:
✅ Expose what this bill really does
✅ Hold every single Republican who supported it accountable at the ballot box
✅ Elect Democrats who will RESTORE what Republicans destroyed and protect working families for good
This is it. We can’t undo Trump’s signature - but we can make every Republican who stood with him regret it.
Chip in $25, $50, $100 - whatever you can - to help us hold Republicans accountable and elect Democrats who will rebuild what we’ve lost >> [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-biguglybill?refcode=071025_2]

name: Abigail Spanberger
subject: We sent a message:
body:
---
Derek, Last week, Virginians sent a message to every corner of the Commonwealth, to our neighbors and our fellow Americans across the country, to the whole world. We chose leadership that will put pragmatism over partisanship. We chose leadership that will focus on problem-solving, not stoking division. We chose leadership that will focus relentlessly on what matters most: lowering costs, keeping our communities safe, and strengthening our economy for every Virginian. We chose a Governor who will always put Virginia — not a president, not a political party — first. **It is the honor of my lifetime to be elected the 75th Governor of the Commonwealth of Virginia!** To everyone who helped us achieve this win, thank you for the trust you have placed in me. To our tireless volunteers and grassroots donors, thank you for devoting your time, energy, and hard-earned dollars to his campaign. _We won this race because of you._ [**Now, I’m asking for your help one more time. We set an example for the entire nation, and now we have to help the DNC make sure more Democrats follow our lead. Can you pitch in $7 or more today to help support their work ahead of next year’s midterms?**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-november-2025?amount=7&refcode=em_20251111_5201_fr&refcode2=5201_22930333_hzSKxf&akid=5201%2E22930333%2EhzSKxf) _If you 've stored your info with ActBlue, we'll process your contribution instantly._ | | | **[Donate $7](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-november-2025?express_lane=true&amount=7&refcode=em_20251111_5201_fr&refcode2=5201_22930333_hzSKxf&akid=5201%2E22930333%2EhzSKxf)**

name: Eli Crane
subject: I know you're tired of politicians emailing you asking for money
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the NRCC and not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee
body:
I understand, Peter.
I know you're tired of politicians texting you asking for money, and I don't blame you. With inflation still being reeled in, every dollar counts.
But let me tell you, this is different.
My name is Eli Crane, and I'm a Navy SEAL Conservative warrior from Arizona. I've been on the front lines, fighting for our country in 5 war-time deployments.
I'm not afraid to take a stand: I've always proudly championed the America First agenda, I'm for a secure border, I'm for election integrity, and I'm for protecting our kids from woke indoctrination.
Democrats hate me, and the Establishment Republicans aren't too fond of me either. That's why they're throwing everything they've got at me - a whopping $50 MILLION war chest to take me down in 2026.
But I'm not backing down. I'm a Navy SEAL, and we don't give up. That's why I'm asking you, with all humility, to chip in a few bucks before my critical fundraising deadline. [CHIP IN >>

clean_body: I understand, Peter. I know you're tired of politicians texting you asking for money, and I don't blame you. With inflation still being reeled in, every dollar counts. But let me tell you, this is different. My name is Eli Crane, and I'm a Navy SEAL Conservative warrior from Arizona. I've been on the front lines, fighting for our country in 5 war-time deployments. I'm not afraid to take a stand: I've always proudly championed the America First agenda, I'm for a secure border, I'm for election integrity, and I'm for protecting our kids from woke indoctrination. Democrats hate me, and the Establishment Republicans aren't too fond of me either. That's why they're throwing everything they've got at me - a whopping $50 MILLION war chest to take me down in 2026. But I'm not backing down. I'm a Navy SEAL, and we don't give up. That's why I'm asking you, with all humility, to chip in a few bucks before my critical fundraising deadline. know times are still tough, and if you can't afford to give, I understand. But I'm counting on patriots like you to help me take on the Establishment and the radical Left. My candidacy is in your hands. bless you.- Eli Crane USE OF MILITARY RANK, JOB TITLES AND PHOTOGRAPHS IN UNIFORM DOES NOT IMPLY ENDORSEMENT BY THE DEPARTMENT OF DEFENSE OR ANY OF ITS BRANCHES.| | Paid for by the NRCC and not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. ## [Trump declares Veterans Day as 'Victory Day' at Arlington Cemetery](https://substack.com/redirect/f53bc3e9-8405-4755-8baa-e26abf78947c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)

| 10:34 PM
[](https://substack.com/redirect/f53bc3e9-8405-4755-8baa-e26abf78947c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
[President Donald Trump used Tuesday's Arlington National Cemetery observance to officially designate Veterans Day as a commemoration of America's World War I victory, or "Victory Day," marking the anniversary of Germany's surrender.](https://substack.com/redirect/f53bc3e9-8405-4755-8baa-e26abf78947c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)

* * *
# **Senate ends record shutdown, sets January deadline with uncertainty ahead**
The Senate advanced a bipartisan deal late Monday night to **[reopen the government until Jan. 30](https://substack.com/redirect/93bce324-cb36-41fe-aeae-13b96964c3da?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** , ending the longest shutdown on record, but lawmakers face uncertainty about whether they can avoid another closure early next year.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/93bce324-cb36-41fe-aeae-13b96964c3da?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
* **Path forward** : Senators believe passing appropriations bills the "old-fashioned way" before the January deadline could prevent another shutdown. Senate Appropriations Chair Susan Collins, R-Maine, anticipates combining defense, labor, transportation and housing bills into one package, with lawmakers hoping more completed spending bills will better serve Americans.
* **Thune 's Opinion:** "The immediate objective is to get the government open and enable those conversations to commence," Thune said. "There are Democrats and Republicans who are both interested in trying to do something in the healthcare space. And clearly, there is a need."
* **Healthcare vote guarantee** : Sen. Richard Blumenthal, D-Conn., warning Democrats must remain united in holding Republicans to this commitment. Failure to address the healthcare subsidies could significantly hamper efforts to avert another shutdown.
* **Divided outlook** : While some senators like John Kennedy, R-La., view the January deadline as "light years away," others including Sen. Chris Murphy, D-Conn., predict difficulty passing a long-term bipartisan budget without healthcare provisions and protections against what he called Trump administration "illegality." Democrats want bipartisan funding bills partly to push back against administration cuts.
* * *
# **Trump defends White House ballroom project after Michelle Obama criticism**
President Donald Trump **[defended his East Wing construction](https://substack.com/redirect/7225868f-03a9-427b-b997-fb56e3e232e3?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** Monday, telling Laura Ingraham the new $250-300 million privately funded ballroom will be "one of the greatest" in the world and solve the White House's inadequate capacity for state events.
* **Obama 's criticism**: Former first lady Michelle Obama alleged the demolition "denigrates" the East Wing, where first ladies' offices traditionally sit and the "heart" of her work took place. She criticized Trump for "tearing down" the building and told NBC, "There's no guidebook" for being first lady, adding "There's barely a staff. Now we don't have a building."
* **Trump 's defense**: The president argued the current White House can only accommodate 79 people for seated events, forcing state dinners with foreign leaders into tents on the lawn. "If you have President Xi from China, or if you have some big state event, we have no place to have it," Trump said. He noted the East Wing had been renovated 20 times, including adding a floor that "looked like hell" and "had nothing to do with the original building."
* **Project details** : The sprawling 90,000-square-foot development would give the White House a formal ballroom for the first time in history and adhere to the classical design of the existing structure. The demolition has drawn criticism from Democratic leaders including Rep. Eric Swalwell, House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries and former White House press secretary Karine Jean-Pierre.
* * *

# **Cheryl Hines criticizes Larry David 's 'Crazy' Attack on Bill Maher over Trump**
Cheryl Hines went on Bill Maher's podcast as she promotes her new book "Unscripted" while navigating backlash over her husband Robert F. Kennedy Jr.'s Trump endorsement, and **[defended Republicans](https://substack.com/redirect/0f9e3f25-0abf-4b99-b079-8f9a9d250adf?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** in a stand against Democrat cruelty.
[](https://substack.com/redirect/aa97f19f-c0c4-4e2f-b51b-178d76b1819d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* **Hollywood blacklist comparison** : Maher warned Hines she could lose jobs in an entertainment industry that "loves to romanticize the period in the '50s when there was a blacklist" but now has "their own kind of blacklist." He argued it's evolved from "a witch hunt about communists" to "a witch hunt for people who aren't woke enough," adding, "It's not like we're conservatives."
* **Republican vs. Democrat treatment** : Hines said Republicans "have been very kind to me, from the beginning" even when Kennedy ran as a Democrat, while "I can't say that for the Democrats" which lead to both reflecting on criticism Maher faced from liberals for meeting with President Trump.
* **Lost compassion** : The pair agreed compassion was once the Democratic Party's backbone, but no longer. Maher explained right-wing figures can argue, agree to disagree, and remain friends, but "the left isn't that way." He criticized liberals as "babies" and "not sophisticated people," saying they respond with "'No, if you're not with me, I f---- hate you, and you're a deplorable...'"
* **Liberal hypocrisy claim** : Both Hines and Maher criticized what they see as selective enforcement of anti-bullying principles, with Maher saying "Liberals in theory, they hate bullying," to which Hines added, "except when they do it."

* * *

---
| |
---
| | Dear Peter,
---
While the Redwoods Rising apprentice program is usually about young people learning from our restoration experts, we end up learning a lot from them, too. Take Francisco Saavedra—a two-time apprentice who shared his method for finding good luck in tough moments. (Who couldn’t use that these days?) As Francisco told us: _"I keep something special inside my hard hat: a feather from a northern flicker—or_ pegoy _in Yurok. The northern flicker is native to Northern California and can often be spotted drumming on trees or calling out through the forest._ _In Yurok tradition, the flicker is more than just a bird, it’s a symbol of good luck and medicine. Carrying this feather with me is a way to stay connected to that meaning, especially while working deep in the woods. The feather is a silent source of strength, protection, and presence during long days in the field. _ _And hey ... have you ever seen a flicker bird get hit by a falling log?__I didn’t think so.__That’s why I keep one in my hard hat. It works every time."_
---
---
To see what other must-have items Francisco carries into the field—and a brief glimpse into a day in the life of an apprentice—read his quick post, **[“Gearing up to survey the forest with Redwoods Rising.](https://click.everyaction.com/k/119101168/581940986/-1161625406?utm_campaign=smile-send&utm_medium=email&utm_source=ea-mar&ms=11-11-2025&version=B&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9TVFJXTC9TVFJXTC8xLzg5NTc0IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImJlMDZkZjk3LTUzYmYtZjAxMS04MTk0LTAwMjI0ODIzZmY5YiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=gGCcelRQocUsGHbfc0AU_7g0saSZwHzNKpGkWpyeuYs=&emci=bb06df97-53bf-f011-8194-00224823ff9b&emdi=be06df97-53bf-f011-8194-00224823ff9b&ceid=1504551)”**
---
[ ** More from Francisco** ](https://click.everyaction.com/k/119101169/581940987/-1161625406?utm_campaign=smile-send&utm_medium=email&utm_source=ea-mar&ms=11-11-2025&version=B&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9TVFJXTC9TVFJXTC8xLzg5NTc0IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImJlMDZkZjk3LTUzYmYtZjAxMS04MTk0LTAwMjI0ODIzZmY5YiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=gGCcelRQocUsGHbfc0AU_7g0saSZwHzNKpGkWpyeuYs=&emci=bb06df97-53bf-f011-8194-00224823ff9b&emdi=be06df97-53bf-f011-8194-00224823ff9b&ceid=1504551)

clean_body: How a forest steward finds strength and luck in unexpected places| | | | | | | | | Dear Peter, While the Redwoods Rising apprentice program is usually about young people learning from our restoration experts, we end up learning a lot from them, too. Take Francisco Saavedra—a two-time apprentice who shared his method for finding good luck in tough moments. (Who couldn’t use that these days?) As Francisco told us: "I keep something special inside my hard hat: a feather from a northern flicker—or pegoy in Yurok. The northern flicker is native to Northern California and can often be spotted drumming on trees or calling out through the forest. In Yurok tradition, the flicker is more than just a bird, it’s a symbol of good luck and medicine. Carrying this feather with me is a way to stay connected to that meaning, especially while working deep in the woods. The feather is a silent source of strength, protection, and presence during long days in the field. And hey ... have you ever seen a flicker bird get hit by a falling log?I didn’t think so.That’s why I keep one in my hard hat. It works every time." To see what other must-have items Francisco carries into the field—and a brief glimpse into a day in the life of an apprentice—read his quick post, ” | | Wishing you good luck—however you find it, Save the Redwoods League Photos from top: ; Francisco Saavedra | | Save the Redwoods League 111 Sutter Street, 11th Floor San Francisco, CA 94104 United States CONNECT WITH US | | | | | | ---|---|---|---|---|--- |

name: Lizzie Pannill Fletcher
subject: It’s Official.
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY ELIZABETH PANNILL FLETCHER FOR CONGRESS
body:
ELIZABETH PANNILL FLETCHER FOR CONGRESS [https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP10/1/82676/images/Non-Re-Elect%20Logo.png]
This weekend, I filed the official paperwork to run for re-election, and today I got the confirmation that it's all in order. That means my name will be on the ballot for Texas’ Seventh Congressional District again in 2026!
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/folfe2511reelection?refcode=folfe2511reelection [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/folfe2511reelection?refcode=folfe2511reelection]
It’s amazing to me that this is already my fifth time putting my name on the ballot for TX07. Back in 2018, people didn’t believe that this district could ever elect a Democrat. It had been more than 50 years, after all. But together we built a broad and diverse coalition to do so, and together, we made history. And we have continued together in the years since, bringing a little more Houston to Washington, D.C.
This year, the stakes could not be higher. Our democracy and our freedom are on the ballot. And so much more—from fighting for access to affordable health care, to ensuring every Texan gets fair representation, to ensuring equality and justice for all—we have so many challenges ahead.
DONATE TO RE-ELECT LIZZIE [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/folfe2511reelection?refcode=folfe2511reelection]
We know the path ahead won’t be easy. But we also know what’s possible when we work together. We witnessed it again just last week—and just in time. Our work for 2026 has already begun, and your participation will carry us to victory in 2026 and beyond, helping build a better future for all Americans.
As you know, we’ve kept our campaign office open all year as a hub for organizing, outreach, and community-building in every corner of TX07. Whether it’s making calls, sending postcards, joining a block walk, or contributing money to help us get the message out and mobilize voters—every kind of contribution matters. And they are all essential to winning. And that’s what we do on #TeamLizzie.
Today, I’d be thrilled if you could make a contribution of $20.26 to mark the moment and prepare for the work ahead. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/folfe2511reelection?refcode=folfe2511reelection&amount=20.26]
It’s all on the line in 2026. Let’s get to work!
Together we can do anything,
Lizzie Signature [https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP10/1/82676/images/lizzie_signature-1.png]

DONATE [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/folfe2511reelection?refcode=folfe2511reelection]

VOLUNTEER [https://secure.ngpvan.com/Ln8e8kjn2Euuh3daVlmozQ2]

name: Team Jon Ossoff
subject: directly and transparently:
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jon Ossoff for Senate
body:
#

Here is the truth, directly and transparently:
We have received far fewer individual contributions so far in November than we had planned for to keep with our budget goals.
We don’t share this to try to scare you or act like the sky is falling — there is _plenty_ of time left to turn things around and get back on track. But it has to start _right now._
For those of you who care deeply about reelecting Jon Ossoff and are in a place to be able to give — **we need your help today.**
[**Please use this link to make a donation to Jon’s reelection campaign.**](https://act.electjon.com/go/32?c0=ossoff-em-nov25.fw0_9v&refcode=em251107-1a-f&t=1&akid=1071%2E5691347%2EumDGFZ)
_We can’t afford to fall short of our goals and risk letting Trump and MAGA Super PACs define this race._
If everyone reading this email chipped in $5, we’d catch up by the end of the day. If everyone gave $30, we’d hit our goal for the month by midnight.
But unfortunately, most people who read this won’t or are not able to give. That’s why we’re hoping that people like you — who are in the position to give and understand the stakes of this race — can step up and help us close the gap.
**What do you say?**
[**Please make a contribution to Jon Ossoff’s reelection campaign before midnight. Our average online donation is about $30. Many give more. Some give less. The most important thing is that lots and lots of people are all chipping in because that’s how we’re going to win.**](https://act.electjon.com/go/32?c0=ossoff-em-nov25.fw0_9v&refcode=em251107-1a-f&t=2&akid=1071%2E5691347%2EumDGFZ)
Thank you so much for your support.
Team Ossoff
**[DONATE](https://act.electjon.com/go/32?c0=ossoff-em-nov25.fw0_9v&refcode=em251107-1a-f&t=3&akid=1071%2E5691347%2EumDGFZ)**

Paid for by Jon Ossoff for Senate
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Thank you for supporting Jon Ossoff. Email is a critical way to keep up with Jon and his work in the US Senate, but you can click [here](https://act.electjon.com/signup/fewer-emails-signup/?t=4&akid=1071%2E5691347%2EumDGFZ) to sign up to receive fewer emails, or click [here](https://act.electjon.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=5&akid=1071%2E5691347%2EumDGFZ) if you'd like to unsubscribe.

name: Brad Finstad
subject: They’re coming for us.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Finstad for Congress
body:
| | | | |
---
| Friend, The Left is on the march. From New York to Virginia to right here in southern Minnesota, liberal activists are mobilizing, redrawing maps, and pouring money into districts just like ours. They know they can’t win on their record, so they’re trying to buy back the House... one seat at a time. But here’s the good news: President Trump has my back, and with your help, we can fight back and win.
---

clean_body: | | | | | | Friend, The Left is on the march. From New York to Virginia to right here in southern Minnesota, liberal activists are mobilizing, redrawing maps, and pouring money into districts just like ours. They know they can’t win on their record, so they’re trying to buy back the House... one seat at a time. But here’s the good news: President Trump has my back, and with your help, we can fight back and win. | | | We’re one year out from Election Day. Lacking critical support in your state
| _**LISTEN! LISTEN! LISTEN!**_ Democrats are gaining momentum to REMOVE Trump from office via the 25th Amendment!

**_There’s only one problem:_** We’re lacking critical grassroots support in your state and your name is MISSING from our records.

Thank you,
The Voter Protection Project

Donald Trump is STEALING House seats in Louisiana, Utah, Texas, North Carolina, and more...

He’s trying to RIP the Voting Rights Act to shreds...

Not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. 401 2nd Ave. S, Ste. 303, Seattle, WA 98104

The Voter Protection Project is on an important mission: To end partisan gerrymandering and voter suppression by electing candidates at the state and federal levels that are forceful advocates for voting rights. Our enfranchisement agenda includes working to ensure that every state has automatic voter registration and online voter registration, convenient early voting, and independent redistricting commissions to ensure politicians are not able to draw their own maps.

Please, consider making a [one-time contribution by clicking here](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/121?t=1&akid=21814%2E878179%2E0sf2QQ). **Or, to make the most powerful lasting impact and help us oust Republicans up and down the ballot who attack our right to vote, make a[monthly sustaining contribution by clicking here](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/122?t=2&akid=21814%2E878179%2E0sf2QQ).**

This Wednesday is the deadline -- and our baby girl is leading the charge [to make a difference.](https://substack.com/redirect/9da67b77-b9a9-42eb-9b97-dfebffe65521?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
She is an elite athlete -- training six days a week. She takes honors and AP classes and carries a perfect 4.0 GPA. She's disciplined, kind, and focused -- the kind of young woman who believes hard work and heart can change the world.
Click [here](https://substack.com/redirect/9da67b77-b9a9-42eb-9b97-dfebffe65521?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to donate.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/9da67b77-b9a9-42eb-9b97-dfebffe65521?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Now she's putting that same drive into something bigger than herself. She's serving as a team leader for her school's **Mini-THON** , a student-led movement that raises funds and awareness for **[Four Diamonds](https://substack.com/redirect/9da67b77-b9a9-42eb-9b97-dfebffe65521?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**[,](https://substack.com/redirect/9da67b77-b9a9-42eb-9b97-dfebffe65521?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) an organization devoted to conquering childhood cancer.
These kids train, organize, and fundraise -- not for themselves, but for children fighting the hardest battle of all.
Will you help her reach her goal before the Wednesday deadline?
We couldn't be prouder of her -- not just for her achievements, but for her heart. Thank you for standing with her, and with every family praying for a cure.
**For the kids. For the future. For the fight.**

name: America First War Room
subject: RFK Jr drops BOMBSHELL at US Senate hearing
body:
[View in Web Browser](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.com/click/e31488443/VaHR0cHM6Ly93cm8ubnVjbGV1c2VtYWlsLmNvbS9hbXBsaWZ5L3YvYlpaNFdlRDdlWQ/SZW1haWxfYW1lcmljYU9ORXN0d2Fycm9vbV9jb20yNTExMTE/LbmNsUEVYSUdXVw/qP2hpZHM9bmNLS2tTb2VlVThZNU1scw/gaRPMVg/s5q17812ead)
|

---
Dear reader,

RFK Jr just testified before the US senate...

And he was NOT holding back.

Angry liberals accused him of boosting _**conspiracy theorists, crockpots and grifters**_ ...

But Kennedy defiantly looked them dead in the eye...

And said exactly what they feared most...

“ _**[The American people] deserve the truth and that’s what we’re going to give them for the first time...**_ ”

And now, he’s issued [**a terrifying new warning**](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.com/click/e31488443/VaHR0cHM6Ly9wcm8ubmhzLW5ld3MuY29tL20vMjQ5MjI3Mg/SZW1haWxfYW1lcmljYU9ORXN0d2Fycm9vbV9jb20yNTExMTE/LbmNsUEVYSUdXVw/gaRPMVg/s7jcf4df3c2) that Americans need to hear in 2025.

If you were born before Jan 1st, 1965, [**THIS**](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.com/click/e31488443/VaHR0cHM6Ly9wcm8ubmhzLW5ld3MuY29tL20vMjQ5MjI3Mg/SZW1haWxfYW1lcmljYU9ORXN0d2Fycm9vbV9jb20yNTExMTE/LbmNsUEVYSUdXVw/gaRPMVg/s7jcf4df3c2) affects you most of all.

Get the full facts [**here**](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.com/click/e31488443/VaHR0cHM6Ly9wcm8ubmhzLW5ld3MuY29tL20vMjQ5MjI3Mg/SZW1haWxfYW1lcmljYU9ORXN0d2Fycm9vbV9jb20yNTExMTE/LbmNsUEVYSUdXVw/gaRPMVg/s7jcf4df3c2).

P.S. Liberals will call [**THIS**](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.com/click/e31488443/VaHR0cHM6Ly9wcm8ubmhzLW5ld3MuY29tL20vMjQ5MjI3Mg/SZW1haWxfYW1lcmljYU9ORXN0d2Fycm9vbV9jb20yNTExMTE/LbmNsUEVYSUdXVw/gaRPMVg/s7jcf4df3c2) crazy too, until they [**see the data**](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.com/click/e31488443/VaHR0cHM6Ly9wcm8ubmhzLW5ld3MuY29tL20vMjQ5MjI3Mg/SZW1haWxfYW1lcmljYU9ORXN0d2Fycm9vbV9jb20yNTExMTE/LbmNsUEVYSUdXVw/gaRPMVg/s7jcf4df3c2). Learn why RFK Jr is warning Americans about this emerging health crisis before it’s too late. Watch [**this video**](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.com/click/e31488443/VaHR0cHM6Ly9wcm8ubmhzLW5ld3MuY29tL20vMjQ5MjI3Mg/SZW1haWxfYW1lcmljYU9ORXN0d2Fycm9vbV9jb20yNTExMTE/LbmNsUEVYSUdXVw/gaRPMVg/s7jcf4df3c2) now.

This ad is sent on behalf of NewMarket Health Publishing, LLC. P.O. Box 913, Frederick, MD 21705, USA. If you would like to get fewer emails, If you would like to unsubscribe, BREAKING via NBC : "Trump just realigned the entire political map. Democrats have 'no easy path' to fix it." | This petition is registered to you only. Donald Trump only became President in 2016 because of the outdated Electoral College. Hillary Clinton won the popular vote by nearly 2.9 million. But the Electoral College overruled her popular vote victory. Now, Sen. Elizabeth Warren is urgently pleading with Democrats: We couldn't agree more. But now that Trump is in office, he and his Republican cronies will do everything in their power to uphold the Electoral College. So we need to act now -- because we must prevent unpopular, unqualified candidates from EVER becoming President again. Thank you, Fight for Reform | This message was sent to: dpwillis67@gmail.com | | Paid political advertisement paid for and provided by End Citizens United Nonfederal, P.O. Box 66005, Washington, D.C. 20035. Not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. Your contribution to FFR will be used in connection with FFR’s matching funds program to match the contributions of other contributors. Contributions to End Citizens United Non-Federal are not tax deductible. Contributions to End Citizens United Non-federal will be used in End Citizens United Non-federal’s sole discretion and are not being earmarked for use in any particular state or election.
